description:
---
references:
  request: aura-plugins-eocq
  elicit: aura-plugins-ollt
  urd: aura-plugins-2ojv
  supersedes: aura-plugins-7csc
---
## 1. Executive Summary

Complete re-scope from PROPOSAL-5. ACP alignment (R13, R15-R17) deferred to a dedicated aura-acp plugin epic. v3 core scope is R1-R12 only.

**Why:** Claude Code lacks native ACP support. All harnesses need their own adapters (Claude Code via Agent SDK; Gemini/OpenCode/Codex via ACP adapter). Coupling the core to the ACP SDK produces a leaky abstraction. Harness-agnostic core + harness plugin is the correct long-term design.

**What changes:** Remove all ACP-specific types from core: no AcpRawSignal, AcpMappedSignal, AuraSignalType, AcpEventMapper, map_acp_event activity, AcpTranscriptRecorder, AcpSecurityGate, acp_session_update signal. No agent-client-protocol or pydantic dependency.

**What stays:** All of R1-R12 plus R14 (ToolCall camelCase JSON aliases — naming only, no SDK).

---

## 2. Architecture

### 2.1 Workflow topology (hybrid)

```
EpochWorkflow (orchestrator — keeps EpochStateMachine)
  │
  ├── Sequential phases P1–P3, P5–P8, P11–P12
  │     → run_sequential_phase(@activity.defn)
  │
  ├── P4_REVIEW → P4ReviewWorkflow(@workflow.defn, child)
  │     - submit_vote signal received directly
  │     - gates on CONSENSUS_GATED (all 3 axes ACCEPT)
  │
  ├── P9_SLICE → [P9SliceWorkflow(@workflow.defn, child) × N]
  │     - asyncio.gather fan-out; max_concurrent_slices=20 cap
  │     - each child carries SA_EPOCH_ID
  │
  └── P10_CODE_REVIEW → P10CodeReviewWorkflow(@workflow.defn, child)
        - submit_vote signal received directly
        - tracks blocker_count; gates P10→P11 on blocker_count == 0
```

### 2.2 Cross-workflow types (R3)

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PhaseInput:
    epoch_id: str
    phase_id: PhaseId
    phase_spec: PhaseSpec

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PhaseResult:
    phase_id: PhaseId
    success:  bool
    blocker_count: int = 0
    vote_result:   VoteType | None = None
```

EpochState (mutable) stays in parent only. Never crosses workflow boundaries.

### 2.3 Gate constants — public (R2, B-1 fix)

state_machine.py: make public:
  _CONSENSUS_GATED    → CONSENSUS_GATED
  _BLOCKER_GATED      → BLOCKER_GATED
  _REVISE_DRIVES_BACK → REVISE_DRIVES_BACK

Import chain: phase_workflows.py → state_machine.py (direct). No circular imports.

### 2.4 AuditTrail — real implementation (R4)

```python
class TemporalAuditTrail:
    async def record_event(self, event: AuditEvent) -> None:
        # upsert_search_attributes for SA_EPOCH_ID, SA_PHASE, SA_ROLE, SA_STATUS
        ...

    async def query_events(self, *, phase=None, role=None) -> list[AuditEvent]:
        # ACTIVITY-ONLY: Temporal Visibility API query (network I/O)
        # MUST NOT be called from workflow code — wrap in execute_activity()
        ...

class InMemoryAuditTrail:
    # No-I/O list-backed stub for tests; satisfies AuditTrail Protocol
    ...
```

record_transition(@activity.defn): was no-op stub, now calls TemporalAuditTrail.record_event().

### 2.5 TranscriptRecorder + SecurityGate — stubs (R6, R12)

```python
class NullTranscriptRecorder:
    """TranscriptRecorder stub for unified-schema / harness integration.

    STUB — Motivation: provide a typed no-op until a harness adapter (e.g.
    aura-acp plugin, Claude Agent SDK adapter) is wired. All methods are
    intentional no-ops. Added in aura-plugins v3 (epoch: aura-plugins-eocq).
    Expected implementation: harness-specific plugin epic (aura-acp for
    Gemini/OpenCode/Codex; claude-agent-sdk adapter for Claude Code).
    """
    async def record_phase_transition(self, event: PhaseTransitionEvent) -> None: ...
    async def record_constraint_check(self, event: ConstraintCheckEvent) -> None: ...
    async def record_review_vote(self, event: ReviewVoteEvent) -> None: ...

class NullSecurityGate:
    """SecurityGate stub — allows all tool calls until harness wired.

    STUB — Motivation: provide a permissive no-op gate until Agentfilter
    (or equivalent harness security layer) is wired via harness adapter plugin.
    Added in aura-plugins v3 (epoch: aura-plugins-eocq).
    Expected implementation: aura-acp plugin for Agentfilter ACP integration.
    """
    async def check_tool_permission(self, req: ToolPermissionRequest) -> PermissionDecision:
        return PermissionDecision.ALLOW
```

### 2.6 A2A type updates (R7, R14)

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class FileWithUri:
    """A2A file reference — 3 fields per A2A spec v0.3.0 (PR #663)."""
    uri:       str
    name:      str | None = None
    mime_type: str | None = None

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class FilePart:
    file_with_uri: FileWithUri  # was: file_uri: str

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ToolCall:
    tool_name:   str
    raw_input:   str        # was: tool_input (ACP/A2A: rawInput camelCase JSON alias)
    raw_output:  str | None # was: tool_output
    tool_call_id: str | None = None  # was: absent (ACP/A2A: toolCallId)

# Full A2A part hierarchy:
@dataclass(frozen=True) class ThinkingPart:     thinking: str
@dataclass(frozen=True) class ImagePart:        data: str; mime_type: str; source_type: str = 'base64'
@dataclass(frozen=True) class AudioPart:        data: str; mime_type: str
@dataclass(frozen=True) class ResourceLinkPart: uri: str; title: str | None = None; mime_type: str | None = None

Part = TextPart | FilePart | DataPart | ThinkingPart | ImagePart | AudioPart | ResourceLinkPart
```

---

## 3. Files

### New
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| scripts/aura_protocol/phase_workflows.py | P4ReviewWorkflow, P9SliceWorkflow, P10CodeReviewWorkflow |
| scripts/aura_protocol/phase_activities.py | run_sequential_phase, emit_* activities |
| scripts/aura_protocol/audit_trail.py | TemporalAuditTrail + InMemoryAuditTrail |
| bin/worker.py | Production worker: registers all workflow + activity types |
| nix/temporal-service.nix | home-manager systemd user service for temporal server start-dev |
| tests/test_phase_workflows.py | Child workflow tests (P4 consensus, P10 blocker gate, P9 parallel) |
| tests/test_audit_trail.py | AuditTrail tests |

### Modified
| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| scripts/aura_protocol/workflow.py | Dispatch to child workflows; real record_transition; NullTranscriptRecorder/NullSecurityGate injected |
| scripts/aura_protocol/interfaces.py | FileWithUri (3 fields); FilePart.file_with_uri; ToolCall fields renamed; full A2A hierarchy; NullTranscriptRecorder + NullSecurityGate stubs; AuditTrail.query_events ACTIVITY-ONLY docstring |
| scripts/aura_protocol/state_machine.py | Public gate constants |
| pyproject.toml | version → 0.7.0 (no new runtime deps) |
| flake.nix | homeManagerModules.temporal-dev-server |
| tests/test_interfaces.py | 4 FilePart tests updated for FileWithUri; ToolCall field name tests |
| tests/test_workflow.py | Child workflow type registration |
| CHANGELOG.md | v0.7.0 entry |

---

## 4. Slices (5 vertical)

**Ordering:** SLICE-1 → SLICE-2 → SLICE-3+4 in parallel → SLICE-5

### SLICE-1: PhaseInput + workflow topology
Files: workflow.py, state_machine.py, phase_workflows.py (new), phase_activities.py (new)
Deliverables:
- PhaseInput(frozen=True), PhaseResult(frozen=True)
- P4ReviewWorkflow: submit_vote signal; CONSENSUS_GATED gate
- P9SliceWorkflow: asyncio.gather fan-out; max_concurrent_slices=20
- P10CodeReviewWorkflow: submit_vote signal; BLOCKER_GATED gate; returns blocker_count
- run_sequential_phase(@activity.defn)
- EpochWorkflow: dispatches to child workflows; NullTranscriptRecorder/NullSecurityGate wired
- CONSENSUS_GATED, BLOCKER_GATED, REVISE_DRIVES_BACK public in state_machine.py
AC: All 973 existing tests pass. Full 12-phase hybrid run completes in Temporal sandbox.

### SLICE-2: Interface + A2A type updates
Files: interfaces.py, tests/test_interfaces.py
Deliverables:
- FileWithUri(frozen=True): uri, name, mime_type
- FilePart.file_with_uri: FileWithUri (4 existing tests updated)
- ToolCall: raw_input, raw_output, tool_call_id (camelCase JSON aliases)
- ThinkingPart, ImagePart, AudioPart, ResourceLinkPart
- NullTranscriptRecorder stub (R12 label)
- NullSecurityGate stub (R12 label, returns ALLOW)
- AuditTrail.query_events: ACTIVITY-ONLY docstring
AC: FileWithUri(uri, name, mime_type) constructed. ToolCall.raw_input/raw_output/tool_call_id. All Part union variants present. isinstance(NullTranscriptRecorder(), TranscriptRecorder) True. isinstance(NullSecurityGate(), SecurityGate) True.

### SLICE-3: AuditTrail + event emission (parallel with SLICE-4)
Files: audit_trail.py (new), workflow.py, tests/test_audit_trail.py
Deliverables:
- TemporalAuditTrail: record_event (SA upsert), query_events (Visibility API — ACTIVITY-ONLY)
- InMemoryAuditTrail: list-backed test stub
- record_transition activity: now real (calls TemporalAuditTrail.record_event)
- emit_constraint_check_event, emit_review_vote_event activities
AC: record_transition not a no-op. InMemoryAuditTrail.query_events returns correct events. AuditTrail docstring warns ACTIVITY-ONLY.

### SLICE-4: Infrastructure (parallel with SLICE-3)
Files: bin/worker.py, nix/temporal-service.nix, flake.nix
Deliverables:
- bin/worker.py: registers EpochWorkflow + P4/P9/P10 child workflows + all activities; env-var/CLI config; SIGTERM shutdown
- nix/temporal-service.nix: options: enable, port (7233), ui-port (8080), namespace, db-filename, backend (dev-server|docker-compose|native)
- systemd.user.services.temporal-dev-server
- flake.nix: homeManagerModules.temporal-dev-server
AC: bin/worker.py --help clean. nix/temporal-service.nix evaluates. homeManagerModules.temporal-dev-server exported.

### SLICE-5: Tests + version bump + CHANGELOG
Files: tests/test_phase_workflows.py, tests/test_workflow.py, pyproject.toml, CHANGELOG.md
Deliverables:
- test_phase_workflows.py: P4 consensus, P10 blocker gate, P9 parallel; max_concurrent_slices enforcement
- test_workflow.py: child workflow type registration; updated worker registration
- pyproject.toml: version = 0.7.0 (no new runtime deps — temporalio>=1.9.0 only)
- CHANGELOG.md: v0.7.0 section
AC: All tests pass. Version 0.7.0. No new runtime deps.

---

## 5. Design decisions

D1: Hybrid topology — P4/P9/P10 as child workflows; P1-P3, P5-P8, P11-P12 as activities.
D2: EpochStateMachine stays in parent. Gate constants public for phase_workflows import.
D3: PhaseInput/PhaseResult are the only cross-workflow types. EpochState never crosses boundaries.
D4: AuditTrail.query_events is ACTIVITY-ONLY (Temporal Visibility = network I/O = non-deterministic).
D5: NullTranscriptRecorder/NullSecurityGate are permissive stubs. Real implementations are harness plugins (aura-acp, claude-agent-sdk adapter) in future epics.
D6: No harness SDK dependency in core. pyproject.toml runtime: temporalio>=1.9.0 only.
D7: ACP integration (R13, R15-R17) deferred to aura-acp plugin epic. Claude Code gets its own adapter (not ACP-based — ACP feature request open, not shipped).
D8: R14 (ToolCall camelCase) retained — naming convention only, no SDK dependency.

---

## 6. Acceptance criteria

AC1: All 973 existing tests pass after topology refactor.
AC2: P4ReviewWorkflow, P9SliceWorkflow, P10CodeReviewWorkflow pass Temporal sandbox tests.
AC3: isinstance(NullTranscriptRecorder(), TranscriptRecorder) and isinstance(NullSecurityGate(), SecurityGate) both True.
AC4: ToolCall.raw_input, ToolCall.raw_output, ToolCall.tool_call_id accessible.
AC5: FilePart.file_with_uri is FileWithUri. FileWithUri has 3 fields: uri, name, mime_type.
AC6: bin/worker.py --help exits cleanly.
AC7: homeManagerModules.temporal-dev-server exported from flake.nix.
AC8: record_transition activity is not a no-op (test asserts PhaseTransitionEvent emitted).
AC9: All stubs carry R12-compliant labels (motivation, epoch, expected implementation).
AC10: pyproject.toml version = 0.7.0; no new runtime deps; CHANGELOG has v0.7.0 section.
AC11: P9SliceWorkflow respects max_concurrent_slices cap (default 20).
AC12: AuditTrail.query_events docstring contains ACTIVITY-ONLY warning.
AC13: 4 existing FilePart tests pass with new FileWithUri structure.

## Reviewer C — Elegance Axis — Round 2

### Vote: ACCEPT

### Axis: Is complexity proportional to the problem?

### Round 1 Finding Resolution

Round 1 voted REVISE because 9 slices were over-engineered for a sequential docs refactoring. Recommended consolidation to 2-3 slices following the natural dependency chain: canonical source -> substantive rewrites -> mechanical propagation.

PROPOSAL-2 addresses this exactly:
- SLICE-A (schema.xml): Canonical source, done first. Real complexity lives here.
- SLICE-B (agent def + skills): Substantive rewrites consuming schema changes. 4 files with meaningful rewrite scope.
- SLICE-C (derived docs): Mechanical propagation. 5 files, mostly find-and-replace.

Dependency chain A->B->C is well-reasoned and matches the sequential nature of the work.

### Elegance Assessment

1. **Complexity proportional?** Yes. 3 slices for 3 natural layers of change. No trivial single-line slices. No unnecessary coordination overhead.
2. **API designed for need?** The agents/supervisor.md definition with explicit tools field is exactly the structural enforcement needed — not more, not less.
3. **No over-engineering?** Correct. No plugin systems, no abstractions. Straight file edits organized by dependency order.
4. **No under-engineering?** Correct. Clean review exit definition (0 BLOCKERs + 0 IMPORTANTs) is precisely specified. Autonomous progression lists user-gated phases exhaustively.

### What Improved from PROPOSAL-1
- 9 slices -> 3 slices (my finding)
- Validation checklist now mechanically testable with grep/test commands (Reviewer B)
- BDD criteria reframed as document-verification assertions (Reviewer B)
- R7 atomic commits coverage added (Reviewer A)
- Architect skill autonomous progression added (Reviewer A)

### End-User Impact
Fewer slices = fewer handoffs = lower risk of cross-file inconsistency. The sequential dependency chain ensures each layer is correct before the next begins. End-users (agent operators) get a coherent, consistent protocol update.

## Scope

---
references:
  request: aura-plugins-3ubig
  urd: aura-plugins-q72mt
  proposal: aura-plugins-lkcal
---

## Reviewer A — Correctness Axis (Round 1)

### Vote: REVISE

### Axis Questions

1. **Does the proposal faithfully serve the user's original request?**
   Mostly yes. 5 of 7 URD requirements are fully addressed. Two gaps identified below.

2. **Are technical decisions consistent with the URD requirements?**
   Yes. All 6 tradeoff decisions (T1-T6) map directly to URD design choices (D1-D6). BDD acceptance criteria cover R1-R6 (except R7). The engineering tradeoffs table is well-reasoned and traceable.

3. **Are there gaps where the proposal says one thing but the URD says another?**
   Two gaps found (see below). No contradictions — these are omissions, not conflicts.

### End-User Alignment

1. **Who are the end-users?** Developers/operators who launch and interact with aura supervisor agents.
2. **What would end-users want?** Supervisors that do not waste context exploring, proceed autonomously through non-gated phases, and produce clean-reviewed code before UAT.
3. **How would this affect them?** The proposal fixes the core pain points. However, omitting the architect skill from autonomous progression means the most user-visible symptom ("Should I proceed to URE?") may persist.
4. **Are there implementation gaps?** Yes — GAP-1 and GAP-2 below.
5. **Does MVP scope make sense?** Yes, given the two gaps are addressed. All 9 slices are documentation/protocol changes with no production code risk.
6. **Is validation checklist complete and correct?** No — missing R7 item and architect skill coverage for autonomous progression.

### Findings

**GAP-1: Autonomous progression not applied to architect skill (R5 coverage)**
- URD R5 states: "This must be documented in the epoch skill, supervisor skill, and architect skill."
- Proposal covers epoch/SKILL.md (SLICE-8) and supervisor/SKILL.md (SLICE-3) but has NO slice touching skills/architect/SKILL.md.
- The user's original complaint includes architect-phase symptoms: "Should I proceed to URE?", "Should I handoff to supervisor?" These occur in phases 1-7, which are architect-owned.
- Without updating the architect skill, the most user-visible autonomous progression issue goes unaddressed.
- **Suggestion:** Add a SLICE-10 (or fold into existing slices) that updates skills/architect/SKILL.md with: (a) a C-autonomous-progression constraint for architect phases, (b) updated phase transition behaviors to proceed without prompting for non-gated phases 1/3/4/7.

**GAP-2: URD R7 (atomic commits, early integration) absent from proposal**
- URD R7 requires: "Supervisor plans integration points between slices. Commit small and often — aim for atomic commits. Integrate early and often to minimize drift between slices."
- No slice in the proposal addresses documenting R7 in any file.
- The validation checklist has no item for R7.
- **Suggestion:** Add R7 content to SLICE-3 (supervisor/SKILL.md, under the Ride the Wave or Build stage) and add a validation checklist item: "R7 atomic commits and early integration documented in supervisor skill."

### What Is Correct

- R1 (Cartographer removal): Comprehensive — all 9 files identified with line numbers.
- R2 (Agent definition): Matches URD exactly — tools, format, exclusions.
- R3 (Clean review exit): Strict definition (no BLOCKERs + no IMPORTANTs) matches D5.
- R4 (Worker persistence): Default subagents with TeamCreate escape hatch matches D3.
- R6 (Verbatim documentation): Covered in BDD acceptance criteria.
- T1-T6 tradeoffs are well-structured and traceable to URD design choices.
- Slice decomposition is logical and file-scoped.
title: PROPOSAL-1-REVIEW-A-1: Rework supervisor role

## Review Axis: TEST QUALITY

**Vote: REVISE**

### 1. Validation Checklist Testability

| # | Checklist Item | Testable? | Gap |
|---|----------------|-----------|-----|
| 1 | No remaining Cartographer references | Yes (grep) | None |
| 2 | No remaining standing explore/dual-role | Yes (grep) | None |
| 3 | agents/supervisor.md exists with correct tools | Partially | No verification that EXCLUDED tools are absent from tools list |
| 4 | schema.xml constraint replaced | Yes | None |
| 5 | schema.xml standing-teams removed | Yes | None |
| 6 | Ride the Wave rewritten in 3 files | No | "Rewritten" has no mechanically verifiable definition |
| 7 | Clean review exit in all relevant locations | Partially | "All relevant locations" is vague — which files exactly? |
| 8 | Autonomous progression in 3 skills | Partially | Which specific files must contain it? |
| 9 | P10 transition updated | Yes | None |
| 10 | All files self-consistent | No | Untestable without concrete consistency definition |

Items 6, 7, 8, 10 cannot be mechanically verified as written.

### 2. BDD Criteria Mismatch

The BDD criteria are written as runtime behavioral specs (e.g., "when supervisor attempts Edit, then tools not available") but the deliverable is a documentation/protocol refactoring with no code changes. These criteria cannot be independently verified against the actual output of this proposal. They should be reframed as document-verification assertions.

Example fix: Instead of "Given supervisor agent launched, when attempts Edit/Write, then tools not available" write "Given agents/supervisor.md exists, when parsing its tools frontmatter field, then Edit/Write/NotebookEdit are absent from the list."

### 3. Coverage Gaps

- **Gap 1:** No concrete definition for "self-consistent." Should specify: every constraint ID in schema.xml has a matching section in the relevant SKILL.md; every transition condition in schema.xml matches the prose in PROCESS.md.
- **Gap 2:** Checklist items 1-2 (negative grep) should specify the full search scope explicitly (all files in skills/ and agents/).
- **Gap 3:** No verification that the 5 user-gated phases are exhaustive — all phases in schema.xml should be categorized.
- **Gap 4:** Tool restriction enforcement mechanism is assumed but not verified. Proposal should note how this will be confirmed.
- **Gap 5:** URD R7 (atomic commits, early integration) has no BDD criterion or checklist item. Must be scoped in or explicitly scoped out.

### 4. Actionable Suggestions

1. Replace vague checklist items (6, 7, 8, 10) with grep-verifiable checks specifying exact file sets and expected content patterns.
2. Reframe BDD criteria as document assertions that match the documentation-only deliverable.
3. Add checklist item: "All phases in schema.xml are categorized as user-gated or autonomous."
4. Add checklist item for R7 or explicitly state R7 is out of scope for this proposal.
5. Define "self-consistent" concretely with at least 3 verifiable cross-file checks (e.g., constraint IDs match, transition conditions match, tool lists match).
6. Add a verification note for tool restriction enforcement (e.g., "manual test: launch supervisor and confirm Edit is unavailable").

Python Protocol interfaces for cross-project integration + A2A-compatible content types. Uses structural subtyping (typing.Protocol, runtime_checkable).

### Protocol Interfaces
- ConstraintValidatorInterface — validate(state) -> list[ConstraintViolation]
- TranscriptRecorder — record phase transitions, constraint checks, review votes

- ToolCall (MCP-compatible tool call representation)
- ModelId (models.dev {provider}/{model} composite identifier)

### Event Stub Types (frozen dataclasses)
- PhaseTransitionEvent, ConstraintCheckEvent, ReviewVoteEvent
- AuditEvent, ToolPermissionRequest, PermissionDecision

## Files Owned

- AC10: Given ModelId.parse("anthropic/claude-opus-4-6") when parsed then provider="anthropic" model="claude-opus-4-6" should not accept strings without "/"

description:
Reviewer B (Test Quality) — SLICE-1 (types.py / __init__.py) minor-level findings.

## Finding: TestConstraintSpecs.test_known_constraints_present uses a fixed hardcoded subset — does not verify ALL 22 constraints (MINOR)

test_aura_types.py line 289-294 checks 8 specific constraint IDs are present. The schema-sync integration test (test_schema_types_sync.py) does verify ALL constraints against schema.xml, so this is covered at the integration level. However, the unit test could be replaced with a count check (assert len(CONSTRAINT_SPECS) == 22) to make the intent clearer. This is minor because the integration test provides full coverage.

## Finding: _make_state helper in test_constraints.py uses **kwargs pass-through to EpochState constructor — obscures which fields are being set in individual tests (MINOR)

The _make_state(phase, epoch_id, **kwargs) helper delegates all extra kwargs directly to EpochState. While functional, this means a reader cannot see from the helper signature which fields are valid. Named helper variants (_make_state_at_p4_with_votes, _make_state_at_p10_with_votes, _all_accept_state) partially address this. Recommend adding typed overloads or named parameters for the most common cases (blocker_count, current_role). This improves fixture clarity but does not affect correctness.
title: SLICE-1-REVIEW-B-1 MINOR

## Finding

skills.yaml sub_skill_cases contains only one entry (cmd-sup-plan). The Python gen_skills.py generates sub-skills for multiple commands across multiple roles. If a second command's sub-skill template were to break (e.g., missing template variable for a different command type), no fixture would catch it.

## Impact

Low. The single case still exercises the full GenerateSubSkill code path. A second case for a reviewer or worker command would give broader regression coverage.

## How To Fix

Add 1-2 additional sub_skill_cases to skills.yaml, e.g. a reviewer command or architect command, each with a small must_contain list (command name, section header). No code changes required.

## VOTE: ACCEPT

Reviewer B (Test Quality axis), Round 2.

### I-1 Resolution: VERIFIED

The new test `test_checklist_renders_gate_subheadings` (commit `67e3fbe`) in class `TestCompletionChecklistSection` adequately addresses the I-1 finding from Round 1.

**What the test does:**
- Renders a Worker skill via `generate_skill(RoleId.WORKER, ...)` using `_minimal_with_markers()` (same integration path as sibling tests)
- Asserts `"gates:**" in result`, which matches the template output `**completion gates:**` from `skill_header.j2` line 111: `**{{ cl.gate.value }} gates:**`
- Includes a descriptive assertion message

**Why it is sufficient:**
- The template loop `{% for cl in checklists %}` is generic across gate types, so testing one gate type (completion) exercises the subheading rendering path for all gate types
- This matches my Round 1 suggestion: "One assertion suffices since the template loop is generic"
- The test is an integration test (renders full skill output, not isolated template), consistent with the Axis B preference for integration over unit tests

**FR13 status changed: PARTIAL -> PASS**

### Round 1 MINOR Findings: Still Standing

All 5 MINOR findings from Round 1 remain applicable and are suitable for the existing follow-up epic. Verified each one still applies:

| ID | Finding | Still Stands? | Verification |
|----|---------|---------------|--------------|
| M-1 | Vacuous-pass risk in `test_generated_schema_code_elements_use_cdata` (no `assert code_matches` guard) | Yes | `tests/test_gen_schema.py:1354-1358` — loop still has no non-empty precondition |
| M-2 | No test for `]]>` inside CDATA content | Yes | No test for CDATA terminator in content found in test suite |
| M-3 | `test_constraint_with_examples_renders_code_fence` filters all constraints, not worker-only | Yes | `tests/test_gen_skills.py:1448-1451` — filter is `CONSTRAINT_SPECS.values()` without role filtering |
| M-4 | Residual `>=` assertions in `test_schema_ext_types.py` | Yes | Lines 375 (`>= 3` for worker-completion items) and 394 (`>= 3` for shared commands) |
| M-5 | No explicit test for `CL-worker-production-path` checklist item ID | Yes | No match for `CL-worker-production-path` in any test file |

### Severity Summary

| Severity | Count | Details |
|----------|-------|---------|
| BLOCKER | 0 | None |
| IMPORTANT | 0 | I-1 resolved |
| MINOR | 5 | M-1 through M-5 (unchanged from Round 1, tracked on follow-up epic) |

### Verdict

**ACCEPT** — The single IMPORTANT finding from Round 1 has been resolved with an appropriate integration test. No new issues introduced. All 5 MINOR findings remain as follow-up items (no action required for this cycle). No BLOCKERs.

close_reason: Superseded — PROPOSAL-1 closed, work delivered via PROPOSAL-5
description:
VOTE: ACCEPT — The proposal's architecture is clean and well-proportioned to the problem.

## Elegance Axis Evaluation

### Module Boundaries: GOOD

The proposal introduces four new modules (`config.py`, `formatters.py`, `sqlite_audit.py`, `dual_audit.py`) with clear single-responsibility boundaries:

- **config.py** owns resolution logic (CLI > env > YAML > defaults) for both `aurad` and `aura-msg`. This avoids duplicating connection resolution in two binaries — correct application of DRY. The `ConnectionConfig`, `AuradConfig`, and `AuraMsgConfig` frozen dataclasses are minimal and non-overlapping.
- **formatters.py** isolates output formatting from CLI arg parsing and from workflow logic. This is the right boundary: the CLI dispatches, the formatter renders, neither knows about the other's internals.
- **sqlite_audit.py** and **dual_audit.py** cleanly separate the storage backend from the composition strategy. `DualWriteAuditTrail` composes `SqliteAuditTrail` rather than inheriting — correct use of has-a over is-a.

No module is doing two jobs. No two modules are doing the same job.

### Naming Conventions: CONSISTENT

- Module names follow the existing `snake_case.py` convention in `scripts/aura_protocol/`.
- Dataclass naming (`SliceExecutionConfig`, `SliceStartSignal`, `SliceCompleteSignal`) follows the existing `{Noun}{Purpose}` pattern already established by `PhaseAdvanceSignal`, `ReviewVoteSignal`, `SliceProgressSignal`.
- Exit code constants (`EXIT_SUCCESS`, `EXIT_USER_ERROR`, `EXIT_CONNECTION_ERROR`, `EXIT_WORKFLOW_ERROR`) are properly named screaming-snake constants with clear semantics, not magic numbers.
- CLI subcommand naming (`query state`, `signal vote`, `phase advance`, `epoch start`) follows a consistent `{noun} {verb/noun}` two-word pattern that is both extensible and discoverable.

### Complexity Proportionality: APPROPRIATE

1. **No over-engineering detected.** The proposal does not introduce plugin systems, abstract factories, or premature extension points. The `AuditTrail` Protocol already exists in `interfaces.py` — `SqliteAuditTrail` is simply a second concrete implementation, which is the right time to have an abstraction (when there are two real consumers).

2. **No under-engineering detected.** The dual-write architecture (SQLite for queries + Temporal SA for forensic UI) matches the innate complexity of having two query surfaces. The proposal correctly notes the Temporal determinism boundary: SA upserts in workflow code, not in activities — this is a critical correctness constraint that would be easy to get wrong.

3. **SliceWorkflow execution modes** (shell + signal-driven + heartbeat) introduce real complexity, but it is innate complexity from R9. The proposal does not try to abstract this into a strategy pattern or execution framework — it uses a simple if/else on `execution_config.command` presence, which is proportional.

4. **Config resolution priority chain** (CLI > env > YAML > defaults) is standard practice and not over-engineered. The single `resolve_connection()` function serves both tools.

### Slice Decomposition: LOGICAL

The 7-slice decomposition respects dependency ordering:
- Slices 1 and 2 are independent prerequisites (rename + config) — good parallelism.
- Slices 3 and 4 build the subcommand chain incrementally (read-only first, then write operations).
- Slice 5 (audit trail) depends only on config (Slice 2), not on the CLI — correct isolation.
- Slice 6 (SliceWorkflow) depends only on config — correctly independent from CLI slices.
- Slice 7 (hooks) depends on Slice 3 (needs `aura-msg query state` to work) — correct sequencing.

No slice is too large or too small. Each has a clear deliverable boundary.

### Minor Observations (not blocking)

1. **`dual_audit.py` is thin.** The `DualWriteAuditTrail` class delegates `record_event()` to SQLite only and explicitly notes SA upserts happen in workflow code. This means the "dual-write" name slightly overpromises — the class is really a `SqliteAuditTrail` wrapper with a note about where the other write goes. This is a naming nuance, not an architectural flaw. The proposal's decision log (D5) explains the rationale clearly.

2. **Config file has two sections with identical connection fields.** The `aurad` and `aura-msg` sections both list `namespace`, `task_queue`, `server_address`. A shared `connection` section could reduce duplication, but the current design is clearer for users who only run one tool. Acceptable tradeoff.

3. **The hook script (`session-register.sh`) is minimal.** It queries state if `AURA_EPOCH_ID` is set and exits silently otherwise. This is correct graceful degradation (D10), not under-engineering.

### End-User Impact

- End-users (agent developers using `aura-msg`) get a discoverable two-level CLI with `--help` at every level.
- The `--format json|text` flag respects both programmatic (hooks) and human (terminal) use cases.
- Config file at XDG path means zero-config local development with sensible defaults, while supporting production deployment via YAML.
- Exit codes 0-3 enable reliable scripting and hook error handling.

### Conclusion

The design matches the innate complexity of the problem without adding gratuitous abstraction. Module boundaries are clean. Naming is consistent with the existing codebase. The slice decomposition is logical with correct dependency ordering and good parallelism on independent slices.
title: PROPOSAL-1-REVIEW-C-1: aurad + aura-msg elegance

## Review Axis: Elegance

**VOTE: ACCEPT**

### Summary

The proposal is structurally clean, idiomatically Go, and appropriately complex for the problem. The pkg/internal split is correct, naming conventions are consistent, and the interface surfaces are well-scoped. No over-engineering or unnecessary abstraction is present.

### Findings

**Strengths:**

1. **pkg/protocol vs internal/ split is correct.** Only true convergence types (`PhaseId`, `EventType`, `AuditEvent`, `SessionEntry`) are exported to `pkg/protocol`. Everything else stays internal. This mirrors the reference repo pattern (`pkg/schema` for shared types, `internal/` for implementation). The rule is clear and will hold as the codebase grows.

2. **Dependency injection via struct (T7) is idiomatic Go.** Replacing the Python global `AuditTrail` singleton with DI through constructors is the right call. The `Trail` interface is minimal — two methods — and testable with an in-memory implementation.

3. **Handler functions are CLI-framework-agnostic (T2/D4).** Standalone `func QueryState(ctx, conn, epochID, fmt) (int, error)` functions are testable in isolation. The Cobra `RunE` → handler dispatch pattern is the correct Go idiom (mirrored in the reference repo's `cmd_*.go` files).

4. **Signal/query constants in `internal/temporal/constants.go` (T3/D10).** Prevents name drift between pastured and pasture-msg. Compile-time safety. Simple and correct.

5. **`internal/types/` vs `pkg/protocol/` split.** Internal types (`PhaseId` in types, `PhaseId` in protocol) could be a naming collision concern, but the proposal clearly intends `pkg/protocol.PhaseId` as the canonical public type and `internal/types/` as supporting enums (`VoteType`, `ReviewAxis`, `Domain`, `RoleId`). This is clean.

6. **`StructuredError` (T8) implements `error` + `Report(io.Writer)`.** Testable via `bytes.Buffer`. Actionable fields (what/why/impact/fix). Not over-engineered — no reflection, no dynamic dispatch. Correct.

7. **`modernc.org/sqlite` (T4).** Pure Go, CGO_ENABLED=0 compatible. Appropriate for audit trail workload (low-volume writes, no high-throughput reads). The tradeoff is clearly documented and proportionate.

8. **Wave schedule is sound.** S1/S2/S3 as foundation, then parallel core, then integration. Wave 4 (pasture-release) correctly noted as independent. No false dependencies.

9. **Naming conventions are consistent.** `pasture-*` for Go binaries, `aura-*` for Python tools. Module path `github.com/dayvidpham/pasture` is clean (no vanity domain, no `/v2`). Aligns with D1/D2 in URD.

10. **`ParsePhaseId` supporting multiple formats** ("p1", "1", "request", "p1_request") is appropriate — agents may produce any of these and the parser is the right place to normalize.

**Minor observations (non-blocking):**

- `AuditTrail` string field in `PasturedConfig` uses `"memory" | "sqlite"` as raw strings. A typed `AuditTrailBackend` enum exists in `internal/types/config_types.go` — the config struct should use that type directly rather than `string`. The proposal has this enum defined but the config struct is stringly-typed. Not a blocker since it's an implementation detail, but worth noting for the implementation slices.

- `SemVer.Bump(kind string)` takes a string argument (`"major" | "minor" | "patch"`) rather than a typed enum. There is a `BumpKind` type opportunity here. Again, implementation detail — the interface defines what's needed.

- `internal/acp/` has both `Client` (struct) and `SessionHandler` (interface). The struct is not exported, which is correct for an internal package. However, `NewClient(handler SessionHandler) *Client` returning a concrete `*Client` from an internal package is fine since callers inside the same module will use it.

- The `Exec(cmd string, args ...string) error` on `PluginRegistry` is a shell-execution method on what is otherwise a data type. This is a slight mixing of concerns (registry as data model vs registry as command runner). Acceptable for MVP scope; could be refactored later into a separate `RegistryRunner` type.

**No blockers identified.**

The design complexity is proportional to the innate complexity of the problem (Temporal-backed multi-agent orchestration + ACP client + polyrepo release management). Nothing is added without purpose. The architecture aligns with the reference repo patterns where directly comparable (`pkg/schema` → `pkg/protocol`, `cmd/aura/cmd_*.go` → `cmd/pasture-msg/*.go`, `internal/config` → `internal/config`).

description:
VOTE: ACCEPT - All three round-1 blockers are resolved.

## BLOCKER-1 (Double-bump guard): RESOLVED
The guard `resolved_mp not in {vf.path.resolve() for vf in files}` is correct and sufficient. MarketplaceEntry.path is already resolved at load time (expanduser().resolve() in load()), and vf.path.resolve() is called inline for the comparison. Symlinks and ~ expansion are both handled. Guard is correct.

## BLOCKER-2 (Rollback hazard): RESOLVED
Registry marketplace write is placed AFTER the try/except block (after git commit and tag). This correctly removes the rollback hazard: if the marketplace write fails post-commit, the release is already committed and tagged locally; the user can fix the external file manually. The rationale in the proposal is sound.

## BLOCKER-3 (Entry-level deserialization): RESOLVED
PluginRegistry.load() now explicitly checks `if 'name' not in p or 'path' not in p` per plugin entry, prints a warning to stderr, and continues (warn+skip). This satisfies R6 spirit. Structural failures (invalid JSON, missing marketplaces key) still exit 2.

## New findings (non-blocking)

### NOTABLE: Marketplace entry missing 'path' key not symmetrically guarded
`m.get('path', '')` for marketplace entries will resolve '' to CWD if path key is absent. This is the same pattern as the old BLOCKER-3 but one level up. Since the marketplace entry IS checked via `mp_path.exists()` (CWD always exists), a malformed entry missing 'path' would NOT be warned/skipped — it would silently try to treat CWD as a marketplace.json path. This is a minor correctness gap, acceptable at MVP scope (registries are user-constructed), but should be tracked as a follow-up hardening item.

### MINOR: Pseudocode uses new_version_str vs actual variable new_str
Naming inconsistency in SLICE-5 pseudocode; implementation must bind to the actual variable name. No blocker.

## Summary
The proposal correctly addresses all stated user requirements. The three correctness blockers from round 1 are resolved with appropriate mechanisms. One minor gap exists (empty/missing marketplace path key) but is narrow in scope and does not affect the primary use case. ACCEPT.

description:

references:
  review: aura-plugins-qxgg
---

## Finding

scripts/aura_protocol/gen_skills.py:292-318 duplicates the filtering logic for checklists, coordination_commands, workflows, and review_axes that is already computed inside get_role_context() in context_injection.py.

The two implementations are identical in behavior:
- checklists: spec.role_ref == role
- coordination_commands: cmd.role_ref == role or cmd.shared
- workflows: wf.role_ref == role
- review_axes: REVIEW_AXIS_SPECS.values() if role == RoleId.REVIEWER else []

RoleContext already stores checklists, coordination_commands, workflows, and review_axes with the correct types. _render_header() ignores them and recomputes from scratch instead.

## Why It Matters

1. Maintenance coupling: if filtering rules change (e.g., 'shared' semantics for coordination commands), both context_injection.py AND gen_skills.py must be updated. No static enforcement links them.
2. Undermines RoleContext value: the fields on RoleContext are precomputed but not used by the primary consumer (gen_skills.py).
3. Inconsistency: constraints use get_role_context() via _constraints_for_role(); all other new fields bypass it.

## Fix

Call get_role_context(role_id) once in _render_header() and pull checklists, coordination_commands, workflows, review_axes from the returned RoleContext instead of re-filtering from canonical dicts.

Constraint: for constraints specifically, the workaround (look up ConstraintSpec by ID) is needed because RoleContext.constraints is frozenset[ConstraintContext] (a stripped-down type), not frozenset[ConstraintSpec]. This is a separate latent issue but does not apply to the other four fields.
title: IMPORTANT-C-1: DRY violation — filtering logic duplicated between context_injection.py and gen_skills.py

# URD: Automated Release Tagging and Version Bumping

## 1. Purpose
A CLI tool (bin/aura-release) that automates version bumping, CHANGELOG generation, git commit, and annotated tagging for the aura-plugins repository.

## 2. Scope
- Single Python 3.10+ script, stdlib only (zero external dependencies)
- Follows existing bin/ tool conventions (argparse, same shebang pattern)
- Operates on the local git repository only (no push)

## 3. Functional Requirements

### FR-1: Version Bump
- Accept bump type: major | minor | patch
- Parse current version from pyproject.toml (source of truth)
- Compute new version per semver rules
- Update version in exactly 3 files:
  - pyproject.toml: project.version field
  - .claude-plugin/plugin.json: top-level version field
  - .claude-plugin/marketplace.json: metadata.version AND plugins[0].version
- Must NOT modify plugins[1].version (agentfilter, external)

### FR-2: Version Consistency Check
- Before bumping, validate all 3 files have the same version
- If versions are inconsistent, offer a --sync flag to align them to pyproject.toml's version before bumping
- On first run with existing drift, --sync aligns marketplace.json to current 0.2.2

### FR-3: CHANGELOG Generation
- Auto-generate CHANGELOG.md entries from git log between tags
- Use "Keep a Changelog" format (https://keepachangelog.com)
- Group commits by type using conventional commit prefixes (feat, fix, chore, etc.)
- If no prior tag exists, include all commits since initial commit

### FR-4: Git Commit
- Stage the 3 version files + CHANGELOG.md
- Create commit with message: "chore: release vX.Y.Z"

### FR-5: Git Tag
- Create annotated tag: vX.Y.Z
- Tag message: "Release vX.Y.Z"

## 4. Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-1: Pre-flight Checks
- Working tree must be clean (no uncommitted changes beyond .beads/)
- Must be on a branch (not detached HEAD)
- Current version must parse as valid semver

### NFR-2: Dry Run
- Support --dry-run flag showing what would happen without making changes

### NFR-3: Error Handling
- Atomic: if any step fails, roll back file changes (git checkout the files)
- Clear error messages for each failure mode

### NFR-4: Idempotency
- Running with --sync when versions already match is a no-op

## 5. CLI Interface

```
aura-release <major|minor|patch> [--dry-run] [--sync] [--no-changelog] [--no-commit] [--no-tag]
aura-release --check  # just validate version consistency
```

## 6. Files Affected
- bin/aura-release (NEW — the tool itself)
- pyproject.toml (version field)
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json (version field)
- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (2 version fields)
- CHANGELOG.md (created/appended)
title: URD: Release automation requirements document

description:
VOTE: REVISE — The proposal is strong overall and covers 14 of 15 requirements with clear, well-reasoned design. However, there are several correctness issues that must be addressed before ratification.

---
references:
  request: aura-plugins-oqhjg
  urd: aura-plugins-bwfqm
  proposal: aura-plugins-ux9an
---

## Correctness Findings

### ISSUE-1 (MUST FIX): SliceResult missing `output` field — BDD criterion contradicts existing type

The proposal's BDD acceptance criterion states:

> Given SliceWorkflow with command="python run_slice.py" When command exits 0 Then SliceResult(success=True, output=stdout)

But the existing `SliceResult` dataclass (workflow.py:184-194) only has `slice_id`, `success`, and `error`. There is no `output` field. The proposal's `execute_slice_command` activity is defined as returning `SliceResult`, but the proposal never updates the `SliceResult` dataclass to include an `output: str` field.

Similarly, the `SliceCompleteSignal` has an `output: str` field, but the signal-driven path also needs `SliceResult` to carry the output forward.

**Fix:** Explicitly add `output: str = ""` to `SliceResult` in the Modified Modules section (Section 7), alongside the existing `error` field. This is a breaking change to an existing type and must be called out.

### ISSUE-2 (MUST FIX): `format_epoch_state` claims `available_transitions` is part of EpochState — it is not

The proposal's BDD criterion states:

> Given aura-msg query state --epoch-id E1 --format json When epoch E1 is running Then stdout contains JSON with current_phase, current_role, available_transitions

And the validation checklist item says: `aura-msg query state --epoch-id X` returns JSON by default.

But `available_transitions` is NOT a field on `EpochState`. It is a computed property on `EpochStateMachine` (state_machine.py:156), and it is exposed as a *separate* workflow query (`EpochWorkflow.available_transitions()` at workflow.py:486-495).

The `cmd_query_state` implementation would need to issue TWO queries (one for `current_state`, one for `available_transitions`) and merge results, OR the `current_state` query must be modified to return a richer DTO that includes available transitions. Neither approach is specified.

**Fix:** Either (a) define a new `EpochStateView` DTO that combines `EpochState` fields + available transitions, or (b) have the CLI issue two queries and merge locally, and document this explicitly.

### ISSUE-3 (MUST FIX): R2 requirement coverage gap — `--reviewer-id` missing from `ReviewVoteSignal` mapping

The proposal's `signal vote` subcommand accepts `--reviewer-id` as a required argument. The `ReviewVoteSignal` dataclass (workflow.py:123-137) does have a `reviewer_id: str` field. However, the proposal's example implementation in Section 5 shows the argparse setup but omits the `cmd_signal_vote` implementation body. Since the mapping from CLI args to signal dataclass is the critical path for correctness, the stub `async def cmd_signal_vote(client, args, fmt): ...` should at minimum show the construction of `ReviewVoteSignal(axis=ReviewAxis(args.axis), vote=VoteType(args.vote.upper()), reviewer_id=args.reviewer_id)` to confirm the enum conversions are correct.

The issue: `--vote` accepts lowercase `accept|revise` but `VoteType` values are uppercase `ACCEPT|REVISE`. The proposal doesn't show the case conversion. This is a correctness risk in the implementation.

**Fix:** Show the `ReviewVoteSignal` construction in the stub body, including the `.upper()` call or `VoteType(args.vote)` relying on StrEnum case-insensitive matching (which Python StrEnum does NOT support — `VoteType("accept")` raises ValueError; it must be `VoteType("ACCEPT")`).

### ISSUE-4 (SHOULD FIX): DualWriteAuditTrail design note contradicts itself

The proposal correctly identifies the Temporal determinism boundary in Section 4:

> Note: Temporal SA upserts happen in workflow code after activity returns, not inside the activity. DualWriteAuditTrail manages the SQLite side; the workflow manages SA upserts.

But the class is named `DualWriteAuditTrail` and the description says it "composes SqliteAuditTrail + Temporal search attribute upserts." The `record_event()` implementation shown only writes to SQLite, with a comment that SA upsert happens elsewhere. This means:

1. `DualWriteAuditTrail` is NOT actually a dual-write — it's just a `SqliteAuditTrail` wrapper with no additional behavior.
2. The SA upsert logic is not specified anywhere. Where exactly in the workflow code would it go? The existing `EpochWorkflow.run()` already upserts SA on transitions, but `AuditEvent` recording is a separate concern.

**Fix:** Either (a) rename to just use `SqliteAuditTrail` directly since the wrapper adds no behavior and the SA upserts already exist in workflow code, or (b) specify exactly where in the workflow the SA upsert for audit events would be triggered and what additional SA keys would be needed beyond the 5 already defined.

### ISSUE-5 (SHOULD FIX): `phase advance --to-phase` accepts string but `PhaseAdvanceSignal.to_phase` requires PhaseId enum

The `phase advance` subcommand has `--to-phase` as a required string argument. But `PhaseAdvanceSignal.to_phase` is typed as `PhaseId`. The proposal doesn't specify how the string-to-enum conversion happens, nor does it show validation. If the user passes `--to-phase p2`, does `PhaseId("p2")` work? Yes, because `PhaseId` is a `StrEnum` with values like `"p1"`, `"p2"`, etc. But this should be documented, and invalid values should produce exit code 1 with a helpful message, not a Python traceback.

**Fix:** Add `choices=[p.value for p in PhaseId]` to the argparse argument, or show the try/except ValueError handling that maps to exit code 1.

### ISSUE-6 (MINOR): R2 says "remaining subcommands" but doesn't mention R3 integration

R3 requires all subcommands to support `--format json|text`. The proposal has `--format` as a global flag on the top-level parser, which is correct. But the `default_format` from YAML config is only mentioned in `AuraMsgConfig` — the priority resolution for format (CLI `--format` > YAML `default_format` > hardcoded `json`) is not explicitly shown in the command implementations.

### ISSUE-7 (MINOR): Validation checklist item "All existing 1017 tests still pass" — no mechanism

The checklist says all 1017 existing tests must still pass, but the proposal modifies `SliceInput` (adding `execution_config: SliceExecutionConfig`) with a default value. This is backwards-compatible. However, the `SliceResult` output field addition (from ISSUE-1) would also need a default value to remain backwards-compatible. The proposal should confirm backwards compatibility explicitly.

## Requirements Coverage

| Req | Covered? | Notes |
|-----|----------|-------|
| R1  | Yes | Rename aura-msg.py -> aura-msg, clear |
| R2  | Yes | Nested subcommands via argparse, well-designed |
| R3  | Yes | --format json/text with json default |
| R4  | Yes | CLI > env > YAML > defaults config resolution |
| R5  | Partial | query state works but available_transitions sourcing unclear (ISSUE-2) |
| R6  | Partial | signal vote structure correct but enum conversion unspecified (ISSUE-3) |
| R7  | Partial | phase advance correct but PhaseId validation unspecified (ISSUE-5) |
| R8  | Yes | epoch start is straightforward |
| R9  | Partial | SliceWorkflow modes well-designed but SliceResult.output missing (ISSUE-1) |
| R10 | Yes | SqliteAuditTrail design is sound |
| R11 | Partial | DualWriteAuditTrail adds no behavior (ISSUE-4) |
| R12 | Yes | YAML config file format and loading well-specified |
| R13 | Yes | hooks.json and session-register.sh reasonable |
| R14 | Yes | Nix packaging updates correct (adds temporalio, pyyaml, aiosqlite) |
| R15 | Yes | aurad config adoption shares same YAML file |

## Temporal SDK Usage

The Temporal patterns are generally correct:
- SA upserts in workflow code (deterministic boundary respected)
- Activities for I/O operations
- Signal-driven lifecycle for SliceWorkflow
- `workflow.wait_condition()` for signal-driven waiting with timeout

One concern: the `execute_slice_command` activity runs arbitrary shell commands. The `heartbeat_timeout` is set to `heartbeat_interval_seconds * 2`, which is correct Temporal practice. However, the activity must call `activity.heartbeat()` periodically — the proposal says "reports heartbeat periodically" but doesn't show the implementation. If the activity blocks on subprocess I/O without heartbeating, Temporal will cancel it after `heartbeat_timeout`. This should be made explicit.

## End-User Alignment

1. **End users:** Developers using Claude Code with Aura Protocol, and operators managing Temporal workers.
2. **What they want:** A CLI (`aura-msg`) that is intuitive, produces machine-parseable output for hooks, and human-readable output for manual use.
3. **Impact:** The nested subcommand design is good UX. The `--format` flag is well-thought-out. Exit codes are structured for programmatic use.
4. **Gaps:** ISSUE-1 through ISSUE-5 above.
5. **MVP scope:** Reasonable — 7 slices with clear dependencies.
6. **Validation checklist:** Mostly complete but needs fixes per ISSUE-1, ISSUE-2.

## Summary

The proposal demonstrates solid engineering thinking — the decision log is well-reasoned, the slice decomposition makes sense, and the Temporal SDK patterns are largely correct. The MUST-FIX issues (1-3) are type-level correctness problems that would cause runtime errors if implemented as-written. Issues 4-5 are design clarity problems. A revised PROPOSAL-2 addressing these 5 items would receive an ACCEPT vote.

# FOLLOWUP_PROPOSAL-2: Protocol Engine v2 Follow-up (Revised)

## Revision Summary (PROPOSAL-1 → PROPOSAL-2)

Three targeted corrections from reviewer consensus. All other content is unchanged.

| Correction | Axis | Finding | Change |
|-----------|------|---------|--------|
| 1 | A (Correctness) | AC-A1-1 | Added explicit `skill_header.j2` filename constraint; test must provide BOTH temp template dir AND valid SKILL.md with markers |
| 2 | B (Test quality) | B3 / AC-B3-2 | Worker round-trip only preserves 3 fields (id, order, instruction); command/context/next_state are None. Revised AC-B3-2 to assert exactly those 3 fields and confirm the 3 remaining are None. |
| 3 | C (Elegance) | D2 severity | Reclassified D2 from MINOR to IMPORTANT — silent coercion of missing required element is a correctness asymmetry, not a style issue. |

---

## Problem Space

**Axes:** correctness (silent regression risk), test coverage completeness, code naming clarity
**Has-a / Is-a:**
- Group A: Tests *have-a* test isolation problem — the StrictUndefined test creates its own `jinja2.Environment` instead of exercising `gen_skills.py`'s Environment; EPOCH constraints *have-a* coverage gap in `test_context_injection.py`
- Group B: `ProcedureStep` *has-a* 6-field dataclass (id, order, instruction, command, context, next_state) — existing round-trip and sync tests only assert 3 of 6 fields
- Group C: `gen_schema.py` *has-a* naming inconsistency — `_ROLE_CONSTRAINTS`/`_PHASE_CONSTRAINTS` shadow `context_injection.py`'s identically-named dicts with inverted semantics
- Group D: `schema_parser.py` *has-a* silent failure mode (missing `<instruction>` → empty string instead of error); slug pin tests *are* absent; `next-state` optionality *is* undocumented

**Primary risk:** Group A findings are silent regression risks — the StrictUndefined test passes even if `gen_skills.py` removes `StrictUndefined`. Group B findings mean a regression in `ProcedureStep.command` or `.context` generation would not be caught by the round-trip cycle.

## Scope

Groups A + B + D2 are IMPORTANT (7 findings; all must be addressed before close).
Groups C + D1 + D3 are MINOR (4 findings; address in FOLLOWUP-SLICE-3 alongside others).

**Scope change vs PROPOSAL-1:** D2 moved from MINOR to IMPORTANT. It is grouped into SLICE-3 for implementation locality (schema_parser.py + test_schema_parser.py), but is now a blocker for epic close.

---

## Engineering Tradeoffs

| Option | Pros | Cons | Decision |
|--------|------|------|----------|
| Group B: test private `_template_env` or internal rendering paths | Easy to write | Violates public-interface constraint; brittle to refactor | REJECTED |
| Group B: call `generate_skill()` / `parse_schema()` / compare `PROCEDURE_STEPS` directly | Durable, public-interface-only | Slightly more setup | CHOSEN |
| A1: Create a separate bad-template fixture | Precise isolation | Adds more fixture boilerplate | Acceptable; use minimal fixture |
| A1: Call `generate_skill()` with a template that references undefined var | Tests gen_skills.py's actual Environment | Requires correct template filename matching gen_skills.py's loader | CHOSEN |
| C1: Rename using `replace_all` with automated grep check | Safe, complete | Must update all import sites | CHOSEN (all import sites: test_gen_schema.py) |
| D2: Raise `SchemaParseError` vs. return empty string | Consistent with `_require()` pattern; catches malformed XML | Technically a behavior change | CHOSEN (consistent with existing error handling) |

---

## Slice Decomposition

Slices are vertical: each slice owns its files end-to-end with no shared file ownership.

### FOLLOWUP-SLICE-1: Test Isolation Fixes (A2)

**Files owned:**
- `tests/test_context_injection.py` — A2: add parametrized EPOCH Ride the Wave inclusion tests

**What changes:**
- A2: Add a new class `TestEpochRideTheWaveConstraints` in `test_context_injection.py` with 4 parametrized tests, one per Ride the Wave constraint (C-supervisor-cartographers, C-integration-points, C-slice-review-before-close, C-max-review-cycles), asserting each is in `get_role_context(RoleId.EPOCH).constraints` by id.

**No source code changes** — tests only.

---

### FOLLOWUP-SLICE-2: ProcedureStep Test Coverage (A1, B1, B2, B3, B4)

**Files owned:**
- `tests/test_gen_skills.py` — A1 (replace StrictUndefined test), B2 (new rendering test)
- `tests/test_schema_types_sync.py` — B1, B4
- `tests/test_gen_schema.py` — B3 (extend round-trip tests)

**What changes:**

**A1 — test_gen_skills.py:**
Remove the existing `TestStrictUndefined.test_undefined_variable_raises_undefined_error` test that creates its own `jinja2.Environment`. Replace with a test that calls `generate_skill()` with a Jinja2 template that references an undefined variable, and asserts `jinja2.UndefinedError` is raised.

Template fixture requirements (BOTH required):
1. A temp template directory containing a file named `skill_header.j2` with content `{{ undefined_var }}`. The filename MUST be `skill_header.j2` — this is the exact filename loaded by `_render_header()` via `env.get_template('skill_header.j2')`. Any other filename causes `TemplateNotFound` instead of `UndefinedError`, which does not test gen_skills.py's Environment.
2. A valid SKILL.md file at `skill_path` with properly placed BEGIN/END markers. The markers are required before `_render_header()` is called. Without them, a `MarkerError` is raised before the template rendering path is reached, and the test fails to exercise the Environment at all.

**B1 — test_schema_types_sync.py:**
Add assertions for `command` and `context` field values in `TestProcedureStepsMatchSchema`. Extend `test_supervisor_steps_from_schema` or add `test_supervisor_step_command_and_context_values`:
- Assert that `PROCEDURE_STEPS[RoleId.SUPERVISOR]` steps with non-None `.command` have specific expected string values (assert against known literal values from `types.py` via `PROCEDURE_STEPS` — public API, not internal dict).
- Assert that steps with non-None `.context` have specific expected string values.
- Use the public `PROCEDURE_STEPS` dict directly (it is exported from `aura_protocol`).

**B2 — test_gen_skills.py:**
Add `TestProcedureStepCommandContextRendering` class. Call `generate_skill(RoleId.SUPERVISOR, ...)` and assert:
- The rendered output contains at least one backtick-wrapped command (`` (`...`) ``) in a step line (template renders command as `` (`{{ step.command }}`) ``).
- The rendered output contains at least one em-dash italic context (` — _..._`) in a step line.
These assertions are against the rendered string returned by `generate_skill()` — observable output, not template internals.

**B3 — test_gen_schema.py:**
Extend round-trip tests for supervisor and worker steps to assert all fields via the `parsed_spec` fixture (parse_schema → ProcedureStep). The `parsed_spec` fixture already calls `parse_schema()` on gen_schema output — this is a public-interface round-trip test.

For supervisor steps: assert all 6 fields (id, order, instruction, command, context, next_state) survive the Python→XML→parse round-trip.

For worker steps: the schema_parser synthesizes worker step ids from a hardcoded list and only preserves 3 fields. The fields command/context/next_state are NOT stored in `<tdd-layers>` XML — they are all None after round-trip. Assert only the 3 fields that survive the round-trip (id, order, instruction), and explicitly assert that command, context, and next_state are each None after parsing.

**B4 — test_schema_types_sync.py:**
Fix `test_supervisor_steps_from_schema` docstring: extend the test body to assert `step.id == xml_step.get("id")` for each step, making the docstring truthful by matching assertion to description.

**No source code changes** — tests only.

---

### FOLLOWUP-SLICE-3: Code Clarity + Parser Correctness + Documentation (C1, C2, D1, D2, D3)

**Files owned:**
- `scripts/aura_protocol/gen_schema.py` — C1 (rename dict variables), C2 (move lazy import), D3 (doc comment for next-state optionality)
- `scripts/aura_protocol/schema_parser.py` — D2 (raise SchemaParseError on missing `<instruction>`), D3 (doc comment)
- `tests/test_gen_schema.py` — C1 (update import of renamed dicts), D1 (add slug pin tests)
- `tests/test_schema_parser.py` — D2 (add test for missing `<instruction>` raises SchemaParseError)

**What changes:**

**C1 — gen_schema.py:**
Rename module-level dicts:
- `_ROLE_CONSTRAINTS` → `_CONSTRAINT_TO_ROLE_REF`
- `_PHASE_CONSTRAINTS` → `_CONSTRAINT_TO_PHASE_REF`

Update all references within `gen_schema.py` (internal usage) and update `test_gen_schema.py` which imports these names.

**C2 — gen_schema.py:**
Identify the lazy import inside `_constraints_for_role()` (if present after earlier refactors). Move any such import to module-level. Confirm no circular import risk by verifying the import chain: `gen_schema` → `context_injection` (already imported at top). If the lazy import was already removed in a prior refactor, document this finding and mark C2 as a no-op.

**D1 — test_gen_schema.py:**
Add `TestSlugPinLiterals` class with tests that pin specific slug literals:
- `assert any(s.id == "S-supervisor-call-skill" for s in PROCEDURE_STEPS[RoleId.SUPERVISOR])` — verifies the first supervisor step id has not been silently renamed
- `assert any(s.id == "S-supervisor-cartographers" for s in PROCEDURE_STEPS[RoleId.SUPERVISOR])` — verifies Ride the Wave step id is pinned
- `assert any(s.id == "S-worker-types" for s in PROCEDURE_STEPS[RoleId.WORKER])` — verifies worker step ids

These are pinned literal comparisons, not just Python-vs-Python cross-checks.

**D2 — schema_parser.py (IMPORTANT, blocker for epic close):**
In `_parse_procedure_steps()`, change the silent empty-string fallback on missing `<instruction>` to raise `SchemaParseError`:

```python
# Before (silent):
instruction = instr_el.text.strip() if instr_el is not None and instr_el.text else ""

# After (raises):
if instr_el is None or not instr_el.text:
    raise SchemaParseError(
        f"Missing required <instruction> child element on <step id='{step_id}'> "
        f"in startup-sequence in {path}. "
        f"Instruction is required for all procedure steps. "
        f"Fix: add <instruction>...</instruction> as a child element of the <step>."
    )
instruction = instr_el.text.strip()
```

Rationale: `instruction` is a required element. Silent coercion to empty string is inconsistent with how `id` (also required) is handled via `_require()`. This is a correctness asymmetry — both required elements must error on absence.

Add test in `test_schema_parser.py` for isolation: construct a minimal XML with a `<step>` missing `<instruction>`, call `parse_schema()`, assert `SchemaParseError` is raised.

**D3 — gen_schema.py + schema_parser.py:**
Add inline comments at the point where `next-state` is handled clarifying its optionality.

---

## File Ownership Summary (No Overlaps)

| File | Slice | Findings |
|------|-------|----------|
| `tests/test_context_injection.py` | SLICE-1 | A2 |
| `tests/test_gen_skills.py` | SLICE-2 | A1, B2 |
| `tests/test_schema_types_sync.py` | SLICE-2 | B1, B4 |
| `tests/test_gen_schema.py` | SLICE-2 (B3), SLICE-3 (C1-import, D1) | B3, C1, D1 |
| `scripts/aura_protocol/gen_schema.py` | SLICE-3 | C1, C2, D3 |
| `scripts/aura_protocol/schema_parser.py` | SLICE-3 | D2, D3 |
| `tests/test_schema_parser.py` | SLICE-3 | D2 |

**Overlap resolution for test_gen_schema.py:** SLICE-2 adds round-trip assertions (B3) and SLICE-3 adds slug pins (D1) + updates renamed imports (C1). These are additive and non-conflicting. SLICE-3 depends on SLICE-2 (for import stability after B3 additions). SLICE-2 must complete before SLICE-3 begins work on `test_gen_schema.py`.

---

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD GWT+S Format)

### FOLLOWUP-SLICE-1

**AC-A2-1:**
**Given** `test_context_injection.py` and the 4 Ride the Wave constraints in `EPOCH`'s role-scoped mapping,
**When** `get_role_context(RoleId.EPOCH)` is called,
**Then** each of `C-supervisor-cartographers`, `C-integration-points`, `C-slice-review-before-close`, `C-max-review-cycles` appears in the returned `RoleContext.constraints` (checked by `.id`),
**Should not** rely on indirect XML assertions or count comparisons alone — each constraint must be individually asserted by id.

### FOLLOWUP-SLICE-2

**AC-A1-1:**
**Given** (a) a temp template directory containing a file named `skill_header.j2` with content `{{ undefined_var }}`, AND (b) a valid SKILL.md at `skill_path` with properly placed BEGIN/END markers,
**When** `generate_skill(RoleId.SUPERVISOR, skill_path, template_dir=<temp dir>)` is called,
**Then** `jinja2.UndefinedError` is raised,
**Should not** (1) create a `jinja2.Environment` directly in the test body — must go through `generate_skill()`; (2) use any filename other than `skill_header.j2` for the template — any other name causes `TemplateNotFound`, not `UndefinedError`, and does not test gen_skills.py's Environment; (3) omit the BEGIN/END markers from SKILL.md — without them `MarkerError` is raised before template rendering is reached.

**AC-B1-1:**
**Given** `PROCEDURE_STEPS[RoleId.SUPERVISOR]` contains steps with non-None `.command` and `.context`,
**When** `PROCEDURE_STEPS` values are inspected directly (public exported dict),
**Then** at least one step has `.command == "Skill(/aura:supervisor)"` (step 1) and at least one step has a non-None `.context` string,
**Should not** inspect internal `types.py` private variables or go through XML.

**AC-B2-1:**
**Given** a valid SKILL.md with markers,
**When** `generate_skill(RoleId.SUPERVISOR, skill_path, template_dir=TEMPLATE_DIR, diff=False, write=False)` is called,
**Then** the returned string contains at least one substring matching the backtick pattern `` (`...`) `` and at least one substring matching the em-dash italic pattern ` — _..._`,
**Should not** inspect template source code or Jinja2 AST.

**AC-B3-1 (supervisor — 6-field round-trip):**
**Given** the `parsed_spec` fixture (round-trip: `types.py → gen_schema() → parse_schema()`),
**When** `parsed_spec.procedure_steps[RoleId.SUPERVISOR]` is compared against `PROCEDURE_STEPS[RoleId.SUPERVISOR]`,
**Then** for each step, all 6 fields (id, order, instruction, command, context, next_state) match field-by-field,
**Should not** skip any field — command/context/next_state must be explicitly asserted.

**AC-B3-2 (worker — 3-field round-trip, 3 fields None):**
**Given** the `parsed_spec` fixture (round-trip: `types.py → gen_schema() → parse_schema()`),
**When** `parsed_spec.procedure_steps[RoleId.WORKER]` is compared against `PROCEDURE_STEPS[RoleId.WORKER]`,
**Then** (a) for each step, the 3 fields that survive the round-trip (id, order, instruction) match field-by-field; AND (b) for each step, command, context, and next_state are each explicitly asserted to be None — worker steps are parsed from `<tdd-layers>` (Phase 9 section), not from `<startup-sequence>`, and the schema_parser does not store command/context/next_state there,
**Should not** assert command/context/next_state equal the Python-side values — they are intentionally discarded by the parser for worker steps.

**AC-B4-1:**
**Given** `test_supervisor_steps_from_schema` in `TestProcedureStepsMatchSchema`,
**When** the test runs,
**Then** the test body asserts `step.id == xml_step.get("id")` for each supervisor step AND the docstring accurately describes what is tested,
**Should not** have docstring claims that are not matched by assertions in the test body.

### FOLLOWUP-SLICE-3

**AC-C1-1:**
**Given** `gen_schema.py` defines module-level constraint-to-role and constraint-to-phase mapping dicts,
**When** the module is imported,
**Then** the dicts are named `_CONSTRAINT_TO_ROLE_REF` and `_CONSTRAINT_TO_PHASE_REF` (not `_ROLE_CONSTRAINTS`/`_PHASE_CONSTRAINTS`), and all usages within `gen_schema.py` and all imports in test files use the new names,
**Should not** leave any reference to the old names anywhere in the codebase.

**AC-C2-1:**
**Given** `gen_schema.py`'s `_constraints_for_role()` function (if it still exists),
**When** the module is loaded,
**Then** any import that was previously lazy (inside function body) is moved to module-level,
**Should not** introduce circular imports — verify by running `python -c "import aura_protocol.gen_schema"` with 0 errors.

**AC-D1-1:**
**Given** `PROCEDURE_STEPS` in `types.py`,
**When** slug pin tests run in `TestSlugPinLiterals`,
**Then** `"S-supervisor-call-skill"` is found as an `.id` in supervisor steps, `"S-supervisor-cartographers"` is found, and `"S-worker-types"` is found in worker steps — all as literal string comparisons,
**Should not** assert only that `step.id == py_step.id` (Python-vs-Python) — must compare against hard-coded literal strings.

**AC-D2-1 (IMPORTANT — blocker for epic close):**
**Given** a `<step>` element in schema.xml that has `id` and `order` attributes but no `<instruction>` child element,
**When** `parse_schema()` processes this element,
**Then** `SchemaParseError` is raised with a message identifying the step id and explaining how to fix the problem,
**Should not** return an empty string for `instruction` silently — `instruction` is a required element with the same status as `id`; both must be enforced via `_require()`-equivalent logic.

**AC-D3-1:**
**Given** the `next-state` attribute handling in `gen_schema.py` and `schema_parser.py`,
**When** a developer reads the relevant code,
**Then** an inline comment clarifies that `next-state` is only present/emitted when `next_state is not None`,
**Should not** require reading the full function to understand optionality.

---

## Validation Checklist

### FOLLOWUP-SLICE-1
- [ ] `test_context_injection.py` has new `TestEpochRideTheWaveConstraints` class
- [ ] 4 parametrized (or 4 individual) tests, one per Ride the Wave constraint id
- [ ] Each test calls `get_role_context(RoleId.EPOCH)` and checks `.constraints` by id
- [ ] `get_role_context` is a public function (imported from `aura_protocol.context_injection`)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (`pytest tests/test_context_injection.py`)

### FOLLOWUP-SLICE-2
- [ ] `TestStrictUndefined.test_undefined_variable_raises_undefined_error` replaced with test calling `generate_skill()`
- [ ] New test provides BOTH: (a) temp template dir with `skill_header.j2` containing `{{ undefined_var }}`; (b) valid SKILL.md at `skill_path` with BEGIN/END markers
- [ ] Template file is named exactly `skill_header.j2` (not any other name)
- [ ] Test asserts `jinja2.UndefinedError` is raised (not `jinja2.Environment` instantiated in test)
- [ ] `test_schema_types_sync.py` asserts `.command` value for at least one supervisor step
- [ ] `test_schema_types_sync.py` asserts `.context` value for at least one supervisor step with context
- [ ] `test_gen_skills.py` has new test asserting backtick-wrapped command in rendered output
- [ ] `test_gen_skills.py` has new test asserting em-dash italic context in rendered output
- [ ] `test_gen_schema.py` round-trip asserts all 6 ProcedureStep fields for supervisor steps
- [ ] `test_gen_schema.py` round-trip asserts id, order, instruction match for worker steps
- [ ] `test_gen_schema.py` round-trip explicitly asserts command=None, context=None, next_state=None for worker steps
- [ ] `test_supervisor_steps_from_schema` body asserts `.id == xml_step.get("id")` for each step
- [ ] All 911 existing tests still pass
- [ ] No test inspects `jinja2.Environment`, private template variables, or internal dicts

### FOLLOWUP-SLICE-3
- [ ] `gen_schema.py` uses `_CONSTRAINT_TO_ROLE_REF` and `_CONSTRAINT_TO_PHASE_REF` (not old names)
- [ ] `test_gen_schema.py` imports updated to use new names
- [ ] No remaining references to `_ROLE_CONSTRAINTS` or `_PHASE_CONSTRAINTS` in `gen_schema.py`
- [ ] Module-level import verified: `python -c "import aura_protocol.gen_schema"` succeeds
- [ ] `TestSlugPinLiterals` class added to `test_gen_schema.py` with literal string pin assertions

- [ ] All 911 existing tests still pass
- [ ] `gen_schema --diff` shows no changes after running (schema.xml still valid)

---

## Dependency Chain

```
FOLLOWUP-SLICE-3
    └── blocked-by FOLLOWUP-SLICE-2   (test_gen_schema.py round-trip additions must land first)
FOLLOWUP-SLICE-2
    └── (no blocking dependency on SLICE-1; can run in parallel)
FOLLOWUP-SLICE-1
    └── (no blocking dependencies)
```

SLICE-1 and SLICE-2 can run in parallel (no shared files).
SLICE-3 should start after SLICE-2 completes, to avoid rebasing conflicts on `test_gen_schema.py`.

---

## Key Risk: A1 Template Fixture Design

Finding A1 requires calling `generate_skill()` with a bad template that references an undefined variable. The template file MUST be named `skill_header.j2` — this is the exact filename loaded by `_render_header()` via `env.get_template('skill_header.j2')`. Any other filename causes `TemplateNotFound` instead of `UndefinedError`. Additionally, the SKILL.md at `skill_path` must have valid BEGIN/END markers, as `MarkerError` is raised before template rendering is reached if markers are absent. The worker must supply both preconditions or the test will fail to exercise gen_skills.py's Environment.
title: FOLLOWUP_PROPOSAL-2: Protocol Engine v2 follow-up — test coverage + code clarity (rev)

description:
**File:** scripts/aura_protocol/workflow.py, line ~487 (inside EpochWorkflow._run_p9_slices)

**Problem:**
The implementation uses `asyncio.wait(handles, return_when=asyncio.FIRST_EXCEPTION)` inside EpochWorkflow, which is Temporal workflow code. The Temporal Python SDK explicitly provides `workflow.wait()` as the *deterministic* replacement for `asyncio.wait()` in workflow code. The SDK docstring for `workflow.wait` states:

  'This is a deterministic version of asyncio.wait. This function should be used instead of that one in workflows.'

Using `asyncio.wait()` inside a Temporal workflow risks non-determinism on replay, which can cause workflow history corruption.

**Evidence:**
- `temporalio.workflow.wait` exists and is the approved replacement (confirmed from temporalio source).
- The task description (aura-plugins-cz2t) specifies: 'workflow.wait(handles, return_when=FIRST_EXCEPTION)'.
- The code comment says 'asyncio.wait is the approved pattern' — this is incorrect.
- The code already imports `from temporalio import activity, workflow`, so `workflow.wait` is accessible as-is.

**Fix:**
Replace:
  done, pending = await asyncio.wait(handles, return_when=asyncio.FIRST_EXCEPTION)
With:
  done, pending = await workflow.wait(handles, return_when=asyncio.FIRST_EXCEPTION)

Update the comment to correct the claim. The rest of the _run_p9_slices logic (cancellation, result collection) is correct.
title: BLOCKER: asyncio.wait() used inside EpochWorkflow._run_p9_slices() — must be workflow.wait()

description:
File: scripts/aura_protocol/workflow.py, lines 418 vs 594

EpochWorkflow.advance_phase (line 407):    @workflow.signal  def advance_phase(...)
EpochWorkflow.submit_vote (line 417):      @workflow.signal  def submit_vote(...)
ReviewPhaseWorkflow.submit_vote (line 594): @workflow.signal  async def submit_vote(...)

Both signal body types do synchronous work (dict write / list append).
Neither awaits anything. The async def on ReviewPhaseWorkflow.submit_vote is
unnecessary and creates an inconsistency that may cause future maintainers to
wonder whether there is a meaningful difference.

While Temporal supports both sync and async signal handlers, the codebase
should be consistent. All three signal handlers here are functionally sync;
ReviewPhaseWorkflow.submit_vote should be def (not async def) to match the
pattern established by EpochWorkflow.

Slice: aura-plugins-cz2t
title: MINOR: ReviewPhaseWorkflow.submit_vote uses async def while EpochWorkflow signals use def — inconsistent Temporal signal handler style

description:
In test_gen_types.py::TestGenTypesDataIntegrity::test_frozen_dataclass_decorator_present (line 165), the test only checks that 'frozen=True' appears anywhere in the generated source. This passes even if only one class uses @dataclass(frozen=True) while others use @dataclass. The proposal requires ALL new spec types to be frozen dataclasses. The assertion does not verify the count or that every class definition has the decorator.

Remediation: Use ast.parse() to count @dataclass(frozen=True) decorators and verify it matches the expected number of dataclass definitions, or at minimum check that the count of 'frozen=True' occurrences equals the number of expected dataclass names defined. Low risk since production code is verified; this is a test quality concern only.
title: S1-B1-MINOR: test_gen_types frozen=True check is substring-only, not per-class

description:
File: pasture/internal/hooks/hooks.go lines 219-228

dispatchErrors collects all handler errors in errs []error and implements Unwrap() []error correctly, but the Error() string method discards the individual messages in the multi-error case:
  msgs := make([]string, len(e.errs))
  for i, err := range e.errs { msgs[i] = err.Error() }
  // msgs is never used!
  return fmt.Sprintf("hooks.Manager: %d handlers returned errors", len(e.errs))

The msgs slice is built but thrown away. The caller who calls err.Error() on a Dispatch failure gets only a count, not the reasons. Only callers who use errors.As/Is to unwrap will see the individual errors.

For an end-user looking at pastured logs, 'hooks.Manager: 3 handlers returned errors' with no detail is not actionable.

Fix: Include at least the first error message in the summary string, e.g.:
  'hooks.Manager: 3 handlers returned errors: <first-error>; ...'
or join all messages separated by '; '.
title: IMPORTANT: dispatchErrors.Error() discards individual handler error details

description:
---
references:
  request: aura-plugins-w115
