## Questions and Responses

### Round 1

#### Generation Target
Q: For v2 schema.xml runtime injection, what is the generation target? Should we generate SKILL.md files at build time (committed to git), or inject schema context into agent prompts dynamically at skill invocation time?
Options: Build-time generation (Parse schema.xml → Jinja2 templates → generate SKILL.md files committed to git), Runtime injection (Parse schema.xml at skill invocation time, dynamically construct prompt section), Hybrid: generate + runtime override (Generate SKILL.md at build time as baseline, allow runtime schema queries for dynamic context)
A: Hybrid: generate + runtime override. Build-time generation for SKILL.md + types.py (git-tracked), plus runtime constraint injection and phase-aware context queries.

#### SKILL.md Structure
Q: How should the 38 SKILL.md files be structured after v2? Currently each has unique procedural guidance + duplicated schema content. What stays manual vs generated?
Options: Fully generated from templates (100% generated from schema.xml + Jinja2), Split: generated header + manual body (Schema context generated as header, procedural guidance stays hand-written below marker), Schema inlined via includes (SKILL.md stays hand-written but uses include directives)
A: Split: generated header + manual body. Schema context (phases, constraints, labels) generated as header section with markers. Procedural guidance stays as hand-written markdown below.

#### Types Generation
Q: Should types.py also be generated from schema.xml, or stay manually maintained with sync tests?
Options: Generate types.py from schema.xml (codegen script parses schema.xml → generates types.py), Keep manual + sync tests (types.py stays hand-written, validated by integration tests)
A: Generate types.py from schema.xml. But with critical refinement: "The Python code should become the source of truth: XML as portable schema."

#### MVP Scope
Q: What is the MVP scope for v2?
Options: Schema parser → Python specs, Types.py generation, SKILL.md generation, Constraint injection at runtime
A: ALL FOUR selected. Additional note: "The Python code should become the source of truth: XML as portable schema."

### Round 2

#### Source of Truth Direction
Q: You said 'Python code should become the source of truth: XML as portable schema.' This inverts the original URD R2 direction. Confirm: after bootstrap from schema.xml, the flow becomes Python → schema.xml (export) and Python → SKILL.md (generation)?
Options: Yes — Python is SoT/XML is export (Bootstrap: parse schema.xml → types.py; Steady state: types.py → schema.xml + SKILL.md), No — XML stays SoT/Python is generated, Bidirectional — either can change/sync enforced
A: Yes — Python is SoT, XML is export. Bootstrap once from schema.xml, then Python types.py is authoritative. schema.xml is regenerated from Python. SKILL.md generated from Python specs.

#### Constraint Injection Point
Q: When should constraints be injected into the agent's context?
Options: At skill invocation only, At phase transition, Both: skill + phase
A: Both: skill + phase. Role-level constraints at skill invocation (baseline), phase-specific constraints at each transition (contextual enrichment).

#### Procedural Guidance
Q: What about the 'procedural guidance' content unique to each SKILL.md? Stay hand-written or move?
Options: Stay hand-written forever, Move to schema.xml over time, Move procedures to Python
A: Move procedures to Python. Procedures stored as Python constants/dataclasses, then generated into BOTH schema.xml AND SKILL.md. Note: "Should move procedures to Python, which generates and places them in the schema.xml."
title: ELICIT: Schema.xml runtime context injection (v2) — Python as source of truth

description:
VOTE: ACCEPT - All four REVISE findings from round 1 (aura-plugins-oitf) are fully addressed.

Finding 1 (BLOCKER - SourceFileLoader): RESOLVED. PROPOSAL-2 now documents the correct two-step pattern verbatim: SourceFileLoader -> spec_from_loader -> module_from_spec -> exec_module. The broken spec_from_file_location reference is gone.

Finding 2 (BLOCKER - pythonpath): RESOLVED. Proposal explicitly states no pythonpath change needed and documents BIN_PATH = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / 'bin' as the path constant. The substantive guidance is correct and actionable.

Finding 3 (REVISE - ordering prerequisite): RESOLVED. An explicit 'Ordering note (hard prerequisite)' section states Change 2 MUST follow Change 1, explains the SystemExit(2) mechanism clearly, and the validation checklist lists deletion first.

Finding 4 (REVISE - Cases C and D): RESOLVED. Case C (root + subdir both present, root first) guards against future short-circuit regression. Case D (subdir pyproject with no [project] version key returns empty list) guards against _has_pyproject_version filter removal. Both verified against actual source: _PYPROJECT_VERSION_RE requires [project] header + version = "X.Y.Z" pattern; [tool.foo] content correctly yields False. No git repo needed for any case - discover_version_files uses only pathlib.

Test quality axis: All cases use tmp_path (no git repo setup, fast), assert observable outputs (return value list, length, path attributes), SUT is not mocked (module imported directly via SourceFileLoader), shared _import_aura_release() helper avoids duplication. Cases match the repo's existing class-based test organization pattern.
title: PROPOSAL-2-REVIEW-B-2: aura-release dead code + test

description:
VOTE: REVISE - Four blockers found on the test quality axis.

BLOCKER-1: Module-scope fixture contradicts function-scope autouse cleanup.
The proposed aura_release_mod fixture (scope='module') holds the module for the entire test module. The companion autouse function-scope fixture removes sys.modules['aura_release'] after each test. After the first test, the module-scoped fixture still holds a reference to mod, but mod is no longer registered in sys.modules. Tests 2-5 will fail with AttributeError when @dataclass field resolution calls sys.modules[cls.__module__].__dict__ (this hazard is documented in the existing test file at lines 29-33). Fix: either drop the per-function autouse teardown (let the module-scope fixture's own yield/pop handle cleanup), or use function scope for both the import fixture and cleanup.

BLOCKER-2: No test case specified for MarketplaceVersionFile.write() correctness.
R1 is the highest-priority correctness fix (plugins[*].version must NOT be updated). SLICE-2 implements the fix, but no test is designed or described anywhere in the proposal that verifies: given a marketplace.json with plugins[].version, when write() is called with a new version, then plugins[*].version is unchanged. The checklist line 'New tests added for MarketplaceVersionFile.write() and registry CLI' is present but entirely unspecified. Without this test, SLICE-2 can be silently reverted.

BLOCKER-3: No test cases specified for any registry CLI command.
SLICES 3 and 4 introduce a new subsystem (init/add/list/remove) with load/save/find_plugin logic and warn-vs-error validation semantics. The proposal describes implementation in detail but specifies zero test cases for: registry init (creates file, errors if exists), registry add (adds entry, idempotency), registry list (output format), registry remove (removes correct entries), PluginRegistry.load() (missing file -> None, malformed JSON -> exit(2), nonexistent marketplace path -> warn+continue), find_plugin() (by name vs by cwd). A plan that introduces a new subsystem without test specifications is incomplete.

BLOCKER-4: --dry-run for registry writes untested.
The BDD criterion 'Given --dry-run when registry write would occur then print [dry-run] Would update marketplace' is listed but no test case is designed for it. Two surfaces need coverage: MarketplaceVersionFile.write(dry_run=True) skips the write, and the SLICE-5 release integration path skips the registry-triggered marketplace bump under --dry-run.

Actionable suggestions:
1. Resolve fixture scope conflict: use module scope for import + teardown only in the module fixture's finally block; remove the autouse function-scope cleanup, OR use function scope for everything.
2. Specify a concrete test for MarketplaceVersionFile.write() that asserts plugins[*].version is unchanged after write().
3. Specify test cases for each registry CLI subcommand and PluginRegistry.load() validation branches.
4. Specify a --dry-run test for the marketplace bump path (assert file not written, assert print output).
title: PROPOSAL-1-REVIEW-B-1: Plugin registry + marketplace fix + test quality

description:
refs: aura-plugins-nwnhv

File: pasture/internal/temporal/workflow.go
Lines: 193-204 (error path after sm.Advance fails)

Issue:
When sm.Advance returns an error, EpochWorkflow.Run directly modifies state machine internals by calling w.sm.State().TransitionHistory = append(...) and w.sm.State().LastError = &errMsg. This bypasses the state machine public API and breaks the encapsulation contract: State() is documented as returning a pointer callers must not modify directly.

This is an API contract violation. The EpochStateMachine should expose a RecordFailedTransition or SetLastError method instead, consistent with the existing RecordVote/RecordBlocker/Advance pattern.

Suggested fix:
Add a method to EpochStateMachine (e.g. RecordFailedTransition(sig PhaseAdvanceSignal, err error, now time.Time)) that performs these mutations internally. Call it from EpochWorkflow.Run instead of mutating State() directly.
title: FINDING: EpochWorkflow.Run directly mutates state machine internals on Advance failure

description:
File: pasture/internal/acp/client.go lines 87-91

ContentBlock defines both a Content string field and a Text string field, both intended to hold text for 'text' and 'thinking' blocks. The comment explains the split: 'Adapters should populate Content; the live client uses Text from wire format.'

This is confusing for adapter implementors and indexer code. The SharedIndexer already has to handle both (indexer.go lines 93-96: raw = cb.Content; if raw == '' { raw = cb.Text }).

Fix: Consolidate to a single Text field (matching the ACP wire format). Adapter implementations should map their native text field to Text. This removes the ambiguity and aligns with the ACP content block model.
title: MINOR: ContentBlock has ambiguous dual text fields (Content vs Text)

description:
Axis-C finding (Elegance/API Coherence).

gen_skills.py _constraints_for_role() (lines 93-99) returns all 23 constraints for every role, with a comment:
  'Currently returns all constraints (they apply globally). Future versions may filter by role tag once schema supports it.'

However, context_injection.py (SLICE-4) already implements per-role constraint filtering with _ROLE_CONSTRAINTS. The SKILL.md generator renders all 23 constraints into every role's header — a worker agent's SKILL.md will show supervisor-only constraints like C-supervisor-no-impl and C-vertical-slices, and a reviewer's SKILL.md will show worker-specific C-worker-gates.

This undermines the core value proposition of the SKILL.md generator: providing role-appropriate context. An agent reading their SKILL.md should see only the constraints relevant to them, not the full global list.

More importantly, context_injection.py already has the correct per-role filtering. The _constraints_for_role() function in gen_skills.py should delegate to or reuse the same _ROLE_CONSTRAINTS mapping rather than ignoring it.

Fix: In gen_skills.py, change _constraints_for_role() to use _ROLE_CONSTRAINTS from context_injection.py (or a shared location). Return only the constraints relevant to the given role, consistent with what get_role_context() returns at runtime.
title: SLICE-3-REVIEW-C-1-F9: gen_skills.py _constraints_for_role returns ALL constraints for every role — not filtered

description:
VOTE: ACCEPT - All four correctness questions verified cleanly.

1. DEAD CODE CONFIRMATION (lines 33-57): Confirmed true dead code. Grep across the entire file shows _discover_repo_root, REPO_ROOT, VERSION_FILES, and CHANGELOG_PATH appear ONLY within lines 33-56. No reference to any of these four symbols exists anywhere in lines 58-637. The replacement machinery (repo_root() at line 83, discover_version_files() at line 227, changelog_path = repo_root() / 'CHANGELOG.md' at line 395, changelog_path = root / 'CHANGELOG.md' at line 530) is fully self-contained and independent.

2. IMPORTLIB SAFETY: Correct. After removing lines 33-57, the only module-level executable statements are pure data literals (COMMIT_SECTIONS dict, DEFAULT_SECTION string, _PYPROJECT_VERSION_RE compiled regex, _SKIP_DIRS set), plus def/class/decorator statements that do not call repo_root(). The repo_root() function at line 83 is decorated with @functools.lru_cache and is a function definition only — it is NOT invoked at module level. importlib.util.spec_from_file_location will import the module with zero git subprocess calls. This is correct.

3. SUBDIRECTORY HANDLING: discover_version_files correctly handles the case where root has no pyproject.toml but a subdirectory does. The logic at lines 240-251: it first checks root/pyproject.toml (skipped if absent), then iterates _subdirs(root) — sorted child directories excluding hidden and known junk dirs — and appends any subdirectory pyproject.toml that passes _has_pyproject_version(). The regex _PYPROJECT_VERSION_RE requires a [project] section with a version = 'X.Y.Z' field, so bare pyproject.toml files without a [project] version are correctly excluded. If MULTIPLE subdirectories have valid pyproject.toml files, all are returned and version-consistency is enforced downstream — this is correct monorepo behavior.

4. EDGE CASES: No missing critical edge cases for MVP scope. One minor cosmetic note that does NOT affect correctness: the comment on line 32 ('Conventional commit prefix -> Keep a Changelog section') currently appears above the dead code block rather than above COMMIT_SECTIONS. After deleting lines 33-57, this comment will immediately precede COMMIT_SECTIONS at what becomes line 33 — it will then correctly describe that dict. No action needed.

One behavioral difference between the dead _discover_repo_root (check=False, custom error + sys.exit(2)) and the live repo_root() (check=True, raises CalledProcessError) is irrelevant post-removal since all git calls go through repo_root().

The proposal is technically sound, MVP-scoped, and faithfully serves the end-user requirement of aura-release working correctly in repos where pyproject.toml lives in a subdirectory.
title: PROPOSAL-1-REVIEW-A-1: aura-release dead code + test

## Finding

GenerateSubSkill (skills.go:569) has 61.9% statement coverage. The Init=true code path — which prepends markers to a sub-skill file that lacks them, then optionally writes the prepended content before rendering — is not tested. skills_test.go only tests Init=true for GenerateSkill (TestGenerateSkill_InitMode), not for GenerateSubSkill.

The Init path in GenerateSubSkill has a nested Write branch (if opts.Init && !HasMarkers then PrependMarkers; if opts.Write then os.WriteFile). The write-failure error in that branch is also untested.

## Why It Matters

--init flag is a production workflow: when adding a new sub-skill file, the operator calls GenerateSubSkill with Init=true. If the PrependMarkers + Write branch has a bug, operators will see silent failures or corrupt files.

## How To Fix

Add TestGenerateSubSkill_InitMode to skills_test.go mirroring TestGenerateSkill_InitMode:
1. Write a file without markers (just a heading).
2. Call GenerateSubSkill with Init=true, Write=true.
3. Assert no error and that the heading prefix is preserved.
4. Assert the hand-authored content below markers is preserved.
5. Read back the file from disk and verify contents match.
title: CODEGEN-REVIEW-B-1 IMPORTANT: GenerateSubSkill Init mode untested (61.9% coverage)

## Components Reviewed

### Component 1: Registry schema + validation
**Definition shown:** PluginEntry(name, path: Path, remote), MarketplaceEntry(path: Path, plugins), PluginRegistry(marketplaces) frozen dataclasses.

**Question:** Which validation behavior when loading a malformed plugin entry?
Options: Warn+skip (current proposal) / Fail+exit 2
**User response:** "Will error if trying to add a malformed entry with informative error message, but if user edits later, will warn+skip."
→ Delta: `registry add` validates and errors; `load()` warns and skips. Proposal already matches.

**Question:** Any concerns about registry schema or JSON structure?
**User response:** "No concerns — proceed"

---

### Component 2: Registry CLI — `registry add`
**Definition shown:** `aura-release registry add <name> [--path <local>] [--remote <url>] --marketplace <mp>`

**Question:** Path storage — absolute or relative?
Options: Always absolute / Store as-given
**User response:** "Always absolute (resolve at add time)"

**Question:** Duplicate handling — error or update?
Options: Error if exists / Update silently
**User response:** "Update verbosely. State what existed before, and should require interaction to update. Have --yes flag to update non-interactively."
→ **UAT DELTA**: Show old entry + interactive confirm prompt + --yes flag to skip. This is NEW behaviour not in PROPOSAL-2.

---

### Component 3: Plugin auto-detection + --plugin flag
**Definition shown:** Auto-detect by CWD.resolve() == PluginEntry.path.resolve(); explicit --plugin <name> override. Double-bump guard.

**Question:** When no registry match, what should happen?
Options: Proceed silently (proposal) / Warn and proceed / Error if --plugin given only
**User response:** "Error and stop verbosely in all cases if both auto-detect fails and registry file exists."
→ **UAT DELTA**: If registry EXISTS but no CWD match → error verbosely. Proposal said proceed silently. This is stricter.

**Question:** Should --check show registry info?
Options: Yes — show registry plugin + marketplace / No — unchanged
**User response:** "Yes — show registry info in --check output"
→ **UAT DELTA**: Add registry info to --check output. Not in PROPOSAL-2.

---

### Component 4: Test quality (aura-plugins-klac)
**Definition shown:** module-scoped aura_release_mod fixture + autouse _clear_caches (cache_clear only, not sys.modules).

**Question:** Migrate A-D to fixture or leave as-is?
Options: Migrate all A-E (current proposal) / Leave A-D unchanged
**User response:** "Migrate all (A-E) to aura_release_mod fixture (current proposal)"

**Question:** Any concerns about test quality changes?
**User response:** "No concerns — proceed"

---

### Catch-all
**Question:** Any gap or drift from original URE?
**User response:** "Nothing else — ACCEPT the plan"

## Final Decision
**ACCEPT** — with 4 deltas noted above that must be reflected in implementation:
1. `registry add` duplicate: interactive confirm + --yes flag
2. Path always stored absolute at add time
3. Registry exists + no CWD match → error verbosely (not silent)
4. --check output includes registry info when found
title: UAT: Plan acceptance — plugin registry + marketplace fix + test quality

description:
---
references:
  request: aura-plugins-w115
  urd: aura-plugins-w07o
  original_urd: aura-plugins-umi
  original_proposal: aura-plugins-gmv
---

## Problem Space

**Axes:** code generation (schema → code, code → schema), drift elimination (38 SKILL.md files × 1 schema.xml), runtime context injection (role-level + phase-level), source-of-truth inversion (schema.xml → Python types.py)

**Has-a / Is-a relationships:**
- SchemaSpec HAS-A collection of PhaseSpec, RoleSpec, CommandSpec, ConstraintSpec, HandoffSpec, LabelSpec, ReviewAxisSpec
- RoleSpec HAS-MANY owned phases, invariants, label-awareness declarations
- CommandSpec HAS-A role-ref, phase-refs, creates-labels
- PhaseSpec HAS-MANY SubstepSpec (with startup sequences, TDD layers)
- Generator IS-A function (SchemaSpec → output format)
- ContextInjector IS-A function (RoleId|PhaseId → relevant constraints/phases/commands)

## Engineering Tradeoffs

| Decision | Options | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|---------|--------|-----------|
| Source of truth | schema.xml / Python types.py / bidirectional | Python types.py | User requirement. Python is more expressive, testable, and directly usable at runtime. XML is for portability. |
| Bootstrap strategy | Manual expansion / auto-parse schema.xml | Auto-parse + human review | 1,314-line schema.xml with 12 entity types — too much to manually transcribe. Parse once, verify, then Python is SoT. |
| Template engine | Jinja2 / string.Template / f-strings | Jinja2 | Industry standard for prompt generation. StrictUndefined catches missing vars. Supports conditionals, loops, inheritance. Minimal dependency. |
| XML generation | lxml / xml.etree / template-based | xml.etree.ElementTree | Already in stdlib, already used in validate_schema.py. No new dependency. |
| SKILL.md split strategy | Full generation / marker-based split / includes | Marker-based split | User chose split: generated header + manual body. Markers are simple, git-friendly, no preprocessor needed. |
| Procedure storage | schema.xml / Python / separate YAML | Python dataclasses | User chose Python. Procedures generate into both schema.xml AND SKILL.md. Python is more readable for procedural text. |
| Runtime injection integration | State machine / standalone module | Standalone module + state machine hook | Keep injection logic decoupled from state machine. Hook into advance() for phase-level. Standalone for role-level. |

## MVP Milestone

### Architecture Overview

```
== BOOTSTRAP (one-time) ==
schema.xml → [schema_parser.py] → SchemaSpec → verify → expanded types.py

== STEADY STATE (ongoing) ==
types.py (SOURCE OF TRUTH)
    ├─→ [gen_schema.py] → schema.xml (portable export)
    ├─→ [gen_skills.py] → SKILL.md (generated headers with markers)
    └─→ [context_injection.py] → runtime role/phase context
```

### Module 1: `scripts/aura_protocol/schema_parser.py` — Bootstrap Schema Parser

Parses schema.xml into a comprehensive SchemaSpec. Used ONCE for bootstrap, then kept for round-trip verification.

```python
@dataclass
class SchemaSpec:
    """Complete protocol specification extracted from schema.xml."""
    phases: dict[str, PhaseSpec]        # PhaseId → PhaseSpec (expanded with substeps)
    roles: dict[str, RoleSpec]          # RoleId → RoleSpec (with invariants, standing teams)
    commands: dict[str, CommandSpec]     # command-id → CommandSpec
    constraints: dict[str, ConstraintSpec]  # constraint-id → ConstraintSpec
    handoffs: dict[str, HandoffSpec]    # handoff-id → HandoffSpec
    labels: dict[str, LabelSpec]        # label-id → LabelSpec
    review_axes: dict[str, ReviewAxisSpec]  # axis-id → ReviewAxisSpec
    title_conventions: list[TitleConvention]
    dependency_model: DependencyModel

def parse_schema(path: Path) -> SchemaSpec:
    """Parse schema.xml into a complete SchemaSpec.
    
    This is the bootstrap entry point. After initial extraction,
    types.py becomes the source of truth and this parser is used
    only for round-trip verification.
    """
```

### Module 2: Expanded `scripts/aura_protocol/types.py` — Protocol Types (SoT)

Add new spec dataclasses and canonical dicts:

```python
# NEW dataclasses
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SubstepSpec:
    id: str
    type: str
    execution: str  # "sequential" | "parallel"
    order: int
    label_ref: str
    parallel_group: str | None = None
    startup_sequence: tuple[str, ...] = ()  # ordered step descriptions

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RoleSpec:
    id: RoleId
    name: str
    description: str
    owns_phases: frozenset[PhaseId]
    label_awareness: tuple[str, ...]
    invariants: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    delegates: tuple[DelegateSpec, ...] = ()

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DelegateSpec:
    to_role: RoleId
    phases: frozenset[PhaseId]
    description: str

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CommandSpec:
    id: str
    name: str
    role_ref: RoleId
    description: str
    phase_refs: frozenset[PhaseId]
    creates_labels: tuple[str, ...]
    file_path: str | None = None

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class LabelSpec:
    id: str
    value: str
    phase_ref: PhaseId | None = None  # None for special labels
    substep_ref: str | None = None
    special: bool = False

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ReviewAxisSpec:
    id: str
    letter: str  # "A", "B", "C"
    name: str
    focus: str
    key_questions: tuple[str, ...]

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TitleConvention:
    pattern: str
    label_ref: str
    created_by: RoleId
    phase_ref: PhaseId | None = None

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ProcedureStep:
    """Procedural guidance step for a role or command."""
    order: int
    instruction: str
    command: str | None = None  # beads/git command example
    context: str = ""  # "first-steps", "creating-slice", etc.

# NEW canonical dicts (source of truth)
ROLE_SPECS: dict[RoleId, RoleSpec]
COMMAND_SPECS: dict[str, CommandSpec]
LABEL_SPECS: dict[str, LabelSpec]
REVIEW_AXIS_SPECS: dict[str, ReviewAxisSpec]
TITLE_CONVENTIONS: list[TitleConvention]
```

### Module 3: `scripts/aura_protocol/gen_schema.py` — Python → schema.xml Generator

```python
def generate_schema(output_path: Path) -> None:
    """Generate schema.xml from Python types.py canonical definitions.
    
    Output must pass validate_schema.py validation.
    Uses xml.etree.ElementTree (stdlib).
    """

def verify_round_trip(schema_path: Path) -> list[str]:
    """Parse generated schema.xml, compare against Python types.
    
    Returns list of discrepancies (empty = perfect round-trip).
    """
```

### Module 4: `scripts/aura_protocol/gen_skills.py` — Python → SKILL.md Generator

```python
GENERATED_BEGIN = "<!-- BEGIN GENERATED FROM schema -->"
GENERATED_END = "<!-- END GENERATED -->"

def generate_skill(
    role: RoleId,
    command_id: str | None = None,
    template_dir: Path = Path("skills/templates"),
    output_path: Path | None = None,
) -> str:
    """Generate SKILL.md content for a role or command.
    
    If output_path exists and has a manual section below GENERATED_END,
    that section is preserved. Only the generated header is replaced.
    
    Uses Jinja2 templates from template_dir.
    """

def generate_all_skills(
    template_dir: Path = Path("skills/templates"),
    output_dir: Path = Path("skills"),
) -> dict[str, Path]:
    """Generate all SKILL.md files. Returns {skill_name: output_path}."""
```

### Module 5: `scripts/aura_protocol/context_injection.py` — Runtime Context Injection

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RoleContext:
    """All protocol context relevant to a specific role."""
    role: RoleSpec
    owned_phases: tuple[PhaseSpec, ...]
    constraints: tuple[ConstraintSpec, ...]  # constraints mentioning owned phases
    commands: tuple[CommandSpec, ...]
    handoffs: tuple[HandoffSpec, ...]  # handoffs at owned phases
    labels: tuple[LabelSpec, ...]  # labels for owned phases

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PhaseContext:
    """Protocol context specific to a single phase transition."""
    phase: PhaseSpec
    constraints: tuple[ConstraintSpec, ...]  # constraints specifically for this phase
    labels: tuple[LabelSpec, ...]  # labels for this phase
    transitions: tuple[Transition, ...]  # available transitions
    handoff: HandoffSpec | None  # handoff required at this phase (if any)

def get_role_context(role: RoleId) -> RoleContext:
    """Get all protocol context for a role. Used at skill invocation."""

def get_phase_context(phase: PhaseId) -> PhaseContext:
    """Get protocol context for a phase. Used at phase transitions."""

def format_role_context(ctx: RoleContext) -> str:
    """Format role context as markdown for agent prompt injection."""

def format_phase_context(ctx: PhaseContext) -> str:
    """Format phase context as markdown for agent prompt injection."""
```

### File Tree

```
scripts/
  aura_protocol/
    __init__.py                   # Updated exports
    types.py                      # EXPANDED: +7 new dataclasses, +4 new dicts
    schema_parser.py              # NEW: schema.xml → SchemaSpec bootstrap parser
    gen_schema.py                 # NEW: Python → schema.xml generator
    gen_skills.py                 # NEW: Python → SKILL.md generator (Jinja2)
    context_injection.py          # NEW: runtime role/phase context extraction
    state_machine.py              # HOOK: phase transition emits PhaseContext
    constraints.py                # UNCHANGED
    workflow.py                   # UNCHANGED
    interfaces.py                 # UNCHANGED
skills/
  templates/                      # NEW directory
    role_architect.j2             # Jinja2 template for architect role SKILL.md
    role_supervisor.j2            # Jinja2 template for supervisor role SKILL.md
    role_worker.j2                # Jinja2 template for worker role SKILL.md
    role_reviewer.j2              # Jinja2 template for reviewer role SKILL.md
    command_base.j2               # Base template for command skills
    _macros.j2                    # Shared macros (constraint list, phase table, etc.)
tests/
  test_schema_parser.py           # NEW: parser extracts all entities correctly
  test_gen_schema.py              # NEW: round-trip Python → XML → validate
  test_gen_skills.py              # NEW: generated SKILL.md matches expected
  test_context_injection.py       # NEW: correct context returned per role/phase
  test_schema_types_sync.py       # UPDATED: becomes round-trip verification test
  test_aura_types.py              # UPDATED: covers new spec types
```

### Dependencies

```toml
# pyproject.toml additions
[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = ["pytest>=8.0", "pytest-asyncio>=0.24", "jinja2>=3.1"]
```

Jinja2 is the only new dependency. It's a dev dependency (used by gen_skills.py) — not needed at runtime. The runtime context injection uses Python directly.

## Vertical Slice Decomposition

### SLICE-1: Schema Parser + Type Expansion (Foundation)
Files: schema_parser.py, types.py (expanded), test_schema_parser.py, test_aura_types.py
Depends on: nothing (this is the foundation)
Worker model: sonnet (complex XML parsing + 7 new dataclasses)

### SLICE-2: Schema.xml Generator (Python → XML)
Files: gen_schema.py, test_gen_schema.py, test_schema_types_sync.py (updated)
Depends on: SLICE-1 (needs expanded types)
Worker model: sonnet (XML generation + round-trip verification)

### SLICE-3: SKILL.md Generator (Python → SKILL.md)
Files: gen_skills.py, skills/templates/*.j2, test_gen_skills.py
Depends on: SLICE-1 (needs expanded types)
Worker model: sonnet (Jinja2 templates + marker-based split logic)

### SLICE-4: Runtime Context Injection
Files: context_injection.py, test_context_injection.py, state_machine.py (hook)
Depends on: SLICE-1 (needs expanded types)
Worker model: sonnet (context extraction + state machine integration)

Layer graph:
```
SLICE-1 (Foundation)
    ├─→ SLICE-2 (gen_schema.py)    [parallel]
    ├─→ SLICE-3 (gen_skills.py)    [parallel]
    └─→ SLICE-4 (context_injection)[parallel]
```

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] SchemaSpec extracts all 12 phases with substeps, transitions, TDD layers
- [ ] SchemaSpec extracts all 5 roles with invariants, standing teams, owned phases
- [ ] SchemaSpec extracts all 38 commands with role-ref, phase-refs, creates-labels
- [ ] SchemaSpec extracts all 26 constraints with Given/When/Then/Should
- [ ] SchemaSpec extracts all 6 handoffs with required fields
- [ ] SchemaSpec extracts all 16 labels with phase-ref, substep-ref
- [ ] SchemaSpec extracts all 3 review axes with key questions
- [ ] New spec types (CommandSpec, LabelSpec, RoleSpec, etc.) are frozen dataclasses
- [ ] ROLE_SPECS, COMMAND_SPECS, LABEL_SPECS, REVIEW_AXIS_SPECS are canonical dicts
- [ ] gen_schema.py produces valid schema.xml that passes validate_schema.py
- [ ] Round-trip test: Python types → schema.xml → parse → matches original
- [ ] gen_skills.py generates all role SKILL.md files with correct content
- [ ] gen_skills.py preserves hand-written body below marker
- [ ] Jinja2 templates produce correct phase tables, constraint lists, label schemas
- [ ] get_role_context returns correct constraints/phases/commands per role
- [ ] get_phase_context returns correct constraints/labels/transitions per phase
- [ ] format_role_context produces readable markdown for prompt injection
- [ ] State machine advance() can optionally emit PhaseContext
- [ ] Existing 554 tests pass (no regressions)
- [ ] New spec types have enum coverage and completeness tests

## BDD Acceptance Criteria

### AC1: Schema Parser Bootstrap
**Given** the current schema.xml (1,314 lines) **when** parse_schema() is called **then** returns a SchemaSpec with 12 phases, 5 roles, 38 commands, 26 constraints, 6 handoffs, 16 labels, 3 review axes **should not** silently skip any entity or lose attributes

### AC2: Python as Source of Truth
**Given** types.py with expanded ROLE_SPECS, COMMAND_SPECS, LABEL_SPECS **when** gen_schema.py runs **then** produces a schema.xml that passes validate_schema.py with 0 errors **should not** require manual editing of the generated XML

### AC3: Round-Trip Consistency
**Given** Python types.py as input **when** generating schema.xml then parsing it back **then** all entity counts, IDs, and attribute values match the original Python definitions **should not** have lossy conversion in either direction

### AC4: SKILL.md Generation with Markers
**Given** a role SKILL.md with a hand-written body **when** gen_skills.py runs **then** the generated header between markers is updated but the hand-written body is preserved **should not** overwrite or remove hand-written procedural guidance

### AC5: Role Context Injection
**Given** role=SUPERVISOR **when** get_role_context is called **then** returns phases p7-p12, relevant constraints (C-supervisor-no-impl, C-supervisor-explore-team, C-slice-leaf-tasks, etc.), supervisor commands, and handoff specs h1-h3 **should not** include constraints/commands for unrelated roles

### AC6: Phase Context Injection
**Given** phase=P10_CODE_REVIEW **when** get_phase_context is called **then** returns C-severity-eager, C-review-consensus, C-blocker-dual-parent, the review axis specs, and severity labels **should not** include constraints for unrelated phases

### AC7: No Regressions
**Given** the expanded types.py and new modules **when** running the full test suite **then** all 554 existing tests pass **should not** break state_machine, constraints, workflow, or interfaces

### AC8: Jinja2 Template Quality
**Given** a role Jinja2 template **when** rendering with StrictUndefined **then** all variables are defined from Python specs **should not** have undefined variable errors or missing context

### AC9: Expanded Type Coverage
**Given** all new spec types (CommandSpec, LabelSpec, RoleSpec, SubstepSpec, ReviewAxisSpec, TitleConvention, ProcedureStep) **when** tested **then** enum coverage is 100%, all spec dicts have correct entry counts, and all fields are populated **should not** have empty or placeholder values

description:

BLOCKER findings for SLICE-2 round 1 (Reviewer A)
title: SLICE-2-REVIEW-A-1 BLOCKER

description:
Axis: C (Elegance)
Reviewer: Claude Sonnet 4.6

## Summary

The proposal is structurally sound and demonstrates proportionate decomposition. 4 slices map to 4 orthogonal modules. The categorized aggregation (4 groups by parameter signature) is a principled and minimal solution to the signature diversity problem — superior to a bloated mega-context object or forcing unrelated constraints into a single signature. The optional timestamp pattern, single audit trail for failed transitions, and conftest.py fixture approach all represent correct minimal choices.

## VOTE: ACCEPT

No BLOCKER issues. Two non-blocking observations recorded for implementors.

## Observations (Non-Blocking)

### OBS-1: check_all name is now semantically misleading
The proposal renames check_state_constraints → check_all, but check_all only covers Group 1 (7 of 22 constraints). Original PROPOSAL-1 described check_all as "Run all constraint checks against current state." The rename is justified (the method does run ALL state-checkable checks), but the name will cause confusion for callers who expect it to cover all 22 C-* constraints.

Suggestion: The module docstring MUST clearly state that check_all(state) means "all checks that take only EpochState" — not all 22. The validation checklist item "check_all(state) aggregates 7+ state-checkable constraints" captures this but the API name alone does not. Implementors should add a docstring note like: "Runs all state-only constraint checks (Group 1, 7 checks). For transition checks call check_transition_constraints(); for naming/structural checks call individual methods."

### OBS-2: severity_groups uses str keys instead of SeverityLevel enum
SLICE-1 adds EpochState.severity_groups: dict[str, set[str]]. The proposal rationale says "level→finding_ids" but uses bare str for the key instead of SeverityLevel enum. This is inconsistent with the strongly-typed enum invariant that is itself one of the core goals of FR3 (Type Safety Hardening). The rest of the design uses SeverityLevel for all severity-related fields.

Suggestion: Change to dict[SeverityLevel, set[str]] = field(default_factory=dict). This is a one-line change that maintains the typed invariant and enables check_severity_tree() to use SeverityLevel.BLOCKER/IMPORTANT/MINOR directly as keys rather than string literals.

### OBS-3: Group 4 constraint count is stale in proposal description
The proposal documents "Group 4: Structural (specific IDs/params) — 8" but enumerates 11+ items in the text. This is a documentation inconsistency only, not a code concern. Implementors should verify the actual count when implementing check_structural_constraints().

## Elegance Assessment

| Aspect | Assessment |
|--------|-----------|
| Complexity proportionality | PASS — 4 slices for 4 orthogonal problem axes |
| API cleanliness | PASS with OBS-1 (docstring needed for check_all) |
| Premature abstractions | NONE FOUND |
| Backwards compatibility | PASS — deprecated alias kept, optional timestamp |
| Type consistency | MINOR GAP (OBS-2: severity_groups key type) |
| Minimal design | PASS — no gold-plating; every change addresses a named finding |

All 6 end-user alignment criteria satisfied. MVP scope is appropriate — refinements to v1, not new features. Module boundaries unchanged. Public API surface changes are additive only.

---
references:
  request: aura-plugins-oqhjg
  urd: aura-plugins-bwfqm
  proposal: aura-plugins-qaswj
---

VOTE: REVISE - Two correctness issues found. One is a field name bug; the other is a type signature mismatch with the AuditTrail Protocol.

## Review Axis: Correctness

Reviewer A evaluated PROPOSAL-4 against the existing codebase (types.py, workflow.py, interfaces.py, state_machine.py, audit_activities.py, session_registry.py, flake.nix) for type alignment, data flow consistency, and requirement coverage.

## Findings

### ISSUE 1 (BLOCKER): `state.votes` should be `state.review_votes` in full_state query

In Section 5, the `full_state` query implementation references `state.votes`:

```python
votes=dict(state.votes),
```

But `EpochState` (state_machine.py line 48) defines the field as `review_votes`:

```python
review_votes: dict[ReviewAxis, VoteType] = field(default_factory=dict)
```

`state.votes` would raise `AttributeError` at runtime. The correct line is:

```python
votes=dict(state.review_votes),
```

**Fix:** Change `state.votes` to `state.review_votes` in the `full_state` query code sample.

### ISSUE 2 (IMPORTANT, not blocking): `SqliteAuditTrail.query_events` signature mismatch with AuditTrail Protocol

In Section 10, the proposed `SqliteAuditTrail.query_events` signature is:

```python
async def query_events(self, *, epoch_id: str = "",
                       phase: str = "", role: str = "") -> list[AuditEvent]:
```

But the `AuditTrail` Protocol in interfaces.py (line 140-146) defines:

```python
async def query_events(
    self,
    *,
    epoch_id: str | None = None,
    phase: PhaseId | None = None,
    role: RoleId | None = None,
) -> list[AuditEvent]:
```

Three mismatches:
1. `epoch_id`: `str=""` vs `str | None = None` — empty string vs None sentinel
2. `phase`: `str=""` vs `PhaseId | None = None` — loses typed PhaseId enum
3. `role`: `str=""` vs `RoleId | None = None` — loses typed RoleId enum

While Python runtime structural subtyping (isinstance) only checks method names, a static type checker would flag these as incompatible. More importantly, using `str` instead of `PhaseId | None` is inconsistent with the project's design principle of preferring strongly-typed enums over stringly-typed APIs.

**Fix:** Align `SqliteAuditTrail.query_events` signature with the AuditTrail Protocol:
```python
async def query_events(self, *, epoch_id: str | None = None,
                       phase: PhaseId | None = None,
                       role: RoleId | None = None) -> list[AuditEvent]:
```

## Items Verified Correct

1. **PROPOSAL-1 fixes carried forward:**
   - SliceResult.output field: correctly added as `output: str = ""` (backwards-compatible default)
   - available_transitions: correctly accessed via `self._sm.available_transitions` (property on EpochStateMachine, not EpochState)
   - VoteType case: correctly handled via `.upper()` at CLI boundary (VoteType is UPPER case StrEnum)

2. **Types alignment:** PhaseId, ReviewAxis, VoteType, RoleId, Transition, TransitionRecord, EpochInput all correctly referenced from their existing definitions in types.py / state_machine.py

3. **Data flows:** CLI → Temporal Client → Workflow signal/query → Activity chain is correct for all subcommands (query state, signal vote, phase advance, epoch start, session register)

4. **UAT-1 (SessionRegisterSignal):** Correctly specified as a new signal + query on EpochWorkflow, separate from the existing SessionRegistry (which is OS-level aura-swarm session tracking, not Temporal workflow session tracking)

5. **UAT-2 (auto-register on /epoch):** Correctly specified as a skill-level concern, not a workflow concern. The shell sequence (epoch start → session register → export AURA_EPOCH_ID) is logically sound

6. **UAT-3 (tmux detection):** shutil.which("tmux") in the activity is correct — activity code is allowed to be non-deterministic. Returning SliceResult(success=False) with an actionable error message is the right pattern

7. **QueryStateResult DTO:** Correctly avoids non-serializable EpochState fields (completed_phases uses set, severity_groups uses dict[SeverityLevel, set[str]]). Uses only JSON-safe types

8. **Config resolution:** 4-level priority (CLI > env > YAML > defaults) is consistent between aurad and aura-msg. The shared ConnectionConfig dataclass is a clean design

9. **Slice decomposition:** All 15 requirements + 3 UAT amendments are covered by the 7 slices with no gaps. Dependencies are logically sound

10. **Backwards compatibility:** Existing current_state and available_transitions queries retained alongside new full_state query

11. **Nix packaging:** aura-msg needs temporalio + pyyaml; aurad needs pyyaml + aiosqlite. Both need PYTHONPATH. Correct per flake.nix patterns

## End-User Alignment

- End-users (Claude agents + human operators) get a clean, consistent CLI interface
- Error messages are actionable (listing valid phases, etc.)
- Config resolution priority is intuitive (explicit > implicit)
- Session registration provides visibility into which sessions are participating in an epoch
- Validation checklist covers all user-facing behaviors

## Verdict

REVISE due to Issue 1 (field name bug that would cause AttributeError at runtime). Issue 2 is IMPORTANT but not blocking — it should be fixed for type safety consistency but would not crash at runtime.

description: --- references: proposal: aura-plugins-032u followup_epic: aura-plugins-24f9 --- ## VOTE: ACCEPT Reviewer B (Test Quality axis), Round 1. All 3 slices reviewed. 1609 tests pass, 1 skipped. No BLOCKERs found. --- ## Findings ### BLOCKER: (0 findings) None. ### IMPORTANT: (1 finding) **I-1: Missing test for gate subheadings (FR13)** - **File:** `tests/test_gen_skills.py`, class `TestCompletionChecklistSection` - **What:** FR13 added `**{{ cl.gate.value }} gates:**` subheadings to `skill_header.j2`, but no test asserts that gate subheadings (e.g. `**completion gates:**`) appear in rendered output. Existing tests only check for `### Completion Checklist` section presence and `- [ ]` item format. - **Risk:** If the template subheading rendering breaks silently, no test catches it. The template change is structural (affects output format for all roles with checklists). - **Suggestion:** Add `assert "gates:" in result` (or `"**completion gates:**"`) to `test_worker_has_completion_checklist` or a new dedicated test. One assertion suffices since the template loop is generic. - **Blocks slice?** No — follow-up epic. ### MINOR: (5 findings) **M-1: Vacuous-pass risk in `test_generated_schema_code_elements_use_cdata`** - **File:** `tests/test_gen_schema.py:1348-1358` - **What:** The integration CDATA test loops over `re.findall(...)` matches but does not assert `len(code_matches) > 0` first. If all CodeExamples were removed from types.py in the future, the loop body would never execute and the test would vacuously pass. - **Irony:** This is the exact anti-pattern FR3 fixed (the `if worker_constraints_with_examples:` guard). Currently non-vacuous because 5 `` elements exist, but structurally fragile. - **Suggestion:** Add `assert code_matches, "Generated schema must contain at least one element"` before the loop. **M-2: No test for `]]>` inside CDATA content** - **File:** `scripts/aura_protocol/gen_schema.py:1538-1541` - **What:** `_wrap_code_elements_in_cdata` does not handle `]]>` appearing inside code content. This sequence breaks CDATA sections in XML. No test covers this edge case. - **Risk:** Low in practice (current code examples are bash commands), but a future CodeExample containing `]]>` would produce malformed XML. - **Suggestion:** Add a unit test and, if desired, split `]]>` per XML spec (`]]]]>`). **M-3: `test_constraint_with_examples_renders_code_fence` filters all constraints, not worker-only** - **File:** `tests/test_gen_skills.py:1428-1431` - **What:** The precondition asserts `CONSTRAINT_SPECS.values()` has examples, but the test renders with `RoleId.WORKER`. Should filter to worker-relevant constraints for precision. - **Risk:** Low — both constraints with examples (C-agent-commit, C-dep-direction) are assigned to worker. Would only matter if examples were moved to non-worker constraints. **M-4: Residual `>=` assertions in `test_schema_ext_types.py`** - **File:** `tests/test_schema_ext_types.py:375, 394` - **What:** `len(cl.items) >= 3` for worker-completion (now 6 items) and `len(shared) >= 3` for shared coordination commands. These use the same weak-assertion pattern that FR11 fixed for behavior counts. - **Not in FR11 scope** (FR11 targeted behavior counts specifically), but the same anti-pattern persists here. **M-5: No explicit test for `CL-worker-production-path` checklist item ID** - **File:** `tests/test_schema_parser.py:479-482` - **What:** The count test was updated from `== 5` to `== 6`, which covers the count. But no test asserts `CL-worker-production-path` exists by ID (similar to how `test_worker_completion_first_item_id` checks for `CL-worker-no-todos`). - **Risk:** Low — count check catches additions/removals, but wrong item ID or replacement would not be caught. --- ## Verification Summary | FR | Status | Notes | |----|--------|-------| | FR3 | PASS | Vacuous `if` guard removed; precondition assert + code fence assert now unconditional | | FR4 | PASS | All 3 enum sync assertions changed from `<=` to `==` with bidirectional diff in error message | | FR8 | PASS | Architect workflow test added, asserts both `### Workflows` section and `Architect State Flow` | | FR9 | PASS | Coordination OR removed; single `"| Action |"` assertion | | FR10 | PASS | All 9 loose-OR assertions changed to AND (9 removed, 9 added confirmed) | | FR11 | PASS | All 4 role behavior counts changed to exact `==` with descriptive error messages | | FR12 | PASS | 4 CDATA tests: basic, empty, multiline, integration round-trip | | FR13 | PARTIAL | Template changed but no test for gate subheadings (see I-1) | | Other anti-patterns | N/A | No remaining `assert...or` in error assertions; no remaining `<=` in sync tests | ## Edge Case Coverage (CDATA Tests) | Test | Covers | |------|--------| | `test_wrap_code_elements_in_cdata_basic` | Escaped entities → CDATA unescaping | | `test_wrap_code_elements_in_cdata_empty` | Self-closing `` unchanged | | `test_wrap_code_elements_in_cdata_multiline` | Newlines preserved in CDATA body | | `test_generated_schema_code_elements_use_cdata` | Integration: all generated `` elements use CDATA | | (missing) | `` non-self-closing empty | | (missing) | Content containing `]]>` (CDATA terminator) | 4 tests present, 2 edge cases not covered (both MINOR risk). ## Verdict **ACCEPT** — No BLOCKERs. 1 IMPORTANT finding (missing gate subheading test) and 5 MINOR findings suitable for the existing follow-up epic. Test fixes are complete and correct for all FRs in scope. The CDATA tests provide solid functional coverage for the current codebase.

description:
IMPORTANT finding from REVIEW-B cycle 2.

What went wrong: The fix to dispatchErrors.Error() in internal/hooks/hooks.go (commit 5d0f3c9) correctly replaced the dead-code multi-error path with a real multi-line message builder. However, no test was added to call .Error() on the returned *dispatchErrors value, causing the method body to remain uncovered.

Why it happened: The hooks_test.go tests at lines 313-340 call Dispatch() and check err != nil, but never call err.Error() or errors.As(err, &dispatchErrors{}), so the new code path is not exercised by the test suite.

Where it failed: internal/hooks/hooks.go:219 dispatchErrors.Error() and line 235 Unwrap() — both at 0%.

When it failed: Cycle 2 re-review coverage run (2026-03-09), after the cycle 1 fix was applied.

What it means for the caller: The internal/hooks package now reads 75.0% coverage, below the 80% threshold established in cycle 1. The fix made the code more correct but less tested than before.

How to fix: Add a test in hooks_test.go that:
1. Dispatches to two handlers that both return errors (triggers multi-error path in dispatchErrors.Error())
2. Calls err.Error() and asserts the output contains "[1]" and "[2]" numbered lines
3. Uses errors.As or a type assertion to call Unwrap() and verify both underlying errors are present
title: hooks.dispatchErrors.Error() fix breaks coverage threshold

description:
**File:** scripts/aura_protocol/__init__.py

**Problem:**
The module docstring (lines 85-89) lists Audit Activities:
  init_audit_trail(trail)  — inject AuditTrail implementation before worker start
  record_audit_event(event) — @activity.defn: persist AuditEvent via injected trail
  query_audit_events(epoch_id, phase) — @activity.defn: query AuditEvents by epoch + phase
  InMemoryAuditTrail       — in-process AuditTrail for testing and local dev

BUT these are NOT imported in __init__.py, and NOT listed in __all__.

The docstring claims they are part of the public API of aura_protocol, but:
- `from aura_protocol import init_audit_trail` will fail at runtime.
- `from aura_protocol import InMemoryAuditTrail` will fail at runtime.

The docstring says these come from audit_activities.py but there is no corresponding import statement.

**Fix:**
Either:
a) Add imports from aura_protocol.audit_activities and add to __all__, OR
b) Update the docstring to say 'import from aura_protocol.audit_activities directly (not re-exported)' and remove them from the public API listing.

Option (b) is lower risk. Option (a) would make the docstring accurate and provide a unified import surface.

Additionally, the docstring signature for query_audit_events is stale: it shows `query_audit_events(epoch_id, phase)` but the actual signature is `query_audit_events(epoch_id, phase=None, role=None)` (role was added by SLICE-5).
title: IMPORTANT: audit_activities exports documented in __init__.py docstring but not imported or in __all__

description:
---
references:
  request: aura-plugins-18zp
  urd: aura-plugins-o7i9
  elicit: aura-plugins-iv5j
  proposal: aura-plugins-nkx7
---

## VOTE: REVISE

### Axis: B (Test Quality)

### Summary

The proposal demonstrates strong test design thinking overall -- YAML-driven fixture extension, method-based dispatch for non-EpochState constraints, and proper separation of state-based vs method-based vs transition-based cases. However, I found **one REVISE-worthy parameter mismatch** that would cause a runtime TypeError, plus several important observations for the architect to address.

### REVISE Reason: C-blocker-dual-parent YAML kwarg mismatch (TypeError at runtime)

The proposal specifies the following violation_args for C-blocker-dual-parent:

```yaml
violation_args:
  blocker_task_id: "leaf-1"
  severity_group_id: ""
  followup_slice_id: "fs-1"
```

But the actual method signature is:

```python
def check_blocker_dual_parent(self, blocker_task_id: str, severity_group_id: str, slice_id: str)
```

The YAML key `followup_slice_id` does not match the method parameter `slice_id`. When dispatched via `method(**tc.violation_args)`, this will produce:

```
TypeError: check_blocker_dual_parent() got an unexpected keyword argument 'followup_slice_id'
```

**Fix:** Change `followup_slice_id: "fs-1"` to `slice_id: "fs-1"` in the C-blocker-dual-parent YAML entry.

Note: The similar-sounding `check_followup_leaf_adoption(leaf_task_id, severity_group_id, followup_slice_id)` DOES use `followup_slice_id` -- the architect likely confused the two method signatures.

### Additional Observations (non-blocking but important for correctness)

**1. C-audit-never-delete and C-audit-dep-chain: _factory approach is sound but needs careful dispatch.**

The proposal routes these through `violation_state` (set by factory) with `violation_method=None`, meaning they use the existing `check_state()` dispatch. This is correct -- `check_audit_trail()` is aggregated inside `check_state()`. The factory approach for constructing TransitionRecord objects is the right call since TransitionRecord is a frozen dataclass that cannot be represented as flat YAML.

However, the proposal should clarify: the `_factory` key is consumed by `_coerce_violation_args` or by a separate branch in `generate_constraint_violation_test_cases()`. The generator code snippet in the proposal shows a `violation_method` branch only. The factory branch for audit constraints needs to be shown as a separate dispatch path in the generator (before the violation_method check), since these entries will have `_factory` in their YAML but no `violation_method`.

**2. Violation args correctness audit (do they actually trigger violations?):**

I verified each proposed violation_args against the actual check method logic:

| Constraint | Args | Triggers Violation? | Correct? |
|------------|------|---------------------|----------|
| C-review-binary | vote: "MAYBE" | Yes -- "MAYBE" not in VoteType values | OK |
| C-dep-direction | parent_id: "", child_id: "child-abc" | Yes -- empty parent_id | OK |
| C-agent-commit | commit_command: "git commit -m 'feat: something'" | Yes -- contains "git commit" without "agent-" | OK |
| C-proposal-naming | title: "SLICE-1: Bad proposal title" | Yes -- does not match PROPOSAL-{N}: | OK |
| C-review-naming | title: "PROPOSAL-1: Wrong type for review" | Yes -- does not match {SCOPE}-REVIEW-{A|B|C}-{N} | OK |
| C-followup-lifecycle | followup_task_title: "URE: Missing FOLLOWUP prefix" | Yes -- does not start with FOLLOWUP_ | OK |
| C-ure-verbatim | question: "Q?", options: [], response: "A" | Yes -- empty options list | OK |
| C-frontmatter-refs | task_description: "No frontmatter", required_ref_keys: ["urd"] | Yes -- no "---" prefix | OK |
| C-blocker-dual-parent | blocker_task_id: "leaf-1", severity_group_id: "", followup_slice_id: "fs-1" | **TypeError** -- wrong kwarg name | **FIX** |
| C-followup-timing | has_important_or_minor: true, followup_created: false | Yes -- findings exist but no followup | OK |
| C-followup-leaf-adoption | leaf_task_id: "leaf-1", severity_group_id: "", followup_slice_id: "fs-1" | Yes -- empty severity_group_id | OK |
| C-supervisor-no-impl | role: "supervisor", action_type: "file_edit" | Yes -- supervisor + impl action | OK |
| C-supervisor-cartographers | phase: "p8", has_explore_team: false | Yes (after coercion to PhaseId) -- at p8 without team | OK |
| C-integration-points | has_integration_points: false | Yes -- no integration points | OK |
| C-slice-review-before-close | slice_closed_by_worker: true, review_completed: false | Yes -- worker closed without review | OK |
| C-max-review-cycles | review_cycle_count: 4 | Yes -- exceeds max of 3 | OK |
| C-slice-leaf-tasks | slice_id: "slice-1", leaf_task_ids: [] | Yes -- no leaf tasks | OK |
| C-vertical-slices | production_code_path: "src/module.py", owner_ids: ["worker-1", "worker-2"] | Yes -- 2 owners | OK |

**19 of 20 violation_args correctly trigger violations.** The one failure (C-blocker-dual-parent) is a kwarg name mismatch that must be fixed.

**3. _coerce_violation_args coverage assessment:**

The proposal lists these coercions:
- `phase` string -> `PhaseId` enum (needed for C-supervisor-cartographers)
- `options` YAML list -> `list[str]` (C-ure-verbatim)
- `leaf_task_ids` / `owner_ids` YAML list -> `list[str]` (C-slice-leaf-tasks, C-vertical-slices)
- `required_ref_keys` YAML list -> `list[str]` (C-frontmatter-refs)
- Boolean/int preserved as-is

This is sufficient. YAML natively handles bool and int, and lists come through as Python lists from `yaml.safe_load()`. The only truly necessary coercion is `phase` -> `PhaseId`. The list coercions are identity operations (YAML already provides lists), but documenting them as explicit passthrough is reasonable defensive coding.

**4. Test isolation with start_local():**

Each of the 4 Temporal sandbox tests creates its own `WorkflowEnvironment.start_local()` context manager. This means 4 separate ephemeral Temporal servers spin up sequentially. This is correct for isolation but may be slow (~2-4s per server startup). The proposal mentions caching via `functools.cache` on the probe, but each test method creates its own environment independently. This is acceptable but worth noting for CI runtime.

The `_temporal_sandbox_works()` probe also needs updating to `start_local()` -- the proposal correctly identifies this (line 866 in current code uses `start_time_skipping()`).

**5. Count discrepancy: 25 vs 24 un-skipped.**

The proposal correctly notes that C-actionable-errors stays skipped, making the actual un-skip count 24 (not 25). But the URD says "0 skipped" as the target (R3). The proposal's validation checklist says "1 skipped (C-actionable-errors)" which is correct. The URD target should be updated to match: 1 skipped, not 0.

**6. Dispatch logic completeness:**

The proposed test dispatch is:
```python
if tc.violation_state is not None:
    violations = _CHECKER.check_state(tc.violation_state)
elif tc.violation_method is not None:
    method = getattr(_CHECKER, tc.violation_method)
    violations = method(**tc.violation_args)
else:
    assert tc.violation_from_phase is not None
    violations = _CHECKER.check_handoff_required(...)
```

This three-way dispatch is correct and complete. The `getattr` approach is safe here because the method names come from the YAML fixture file (trusted input, not user input). The fallthrough to `check_handoff_required` with the assert is a proper safety net.

### End-User Alignment

1. **End-users:** Developers running the test suite.
2. **What they want:** All 24 un-skippable constraints exercised; clear test output when constraint logic breaks.
3. **Impact:** Shifts from 25 skipped (false confidence) to 1 skipped (honest coverage).
4. **Implementation gaps:** The C-blocker-dual-parent kwarg mismatch would cause a test TypeError, which is worse than a skip -- it is a test error that pollutes output. Must be fixed before implementation.
5. **MVP scope:** Appropriate -- all check methods already exist.
6. **Validation checklist:** Complete and verifiable, modulo the count (should be 24 un-skipped, 1 skipped).

### Verdict

**VOTE: REVISE** -- The C-blocker-dual-parent `followup_slice_id` -> `slice_id` parameter mismatch must be corrected. This is a single-line fix in the YAML mapping table. Once corrected, the proposal is ready for ratification.
title: PROPOSAL-1-REVIEW-B-1: Un-skip 25 tests

## 7 UAT Amendments

1. **owner_roles declaration order**: In SerializablePhaseSpec.from_spec(), change sorted(spec.owner_roles, key=lambda r: r.value) to list(spec.owner_roles) — preserve frozenset iteration order (which is the declaration order from PHASE_SPECS)

3. **ToolCall JSON camelCase aliases**: Add camelCase aliases. Since these are frozen dataclasses (not Pydantic), document the alias convention in docstring and add a to_json_dict() method or use __init_subclass__ pattern if the codebase supports it. At minimum, document the JSON field mapping.
4. **InMemoryAuditTrail.query_events role typing**: Change role: object = None → role: RoleId | None = None in audit_activities.py
5. **Refactor test_worker.py to DI-only**: Remove unittest.mock usage. Pass argv as parameter to parse_args, use dict merge for env vars.
6. **Remove duplicate contract tests**: Remove overlapping tests from test_aura_types.py that duplicate test_serializable_phase_spec.py coverage.
7. **Rename ReviewAxis enum values**: A→CORRECTNESS, B→TEST_QUALITY, C→ELEGANCE. Update _REVIEW_AXES in state_machine.py. Update all references in workflow.py, test files, conftest.py.

## Specification

Wave 2 slice (parallel with SLICE-B/D). Ports validate_schema.py (769 lines) to Go. Depends on SLICE-A validate.go interface types.

### Architecture
- Parser: encoding/xml → XMLNode generic tree (NOT streaming decoder)
- Implement Iter(tag), Find(tag), FindAll(tag), Attr(name) helpers on *XMLNode
- Use xml.Unmarshal into XMLNode for full document parse

### 3 Validation Layers
Layer 1 — buildIndex(root *XMLNode) (SchemaIndex, []ValidationError):
  Parse tree, extract all IDs, check required attrs, unique IDs

Layer 2 — checkRefs(root *XMLNode, index SchemaIndex) []ValidationError:
  All cross-refs resolve (phase-ref, role-ref, command-ref, etc.)
  11 major reference types from Python implementation

Layer 3 — checkSemantics(root *XMLNode, index SchemaIndex) []ValidationError:
  Port 12 implemented semantic rules (numbered 1-6 and 9-14; rules 7-8 absent):
  1. Phase numbers sequential (contiguous 1..N)
  2. Phase domain consistency (hardcoded mapping)
  3. Each phase has >= 1 substep
  4. Substep order sequential within phase
  5. Parallel substeps must have parallel-group or instances
  6. Label value uniqueness
  9. Each role owns >= 1 phase
  10. Command with <phases> requires <file> child
  11. Review axis letters unique
  12. Startup sequence step orders sequential
  13. Agent template min-count <= max-count
  14. Domain enum values match phase domains

### Error Contract (PROPOSAL-4 Fix A2)
- io.Reader read failure → return (nil, error)
- XML parse failure → return ([]ValidationError{{Layer: Structural}}, nil)
- Violations → return ([]ValidationError{...}, nil)
- Valid → return (nil, nil)

### Hard Independence Constraint
- Validator MUST NOT import codegen schema types (schema_types.go)
- Only imports validate.go types (ErrorLayer, ValidationError, SchemaIndex, XMLNode)

## Files Owned
- internal/codegen/validate.go (MODIFY — implement stubs from SLICE-A)

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] All 12 semantic rules ported from Python
- [ ] 3 layers: structural, referential, semantic
- [ ] XMLNode tree helpers: Iter, Find, FindAll, Attr
- [ ] Error contract matches PROPOSAL-4 Fix A2
- [ ] No imports from schema_types.go
- [ ] Existing tests still pass
title: SLICE-C: 3-layer schema validator port

## Pasture Go Port Implementation Plan

Port Python aura-protocol (aurad + aura-msg + aura-release) to Go as Pasture.
New repo: github.com/dayvidpham/pasture (submodule of aura-plugins at ./pasture/).

## Vertical Slices

### Wave 1: Foundation (parallel, no deps)
- SLICE-1: Project scaffold (go.mod, flake.nix, Makefile, cmd/ stubs, CI skeleton)
- SLICE-2: Types + constants (pkg/protocol/, internal/types/, internal/temporal/constants.go)
- SLICE-3: Config + errors (internal/config/, internal/errors/)

### Wave 2: Core (parallel, depends on Wave 1)
- SLICE-4: Formatters + hooks (internal/formatters/, internal/hooks/)
- SLICE-5: Handlers + pasture-msg CLI (internal/handlers/, cmd/pasture-msg/)
- SLICE-6: Temporal workflows (internal/temporal/ workflows, state machine, activities)
- SLICE-7: Audit trail (internal/audit/)

### Wave 3: Integration (parallel, depends on Wave 2)
- SLICE-8: pastured daemon (cmd/pastured/)
- SLICE-9: ACP types + indexer + adapters (internal/acp/ minus client)
- SLICE-10: ACP live client (internal/acp/client.go)

### Wave 3.5: Independent (parallel with Waves 2-3, depends on Wave 1 only)
- SLICE-11: pasture-release (internal/release/, cmd/pasture-release/)

### Wave 4: Distribution (depends on S5, S8, S11)
- SLICE-12: Distribution + install-cli (flake.nix buildGoModule, CI, skills/)

## Layer Integration Points

| Integration Point | Owner | Consumers | Contract | Merge Timing |
|-------------------|-------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| pkg/protocol types | S2 | S4,S5,S6,S7,S8,S9 | PhaseId, EventType, AuditEvent | Wave 1 complete |
| internal/types enums | S2 | S4,S5,S6,S7,S8 | VoteType, ReviewAxis, OutputFormat, etc. | Wave 1 complete |
| temporal/constants | S2 | S5,S6,S8 | Signal/query name constants | Wave 1 complete |
| internal/config | S3 | S5,S6,S8 | ConnectionConfig, PasturedConfig, PastureMsgConfig | Wave 1 complete |
| internal/errors | S3 | S4,S5,S6,S7,S8,S9,S10 | StructuredError, Category, Report() | Wave 1 complete |
| internal/hooks | S4 | S6,S8 | HookHandler, Manager, HookEvent | Wave 2 merge |
| internal/formatters | S4 | S5 | FormatEpochState, FormatStartResult, etc. | Wave 2 merge |
| internal/handlers | S5 | S8 | Handler functions (QueryState, EpochStart, etc.) | Wave 2 merge |
| internal/temporal | S6 | S7,S8 | Workflows, activities, state machine | Wave 2 merge |
| internal/audit | S7 | S8 | Trail interface, InMemory/Sqlite impls | Wave 2 merge |
| internal/acp indexer | S9 | S10 | SharedIndexer, Adapter interface | Wave 3 merge |
title: IMPL_PLAN: Pasture Go port — 12 slices across 5 waves

description:
---
references:
  request: aura-plugins-oqhjg
  urd: aura-plugins-bwfqm
  proposal: aura-plugins-r30k3
---

## Components Reviewed

### Component 1: aura-msg CLI Structure
**Definition shown:** Nested subcommands: `query state`, `signal vote`, `phase advance`, `epoch start` with `--format json|text` and connection flags.
**Example shown:** `aura-msg query state --epoch-id E1 --format json` → JSON output; `--format text` → human-readable output.
**Question:** Does the nested subcommand pattern match your intent?
**Options:** ACCEPT nested as proposed (extensible, matches original request) | REVISE: different grouping
**User response:** ACCEPT nested as proposed
**Feedback:** No additional feedback
**Decision:** ACCEPT

### Component 2: Config Resolution + YAML File
**Definition shown:** Single `aurad.config.yaml` with `aurad:` and `aura-msg:` sections. Priority: CLI > env > YAML > defaults.
**Example shown:** YAML config file at `~/.config/aura/plugins/aurad.config.yaml` with per-section connection settings.
**Question:** Should YAML have shared connection key or per-section duplication?
**Options:** ACCEPT per-section duplication (simpler, allows divergent configs) | REVISE: shared connection key (less duplication)
**User response:** ACCEPT per-section duplication
**Feedback:** No additional feedback
**Decision:** ACCEPT

### Component 3: SqliteAuditTrail + Temporal SA Dual-Write
**Definition shown:** SQLite writes in activities, SA upserts in workflow code. Independent writes.
**Clarification needed:** User asked "What is SA?" — explained Search Attributes (key-value metadata on Temporal workflows for forensic queries in the Web UI).
**Question:** Is independent dual-write the right failure isolation?
**Options:** ACCEPT independent writes (SQLite is source of truth, SA is convenience) | REVISE: transactional
**User response:** ACCEPT independent writes (after clarification)
**Decision:** ACCEPT

### Component 4: SliceWorkflow Execution
**Definition shown:** Dual mode — shell command (aurad runs subprocess) and signal-driven (external agents signal completion). Both with timeout + heartbeat.
**Extended discussion:**
- User asked: "Does signal-only mode allow aurad to run aura-swarm?"
- Clarified: Signal-only requires external actor. Shell command mode is needed for aurad-initiated work.
- User asked: "Would we want the UI to be running aura-swarm, not aurad? But we would want to be able to close and re-open the UI."
- Clarified: aurad (durable daemon) launches aura-swarm via shell activity, then switches to signal-driven waiting. UI is ephemeral, sends commands via aura-msg.
**Final question:** Refined hybrid model: aurad launches aura-swarm (shell), waits for completion signals. UI ephemeral, aurad durable.
**User response:** ACCEPT hybrid launch+signal. Also: "we don't require a dependency on tmux, but right now we will. For right now, we should detect whether the user has tmux installed or not."
**Decision:** ACCEPT with amendment — detect tmux availability at runtime, don't hard-require it

### Component 5: Claude Code Hook Integration
**Definition shown:** SessionStart hook queries epoch state when AURA_EPOCH_ID is set. Best-effort, non-blocking.
**Question:** Query-only for now, or also register session?
**Options:** ACCEPT query-only | REVISE: also register session
**User response:** REVISE: also register session. Hook should register Claude session ID with aurad so UI can track active sessions.
**User notes (verbatim):** "Also: if the user launches a fresh Claude instance with no epoch env var set, and then calls the /epoch skill to create a request, it should auto-register at this point. We should also plan for how we will move away from Beads. Beads is highly unstable and quickly turning into a garbage fire."
**Decision:** REVISE — need session registration + auto-registration on /epoch

### Catch-all
**Question:** Is there anything from your original requirements not reflected?
**User response:** Nothing missing

## UAT Amendments Required

### UAT-1: Session registration
- SessionStart hook MUST register Claude session ID with aurad (not just query)
- New `aura-msg session register` subcommand needed
- aurad needs session tracking mechanism (active sessions per epoch)

### UAT-2: Auto-registration on /epoch
- When a fresh Claude instance (no AURA_EPOCH_ID) calls /aura:epoch
- The epoch skill MUST auto-register the session with the newly created epoch
- This means calling `aura-msg session register --epoch-id <new-id> --session-id <claude-session-id>`

### UAT-3: tmux detection
- SliceWorkflow shell command mode MUST detect tmux availability at runtime
- Graceful fallback if tmux is not installed (not a hard dependency)

### UAT-4: Beads migration planning (deferred)

description:
---
references:
  related_epoch: aura-plugins-220
  variant_rich_paper: /home/minttea/dev/agent-data-leverage/unified-schema/llm/research/variant-rich-paper.pdf
  agentfilter_project: /home/minttea/codebases/dayvidpham/agentfilter/
---

## Verbatim User Request

### SessionEntry, session_entries

The next thing is: the database design and code interface design that can encapsulate all the various steps, constraints, and roles of our PROCESS is going to be tricky. These will inherently be dealing with many variant roles and phases, each with their own schema for their transcripts, entries, tool calls, etc. This problem has a few steps.

1. Part of this is a literature research task: I put a reference paper that deals with how to deal with variant-rich software environments in @llm/research/variant-rich-paper. Find its references and related works, and determine what architectures or solutions the software engineering research community has developed to deal with this problem. We are in a variant-rich software environment because our ingestion pipeline must account for (MANY model harnesses which may have MANY configurations, plugins and also interface with MANY models).

2. There is another research task: we need a standardized, stable, uniform interface that we can use to adapt the transcript entries into. This is essentially the purpose of the Agent Context Protocol (ACP), which has been officially merged into the Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A). These have also been officially donated to the Linux Foundation. These protocols integrate and extend the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
   * ACP: (https://agentcommunicationprotocol.dev/introduction/welcome) and its official OpenAPI spec (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/i-am-bee/acp/refs/heads/main/docs/spec/openapi.yaml)
   * A2A: official v1.0 spec (https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/definitions/) ; official A2A Go SDK (https://github.com/a2aproject/a2a-go) ; 
   * MCP: official spec at (https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25) and specified as TypeScript (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/refs/heads/main/schema/2025-11-25/schema.ts)
We should define a database schema and design that is compliant with the A2A and MCP schema. When researching, use these links provided as the official, single source of truth. I have an example of an ACP-compliant Claude Code transcript parser and filtering project at @~/codebases/dayvidpham/agentfilter/ .

### Model Name ID Refactor

Let's also use the database of models at https://github.com/anomalyco/models.dev, since we need a stable model name identifier.

## Classification (4 Axes)

### Scope: LARGE
- Database schema design for variant-rich transcript storage
- Protocol compliance with 3 specs (A2A v1.0, ACP, MCP 2025-11-25)
- Literature survey on variant-rich SPL architectures
- Model name ID standardization via models.dev
- Integration with existing agentfilter project patterns

### Complexity: HIGH
- Multiple protocol specs to reconcile (A2A extends ACP extends MCP)
- Variant-rich environment: MANY model harnesses × MANY configs × MANY models
- Must handle variant transcripts per role/phase/tool with different schemas
- Relevant SPL techniques: Feature Model Synthesis, Constraints Extraction, Variant Integration

### Risk: MEDIUM
- Research and design phase — not touching production yet
- Risk of over-engineering the schema if we don't bound scope
- Risk of protocol spec drift (A2A recently donated to Linux Foundation)

### Domain Novelty: HIGH
- A2A v1.0 spec released 2025 — cutting edge
- SPL variant-rich evolution is active research area (Strüber et al. SPLC'19, 35 citations)
- No existing unified transcript schema standard for multi-harness agent systems
title: REQUEST: SessionEntry database design — A2A/MCP-compliant variant-rich transcript schema

description:
Axis-C finding (Elegance/Naming Consistency).

SubstepSpec.type (types.py line 187) is typed as bare str. Valid values seen in schema.xml are: classify, research, explore, elicit, urd, propose, review, uat, ratify, handoff, plan, slice, landing. These are a closed set.

The existing codebase uses str enums for all other closed sets (PhaseId, Domain, RoleId, ExecutionMode, ContentLevel, ReviewAxis). SubstepSpec.type is the only field in a new dataclass using bare str for what appears to be a closed set.

This is minor because: (1) SubstepSpec is mainly used in the bootstrap tool and parser, not at runtime; (2) the generator writes these back to XML correctly without validation.

Suggestion: Define a SubstepType enum with the 13 known values. Minor because SubstepSpec is not part of the critical runtime path (context injection, Temporal).
title: SLICE-1-REVIEW-C-1-F4: SubstepSpec.type is bare str — consider SubstepType enum for consistency
