## Specification

Add all new types and data to types.py as the source of truth for R1-R7.

### New Enums (5)
- ExampleLabel(StrEnum): correct, anti-pattern, context, template
- ExampleLang(StrEnum): bash, go, python, pseudo, xml, json, markdown
- GateType(StrEnum): completion, slice-closure, review-ready, landing
- WorkflowExecution(StrEnum): sequential, parallel, conditional-loop
- ExitConditionType(StrEnum): success, continue, escalate, proceed (reviewer feedback — NOT str)

### New Dataclasses (9)
- CodeExample(frozen): id, lang(ExampleLang), label(ExampleLabel), code(str), also_illustrates(str|None)
- BehaviorSpec(frozen): id, given, when, then, should_not
- ChecklistItem(frozen): id, text, required(bool=True)
- Checklist(frozen): role_ref(RoleId), gate(GateType), items(tuple[ChecklistItem,...])
- CoordinationCommand(frozen): id, action, template, role_ref(RoleId|None), shared(bool=False)
- WorkflowAction(frozen): id, instruction, command(str|None)
- ExitCondition(frozen): type(ExitConditionType), condition(str)  ← ExitConditionType NOT str
- WorkflowStage(frozen): id, name, order, execution(WorkflowExecution), phase_ref(PhaseId|None), actions(tuple[WorkflowAction,...]), exit_conditions(tuple[ExitCondition,...])
- Workflow(frozen): id, name, role_ref(RoleId), description, stages(tuple[WorkflowStage,...])

### Modified Dataclasses (3) — backward compatible defaults
- ConstraintSpec: +command(str|None=None), +examples(tuple[CodeExample,...]=())
- ProcedureStep: +examples(tuple[CodeExample,...]=())
- RoleSpec: +introduction(str|None=None), +ownership_narrative(str|None=None), +behaviors(tuple[BehaviorSpec,...]=())

### New Canonical Dicts (3) — populate with data
- CHECKLIST_SPECS: dict[str, Checklist] keyed by "{role}-{gate}"
- COORDINATION_COMMANDS: dict[str, CoordinationCommand]
- WORKFLOW_SPECS: dict[str, Workflow] — 3 workflows: Ride the Wave, Layer Cake, Architect State Flow

### Populate Existing Dicts
- ROLE_SPECS: Add introduction + ownership_narrative for all 5 roles
- ROLE_SPECS: Add behaviors for supervisor, worker, architect, reviewer
- CONSTRAINT_SPECS: Add command field where applicable
- CONSTRAINT_SPECS: Add examples (CodeExample) where applicable

## Files Owned
- scripts/aura_protocol/types.py

## Acceptance Criteria
Given types.py with all new types when importing then no errors and all enums/dataclasses are frozen/immutable should never break existing imports or canonical dict structure

Given modified ConstraintSpec/ProcedureStep/RoleSpec when existing code uses them then backward compatible (all new fields have defaults) should never require changes to callers

Given ExitCondition.type when accessing then is ExitConditionType(StrEnum) should never be plain str

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] 5 new enums (including ExitConditionType)
- [ ] 9 new frozen dataclasses
- [ ] 3 modified dataclasses with backward-compatible defaults
- [ ] CHECKLIST_SPECS populated
- [ ] COORDINATION_COMMANDS populated
- [ ] WORKFLOW_SPECS populated with 3 workflows
- [ ] ROLE_SPECS updated with introduction, ownership_narrative, behaviors
- [ ] CONSTRAINT_SPECS updated with command and examples where applicable
- [ ] All existing imports still work
title: SLICE-1: Data Model — types.py enums, dataclasses, canonical dicts

---

## Scope

Pure lookup module for runtime constraint/role/phase context injection.

### Files Created
- scripts/aura_protocol/context_injection.py — get_role_context(RoleId) → RoleContext, get_phase_context(PhaseId) → PhaseContext. Returns frozen dataclasses with ConstraintContext objects (typed when+then, UAT-4). Static _ROLE_CONSTRAINTS and _PHASE_CONSTRAINTS dicts (Python is SoT; gen_schema.py in SLICE-2 exports as role-ref/phase-ref). No side effects, no Temporal dependency.
- tests/test_context_injection.py — Role context correctness (AC5), role context absence (AC5a), phase context correctness (AC6), phase context absence (AC6a), ConstraintContext field verification

### Acceptance Criteria
- AC5: get_role_context(SUPERVISOR) returns RoleContext with p7-p12 phases, ConstraintContext objects with typed when+then, relevant commands (UAT-4)
- AC5a: SUPERVISOR context does NOT include C-worker-gates. WORKER context does NOT include C-supervisor-no-impl.
- AC6: get_phase_context(P10_CODE_REVIEW) returns PhaseContext with ConstraintContext objects with typed when+then, severity constraints, review axes (UAT-4)
- AC6a: P4_REVIEW context does NOT include C-severity-eager. P9_SLICE context does NOT include C-review-consensus.
- AC7: All 554 existing tests pass

### Implementation Notes
- context_injection.py is a pure lookup module — reads from canonical dicts in types.py, returns frozen dataclasses
- _ROLE_CONSTRAINTS: dict[RoleId, frozenset[str]] — hand-authored from constraint given: text
- _PHASE_CONSTRAINTS: dict[PhaseId, frozenset[str]] — hand-authored from constraint given: text
- ConstraintContext(id, when, then) — populated by looking up ConstraintSpec from CONSTRAINT_SPECS
- Invertible pattern: PHASE_SPECS[phase].owner_roles gives role→phase mapping
- Called at skill invocation time (role-level baseline) and at phase transition time (phase-level enrichment)
- NOT called from Temporal workflow — called from prompt construction layer
title: SLICE-4: Runtime Context Injection with ConstraintContext

description: --- references: review: aura-plugins-qxgg --- ## Finding scripts/aura_protocol/gen_schema.py emits content using code_el.text = ex.code (standard ElementTree XML text escaping) instead of CDATA sections. The proposal explicitly chose CDATA as a design decision: 'CDATA for code examples (DocBook pattern)' listed in the proposal tradeoffs table. ## Actual behavior A bash example like: if [[ $foo < 10 ]]; then echo "hello"; fi Is emitted as: if [[ $foo < 10 ]]; then echo "hello"; fi Instead of: ## Impact - XML round-trips correctly (ElementTree unescapes on parse) — no behavioral defect - schema.xml is less human-readable when examples contain special characters - Deviates from stated design intent without documentation ## Fix Post-process the serialized XML to wrap text content in CDATA sections, or use a CDATA-aware serializer. Standard library xml.etree.ElementTree does not natively support CDATA output; lxml or a regex post-processing step can produce it.
title: MINOR-C-1: CDATA not implemented for code examples

7. Remove bin/aura-parallel after merge

description:
---
references:
  request: aura-plugins-dqfz
  followup: aura-plugins-c4pa
  urd_original: aura-plugins-m8az
  followup_urd: aura-plugins-rtln
  proposal: aura-plugins-caqk
---
## VOTE: ACCEPT

### Axis: Test Quality (B)

The proposal claims 10 new tests across 4 test files. After examining the existing test infrastructure, the proposed test strategy, and the 5 coverage areas requested, I find the plan adequate with minor observations.

### Analysis of 5 Coverage Areas

**1. command_refs field validation (sync with COMMAND_SPECS keys) — ADEQUATE**

Proposal: 2 tests in test_schema_types_sync.py — (a) field present on Figure, (b) FIGURE_SPECS command_refs values are valid COMMAND_SPECS keys.

This follows the established pattern exactly. TestFigureSpecsSync already has `test_all_figure_workflow_refs_valid` (validates workflow_refs are WORKFLOW_SPECS keys) and `test_all_figure_role_refs_valid` (validates role_refs are RoleId members). Adding an analogous `test_all_figure_command_refs_valid` that checks `command_refs ⊆ COMMAND_SPECS.keys()` is the right approach. The existing fixtures (schema_root module-scoped fixture) support this cleanly.

**2. Sub-skill figure rendering (position, content, markers) — ADEQUATE**

Proposal: 3 tests in test_gen_skills.py — position, content, marker presence.

These parallel the existing `TestFigurePositionalRendering` class which already tests figure-after-workflow for supervisor, worker, and architect. The proposal adds analogous tests for the new `generate_sub_skill()` / `_render_sub_skill_header()` path. Key question: the proposal says "sub-skill figure rendering position, content, marker presence" — 3 distinct assertions. The existing pattern uses tmp_path + _make_skill_file helper + write=False mode, asserting position and content in a single test. The proposal splitting into 3 tests is actually better granularity than the existing pattern. Each test has a single failure mode, which is good practice.

One observation: The proposal does not explicitly mention testing the negative case (sub-skill file with no matching figures should be skipped). However, the BDD criterion "Given a sub-skill with no command_refs figures when generating then the sub-skill file is skipped" implies this should be tested. The 3 tests cover the happy path. This gap is minor since the skip-if-empty behavior is a guard clause, not a complex code path.

**3. Schema round-trip (command-ref elements) — ADEQUATE**

Proposal: 2 tests in test_schema_parser.py — (a) command-ref parsed, (b) round-trip with command_refs.

The existing test_schema_parser.py has TestSchemaParserFigures which validates figure count, attributes match (id, title, type, role_refs, section_ref, workflow_refs). Adding command_refs to the attribute comparison in `test_figure_attributes_match` is the natural extension. The round-trip test (gen_schema emits command-ref, schema_parser reads it back, values match FIGURE_SPECS) follows the existing integration testing pattern.

**4. Layer-cake L{M} content correctness — ADEQUATE**

Proposal: 1 test in test_gen_skills.py checking layer-cake content shows L{M} pattern.

This is straightforward content verification. The test should assert the generated output does NOT contain hardcoded "Layer 3" as the terminal layer and DOES contain the variable-layer pattern. Single test is sufficient since this is a YAML content change, not a code logic change.

**5. Supervisor body section removal verification — ADEQUATE**

Proposal: 1 test in test_gen_skills.py verifying body sections are removed.

This tests that after generation, supervisor/SKILL.md body does NOT contain the "Ride the Wave — Full Execution Cycle" heading or the "Layer Cake Parallelism (TDD Approach)" heading. A single test checking absence of both section headings is sufficient. The existing `generate_skill()` function preserves body content outside markers, so the removal must happen as a separate step (or the test verifies the final state). This is the right approach — assert the observable outcome (no duplicate sections in output), not internal state.

### Test Quality Criteria Assessment

| Criterion | Verdict |
|-----------|---------|
| Favour integration tests over brittle unit tests | YES — tests use real FIGURE_SPECS, real templates, real schema.xml. No synthetic specs. |
| System under test NOT mocked — mock dependencies only | YES — generate_skill() called with real template_dir, real role context. Only the file system (tmp_path) is test infrastructure. |
| Shared fixtures for common test values | YES — reuses existing schema_root, parsed_spec module-scoped fixtures. Uses _make_skill_file helper. |
| Assert observable outcomes, not internal state | YES — checks rendered output content, not internal data structures. |

### End-User Impact

The end-users are agent models consuming SKILL.md files. The tests ensure:
- Sub-skill files get the right figures (agent reads correct visual instructions)
- Supervisor SKILL.md has no contradictory duplicate sections (no confusion between auto-generated and hand-written versions)
- Layer-cake language is accurate (agents don't assume exactly 3 layers)

### Minor Observations (not blocking)

1. The proposal mentions "test_context_injection.py: 1 test (command_refs preserved through figure loading)" — this is a pass-through test since _load_figure_content reads YAML content, not command_refs metadata. The test would verify that the command_refs field survives the get_role_context() pipeline, which is already implicitly covered by the schema sync tests. However, having an explicit test for this is still valuable for regression safety.

2. No negative test for "sub-skill with empty command_refs" skip behavior, but this is implied by the BDD criteria and can be added during implementation without plan revision.

3. The 10-test count is reasonable for the scope. Not over-tested, not under-tested.

### Conclusion

The test strategy follows established patterns, favours integration tests, does not mock the SUT, uses shared fixtures, and asserts observable outcomes. Coverage addresses all 5 requested areas. The proposal is ACCEPT on the test quality axis.
title: FOLLOWUP_PROPOSAL-1-REVIEW-B-1: Test quality

description:
refs: aura-plugins-jr9n2, aura-plugins-onw3g

File: pasture/internal/handlers/client.go:38-95

The production DefaultClientFactory, realClient, and its methods (Close, QueryWorkflow, SignalWorkflow, ExecuteWorkflow, CancelWorkflow, TerminateWorkflow) are all at 0% coverage. This is expected: they require a live Temporal server. The DI design is correct — all handlers accept a TemporalClientFactory and tests inject mockClient.

However, the realClient.ExecuteWorkflow has a defensive type assertion (options.(client.StartWorkflowOptions)) that returns a StructuredError if the wrong type is passed. This path is not reachable from any production caller, but represents dead code that could confuse future maintainers.

Fix: Add a comment noting that DefaultClientFactory/realClient are integration-test targets only. Consider removing the defensive type assertion and replacing it with a typed parameter (removing the interface{} options type) to eliminate the dead branch.
title: handlers.DefaultClientFactory and realClient at 0% - intentional but worth noting

description:
In test_gen_schema.py::TestRoundTripConsistency::test_roundtrip_procedure_steps_supervisor (line 596) and test_roundtrip_procedure_steps_worker (line 605), the tests only assert len(parsed_steps) == len(python_steps). This does not verify that step descriptions or order values survive the round-trip correctly.

All other round-trip tests in TestRoundTripConsistency compare individual fields: constraint given/when/then/should_not, handoff source/target/phase/content_level, label value/special, role name/owned_phases. ProcedureStep is the only entity type with count-only round-trip verification.

A bug that corrupts step descriptions (e.g., truncation or wrong field in gen_schema.py XML output) would not be caught.

Remediation: Extend both test methods to compare each step's description (and optionally order) field-by-field between python_steps and parsed_steps, mirroring the pattern used in test_roundtrip_constraint_fields_match.
title: S2-B1-IMPORTANT: Round-trip procedure_steps test checks count only, not step descriptions

## Specification
Introduce frozen, JSON-serializable types for cross-workflow boundaries per PROPOSAL-11 UAT-1:

1. SerializableTransition(to_phase: PhaseId, condition: str, action: str | None = None)
2. SerializablePhaseSpec(id, number, domain, name, owner_roles: list[RoleId], transitions: list[SerializableTransition])
   - @staticmethod from_spec(PhaseSpec) → SerializablePhaseSpec
   - owner_roles: sorted list (not frozenset), transitions: list (not tuple)
3. PhaseInput(epoch_id: str, phase_spec: SerializablePhaseSpec)
4. PhaseResult(phase_id: PhaseId, success: bool, blocker_count: int = 0, vote_result: VoteType | None = None)

All fields: StrEnum, str, int, list — fully JSON-serializable via Temporal DataConverter.

## Files Owned
- scripts/aura_protocol/types.py (add new dataclasses near existing PhaseSpec)

## Integration Points
- IP-1 OWNER: SerializablePhaseSpec consumed by SLICE-3 (PhaseInput in audit activities) and SLICE-5 (tests)
- IP-2 CONSUMER: ReviewAxis enum from SLICE-2 may be used in PhaseResult

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] SerializableTransition frozen dataclass defined
- [ ] SerializablePhaseSpec frozen dataclass with list[RoleId] (not frozenset)
- [ ] from_spec static method converts PhaseSpec → SerializablePhaseSpec
- [ ] PhaseInput frozen dataclass with epoch_id + phase_spec
- [ ] PhaseResult frozen dataclass
- [ ] All fields Temporal-serializable (no frozenset, no tuple, no set, no dict)
- [ ] Tests written and passing
- [ ] Existing 945 tests unbroken
notes: Implementation complete. 68 new tests all passing. 1041 total tests pass (0 regressions). Production code: SerializableTransition, SerializablePhaseSpec (with from_spec static method), PhaseInput, PhaseResult all added to types.py and exported from __init__.py. All fields Temporal DataConverter-safe (no frozenset/tuple/set/dict). owner_roles sorted by .value.

description:
VOTE: ACCEPT — All 10 URD requirements remain addressed. B1-B5 test gaps resolved. C1-C2 elegance revisions are structurally sound. One minor observation on R2/R10 deviation (non-blocking).

## Per-Requirement Re-Assessment (R1-R10)

### R1: Figure Dataclass in types.py — PASS
Figure frozen dataclass defined with id, title, type (str), role_ref (RoleId), section_ref (str), workflow_ref (str), content (str, default ''). Field set matches URD R1. The switch from FigureType enum to plain str (per C2) is acceptable; URD R1 says "metadata fields" and does not mandate an enum.

### R2: FIGURE_SPECS Dict in types.py — ADDRESSED VIA ALTERNATIVE (PASS with note)
URD R2 specifies "FIGURE_SPECS dict keyed by figure id, containing Figure metadata entries. Parallels WORKFLOW_SPECS pattern." PROPOSAL-2 eliminates FIGURE_SPECS entirely, embedding figures directly on Workflow via `figure: Figure | None = None`. This is a deliberate structural deviation from the URD's literal wording, but it satisfies the URD's INTENT: figure metadata is still defined in types.py, keyed by workflow association, and follows the same frozen-dataclass pattern. The 1:1 workflow-figure relationship for MVP makes this a natural HAS-A. The deviation was explicitly requested by Reviewer C (Finding 2) and accepted by the architect in the revision. The URD's "parallels WORKFLOW_SPECS pattern" is satisfied in spirit — figures are specified statically in WORKFLOW_SPECS entries rather than in a parallel dict. Non-blocking.

### R3: YAML Content Loading — PASS
context_injection.py loads figure content from YAML at generation time via `_load_figure_content(figure_id, figures_dir)`. YAML remains content source of truth, types.py remains metadata source of truth. The function raises FigureLoadError on missing/malformed/empty YAML. Pipeline flow is preserved.

### R4: Inline Workflow Rendering — PASS
Template renders figures inline after workflow exit conditions as `##### title` + fenced text block. The proposed template snippet `{% if wf.figure %}` inside the workflow loop is correctly positioned after the stage/exit-condition block and before `{% endfor %}`. This is the right insertion point in skill_header.j2 (confirmed: lines 146-153 are exit conditions, line 154 is `{% endfor %}` for the workflow loop). The heading level (h5) is one below the workflow h4, which is correct hierarchy.

### R5: Keep Both section_ref and workflow_ref — PASS
Both fields present on Figure dataclass. section_ref is generic ('workflows'), workflow_ref is specific. Both retained for future extensibility. The embedding on Workflow makes section_ref somewhat redundant for MVP, but it is explicitly kept per URD R5.

### R6: Three Figures for MVP — PASS
1. layer-cake.yaml (exists, to be updated with workflow_ref)
2. ride-the-wave.yaml (exists, to be updated with workflow_ref)
3. architect-state-flow.yaml (NEW)
All three figures are embedded on their corresponding Workflow entries in WORKFLOW_SPECS.

### R7: Schema XML Generation — PASS
gen_schema.py _build_figures() iterates WORKFLOW_SPECS, emits <figure> elements for workflows with figure != None. Metadata only in XML (no content). Consistent with hybrid source-of-truth model. The iteration source changes from FIGURE_SPECS to WORKFLOW_SPECS but the output is identical.

### R8: Schema XML Parsing — PASS
schema_parser.py adds `figures: dict[str, Figure]` to SchemaSpec and _parse_figures() function. Parallel to existing parse functions. Correctly placed in parse_schema() call.

### R9: No Hand-Written Dedup (MVP) — PASS
Validation checklist item 12: "Hand-written diagrams NOT removed." BDD criteria include "should not deduplicate during MVP." Explicitly preserved.

### R10: Context Injection Extension — ADDRESSED VIA ALTERNATIVE (PASS with note)
URD R10 says "context_injection.py extends RoleContext with a figures field." PROPOSAL-2 does NOT add a separate `figures` field to RoleContext. Instead, figures flow through the existing `workflows` field since Figure is embedded on Workflow. This means `role_ctx.workflows[0].figure` replaces `role_ctx.figures[0]`. The intent of R10 (figures are loaded from YAML at context injection time and available in role context) is fully satisfied. RoleContext does not need a new field because the data is already accessible via the existing workflows field. This is structurally cleaner — no parallel tuple to maintain.

## B1-B5 Test Gap Resolution

### B1: context_injection.py coverage — RESOLVED
PROPOSAL-2 specifies 4 figure-presence tests in test_context_injection.py:
- test_supervisor_workflow_has_figure (workflow[0].figure not None, content non-empty)
- test_worker_workflow_has_figure
- test_architect_workflow_has_figure
- test_reviewer_has_no_workflows (hence no figures)

These directly address Gap-1 from Reviewer B. The tests verify the YAML loading path (R3) and context population (R10) end-to-end.

### B2: YAML error-path tests — RESOLVED
4 error-path tests specified:
- Missing YAML file -> FigureLoadError
- Malformed YAML -> FigureLoadError
- Missing content key -> FigureLoadError
- Empty content -> FigureLoadError

Matches Gap-2 from Reviewer B. Follows the existing SchemaParseError pattern in test_schema_parser.py.

### B3: workflow_ref validity sync test — RESOLVED
Test: `Workflow.figure.workflow_ref == Workflow.id` for all workflows with figures.
Since figures are now embedded ON the workflow, this is a structural invariant — the figure's workflow_ref must match its parent Workflow's id. This is actually STRONGER than the original Gap-3 (which only checked that workflow_ref pointed to a valid key in WORKFLOW_SPECS). Now the test verifies identity, not just existence.

### B4: Positional assertion for inline rendering — RESOLVED
3 positional tests specified:
- test_supervisor_figure_after_workflow: rendered.index(exit_condition_text) < rendered.index(figure_title) < rendered.index('END GENERATED')
- test_worker_figure_after_workflow
- test_architect_figure_after_workflow

Directly addresses Gap-4. The triple-index assertion is a correct positional check.

### B5: Figure type attribute sync test — RESOLVED
Test: `<figure type='...'>` values in schema.xml match Figure.type strings in WORKFLOW_SPECS.
Plus test_all_schema_figures_in_python and test_figure_count_matches for bidirectional sync.
Addresses Gap-5. The plain string type (no enum) means the sync test compares string-to-string rather than enum-to-schema, which is simpler but equally correct.

## C1-C2 Elegance Revision Assessment

### C1: Embed figure on Workflow — CORRECTLY APPLIED
Workflow dataclass gains `figure: Figure | None = None`. WORKFLOW_SPECS entries have `figure=Figure(...)` for the 3 MVP figures. No separate FIGURE_SPECS dict. No figure_by_workflow lookup dict in gen_skills.py. Template uses `{% if wf.figure %}`. context_injection populates figure.content when building workflows. This is the exact change Reviewer C requested: the HAS-A relationship is expressed in the type system.

### C2: Drop FigureType enum — CORRECTLY APPLIED
Figure.type is plain `str` (e.g., 'ascii-diagram'). No FigureType enum defined. YAGNI applied correctly: promote to enum when second type arrives. The sync test (B5) still verifies schema.xml type attributes match the string values.

## Internal Consistency Check

### Frozen Dataclass Constraint
Workflow is a frozen dataclass. Adding `figure: Figure | None = None` as a default field at the end is valid because it has a default value and all preceding fields are positional. Figure itself is frozen. Immutability chain is preserved: Workflow.figure returns an immutable Figure.

### context_injection Data Flow
get_role_context() filters WORKFLOW_SPECS by role_ref (existing pattern, line 466-470). The proposal extends this to: for each filtered workflow, if workflow.figure is not None, load YAML content and return a new Workflow with figure.content populated. Since both Workflow and Figure are frozen, this requires constructing new instances (e.g., via dataclasses.replace). The proposal states "return a new Workflow with figure.content populated" which implies replace(). This is consistent with how frozen dataclasses are modified elsewhere in the codebase.

### Template Data Flow
gen_skills.py passes `role_ctx.workflows` to the template as `workflows`. The template iterates `{% for wf in workflows %}` (line 134). Adding `{% if wf.figure %}` after exit conditions (line 153) and before `{% endfor %}` (line 154) is correct insertion point. No changes needed to gen_skills.py's context construction — zero new plumbing, as claimed.

### Schema Round-Trip
gen_schema.py iterates WORKFLOW_SPECS, emits <figure> for workflows with figure != None. schema_parser.py parses <figure> elements into SchemaSpec.figures dict. The round-trip is: types.py -> gen_schema.py -> schema.xml -> schema_parser.py -> SchemaSpec.figures. This parallels workflows, checklists, coordination_commands.

## Minor Observations (non-blocking)

1. **dataclasses.replace chain**: Populating figure.content requires two replace() calls: one for Figure (to set content) and one for Workflow (to set the new figure). The proposal does not spell out this detail explicitly, but it is the only correct approach for frozen dataclasses. Implementation should handle this.

2. **section_ref redundancy**: With figures embedded on Workflow, section_ref is always 'workflows' and is derivable from context. The field is kept per URD R5, which is fine, but it will carry no discriminating information for MVP. If non-workflow figures arrive later, this field becomes meaningful again.

3. **Figure.id == Workflow.id**: In WORKFLOW_SPECS, each Figure's id matches its parent Workflow's id (e.g., Figure(id='ride-the-wave') on Workflow(id='ride-the-wave')). This is a design choice — the figure shares identity with its workflow. The B3 test checks this invariant. If figures ever need independent identity (e.g., multiple figures per workflow), this would need to change. Acceptable for MVP.

4. **YAML workflow_ref field**: The proposal adds workflow_ref to existing YAML files (layer-cake.yaml, ride-the-wave.yaml). Currently these YAMLs have no workflow_ref field. The YAML workflow_ref would need to match the Figure.workflow_ref in types.py, but the proposal does not specify whether _load_figure_content reads and validates workflow_ref from YAML or ignores it. Since metadata lives in types.py and YAML is only content source, the YAML workflow_ref is informational/redundant. Implementation should clarify whether it is validated or ignored.
title: PROPOSAL-2-REVIEW-A-2: Figures pipeline correctness (Round 2)

description:
Axis-C finding (Elegance/Code Organization).

In gen_schema.py _serialize_tree() (line 1589), 'import io' is written inline inside the function body rather than at the module's top-level import block. This is against standard Python style and makes it harder to see the module's dependencies at a glance.

io is a stdlib module that imposes no cost at import time. There is no valid reason for the deferred import here (it's not optional, not slow, not a circular import).

Fix: Move 'import io' to the top of the file alongside the other stdlib imports (difflib, xml.etree.ElementTree, pathlib).
title: SLICE-2-REVIEW-C-1-F7: gen_schema.py _serialize_tree uses deferred import of io module

## Specification
Manage epoch lifecycle state and validate transitions against the protocol. Pure Python — no Temporal dependency.

### Key Types
- EpochState (mutable runtime state)
- TransitionRecord (frozen, immutable audit entry)
- TransitionError (exception with violations list)
- EpochStateMachine (main class)

### Key Behaviors
- advance(to_phase) — validates and executes transitions
- validate_advance(to_phase) — dry-run validation
- record_vote(axis, vote) — record reviewer votes
- has_consensus() — check 3/3 ACCEPT
- record_blocker(resolved) — track BLOCKER count
- available_transitions property

### Transition Rules (from schema.xml)
- Sequential forward: p1→p2→p3→p4→p5→p6→p7→p8→p9→p10→p11→p12→complete
- Review revision loops: p4→p3 (REVISE), p10→p9 (BLOCKER fix)
- Consensus gate: p4→p5 requires 3 ACCEPT, p5→p6 requires UAT pass
- BLOCKER gate: p10→p11 requires blocker_count == 0

## Files Owned
- scripts/aura_protocol/state_machine.py (NEW)
- tests/test_state_machine.py (NEW)

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC1: Given epoch in P1 when advance(p2) then transitions, records transition should not allow p1→p8
- AC2: Given epoch in P4 with 2/3 ACCEPT when advance(p5) then TransitionError should not proceed without consensus
- AC3: Given epoch in P4 with REVISE when checking transitions then only p3 available
- AC4: Given epoch in P10 with blockers when advance(p11) then TransitionError should not proceed to UAT

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] EpochState dataclass defined
- [ ] TransitionRecord frozen dataclass
- [ ] TransitionError with violations list
- [ ] EpochStateMachine class with all methods
- [ ] Tests cover AC1-AC4
- [ ] Transition table covers all schema.xml transitions

## Problem Space

The pasture Go codebase has 329 tests across 31 files, all using manual if/error assertions with no YAML fixture infrastructure. Four coverage gaps exist: CLI binaries at 0% coverage, RunAgentSession with only a connect-error test, config YAML file loading never exercised end-to-end, and ContentBlock dual Content/Text fields ambiguous and untested.

**Axes:**
- **Parallelism:** Test cases are independent; Go subtests run in parallel naturally via t.Parallel()
- **Distribution:** Per-package testdata/ directories (D1), not centralized
- **Has-a:** Fixture loader HAS test case structs; test files HAVE testdata/ YAML files
- **Is-a:** Each coverage gap IS a separate test file with its own YAML schema

## Engineering Tradeoffs

| Decision | Option Chosen | Rationale |
|----------|--------------|-----------|
| Fixture location | Per-package testdata/ | Go-idiomatic, go test recognizes testdata/ (D1) |
| Assertion library | testify/assert | Already in go.mod v1.10.0, more readable (D2) |
| Fixture style | Full table-driven (inputs+expected) | YAML defines everything, Go just runs it (D3) |
| Dependencies | None new | testify + yaml.v3 both already available (D4) |
| Loader location | internal/testutil/fixtures.go | Shared across all packages |

## Public Interfaces

### Fixture Loader (internal/testutil/fixtures.go)

\`\`\`go
package testutil

import (
    "os"
    "path/filepath"
    "testing"

    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    "gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
)

// LoadFixtures reads a YAML fixture file from testdata/{name}.yaml relative
// to the caller's package directory, and unmarshals it into target.
//
// Fails the test immediately (via require) if the file cannot be read or
// parsed — fixture loading errors are infrastructure failures, not test
// failures.
//
// Usage:
//   var cases MyFixtureStruct
//   testutil.LoadFixtures(t, "cli_smoke", &cases)
func LoadFixtures(t *testing.T, name string, target any) {
    t.Helper()
    // testdata path is relative to the test file's directory
    path := filepath.Join("testdata", name+".yaml")
    data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
    require.NoError(t, err, "fixture file %s", path)
    require.NoError(t, yaml.Unmarshal(data, target), "unmarshal %s", path)
}

### CLI Smoke Test Implementation

**File:** cmd/pasture-msg/main_test.go

\`\`\`go
package main

import (
    "bytes"
    "os/exec"
    "testing"

    "github.com/dayvidpham/pasture/internal/testutil"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type CLISmokeCase struct {
    ID                 string   \`yaml:"id"\`
    Args               []string \`yaml:"args"\`
    WantExit           int      \`yaml:"want_exit"\`
    WantStdoutContains string   \`yaml:"want_stdout_contains"\`
    WantStderrContains string   \`yaml:"want_stderr_contains"\`
}

type CLISmokeFixtures struct {
    Tests []CLISmokeCase \`yaml:"tests"\`
}

func TestCLISmoke(t *testing.T) {
    // Build the binary once for all subtests.
    binPath := buildBinary(t, "pasture-msg")

    var fixtures CLISmokeFixtures
    testutil.LoadFixtures(t, "cli_smoke", &fixtures)
    require.NotEmpty(t, fixtures.Tests, "no test cases loaded")

    for _, tc := range fixtures.Tests {
        t.Run(tc.ID, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Parallel()
            cmd := exec.Command(binPath, tc.Args...)
            var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
            cmd.Stdout = &stdout
            cmd.Stderr = &stderr

            err := cmd.Run()

            // Check exit code.
            exitCode := 0
            if err != nil {
                if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
                    exitCode = exitErr.ExitCode()
                } else {
                    t.Fatalf("unexpected exec error: %v", err)
                }
            }
            assert.Equal(t, tc.WantExit, exitCode, "exit code")

            if tc.WantStdoutContains != "" {
                assert.Contains(t, stdout.String(), tc.WantStdoutContains, "stdout")
            }
            if tc.WantStderrContains != "" {
                assert.Contains(t, stderr.String(), tc.WantStderrContains, "stderr")
            }
        })
    }
}

// buildBinary compiles the binary in a temp dir and returns its path.
func buildBinary(t *testing.T, name string) string {
    t.Helper()
    tmpDir := t.TempDir()
    binPath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, name)
    cmd := exec.Command("go", "build", "-o", binPath, ".")
    cmd.Dir = "."  // current package directory
    out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    require.NoError(t, err, "go build: %s", out)
    return binPath
}
\`\`\`

Same pattern applies to cmd/pasture-release/main_test.go with its own testdata/cli_smoke.yaml. The pasture-release test uses newRootCmd().Execute() approach since it exports newRootCmd(), avoiding binary compilation entirely.

**File:** internal/temporal/run_agent_session_test.go

\`\`\`go
package temporal_test

import (
    "context"
    "testing"
    "time"

    "github.com/dayvidpham/pasture/internal/audit"
    "github.com/dayvidpham/pasture/internal/temporal"
    "github.com/dayvidpham/pasture/internal/testutil"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    "go.temporal.io/sdk/testsuite"
)

type RunAgentSessionCase struct {
    ID            string              \`yaml:"id"\`
    Description   string              \`yaml:"description"\`
    AgentUpdates  []AgentUpdateInput  \`yaml:"agent_updates"\`
    AgentHangs    bool                \`yaml:"agent_hangs"\`
    CancelAfterMs int                 \`yaml:"cancel_after_ms"\`
    AgentCmd      string              \`yaml:"agent_cmd"\`
    Want          RunAgentSessionWant \`yaml:"want"\`
}

type AgentUpdateInput struct {
    SessionID  string         \`yaml:"session_id"\`
    Role       string         \`yaml:"role"\`
    Content    []ContentInput \`yaml:"content"\`
    StopReason string         \`yaml:"stop_reason"\`
}

type ContentInput struct {
    Type string \`yaml:"type"\`
    Text string \`yaml:"text"\`
}

type RunAgentSessionWant struct {
    EntriesRecordedMin int    \`yaml:"entries_recorded_min"\`
    SessionID          string \`yaml:"session_id"\`
    StopReason         string \`yaml:"stop_reason"\`
    Error              bool   \`yaml:"error"\`
}

type RunAgentSessionFixtures struct {
    Tests []RunAgentSessionCase \`yaml:"tests"\`
}

func TestRunAgentSession_YAML(t *testing.T) {
    t.Parallel()

    var fixtures RunAgentSessionFixtures
    testutil.LoadFixtures(t, "run_agent_session", &fixtures)
    require.NotEmpty(t, fixtures.Tests)

    binPath := buildFakeAgent(t) // reuses existing helper

    for _, tc := range fixtures.Tests {
        t.Run(tc.ID, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Parallel()
            trail := audit.NewInMemoryAuditTrail()
            acts := newActivities(trail)

            suite := &testsuite.WorkflowTestSuite{}
            env := suite.NewTestActivityEnvironment()
            env.RegisterActivity(acts)

            agentCmd := binPath
            if tc.AgentCmd != "" {
                agentCmd = tc.AgentCmd
            }

            input := temporal.RunAgentSessionInput{
                AgentCmd:  agentCmd,
                AgentArgs: buildFakeAgentArgs(t, tc.AgentUpdates),
                EpochID:   "epoch-" + tc.ID,
            }

            if tc.AgentHangs {
                // For hang tests, use context with cancellation
                // (Temporal test env handles this differently)
                input.AgentCmd = binPath
                input.AgentArgs = []string{} // no output = hangs
            }

            val, err := env.ExecuteActivity(acts.RunAgentSession, input)

            if tc.Want.Error {
                assert.Error(t, err, "expected error")
                return
            }
            require.NoError(t, err)

            var result temporal.RunAgentSessionResult
            require.NoError(t, val.Get(&result))

            assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, result.EntriesRecorded, tc.Want.EntriesRecordedMin)
            if tc.Want.SessionID != "" {
                assert.Equal(t, tc.Want.SessionID, result.SessionID)
            }
            if tc.Want.StopReason != "" {
                assert.Equal(t, tc.Want.StopReason, result.StopReason)
            }
        })
    }
}

package config_test

import (
    "os"
    "path/filepath"
    "testing"

    "github.com/dayvidpham/pasture/internal/config"
    "github.com/dayvidpham/pasture/internal/testutil"
    "github.com/dayvidpham/pasture/internal/types"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type ConfigLoadingCase struct {
    ID             string            \`yaml:"id"\`
    Description    string            \`yaml:"description"\`
    YAMLContent    string            \`yaml:"yaml_content"\`
    UseMissingPath bool              \`yaml:"use_missing_path"\`
    WantPastured   *PasturedWant     \`yaml:"want_pastured"\`
    WantPastureMsg *PastureMsgWant   \`yaml:"want_pasture_msg"\`
    WantError      bool              \`yaml:"want_error"\`
    ErrorContains  string            \`yaml:"error_contains"\`
}

type PasturedWant struct {
    Namespace     string \`yaml:"namespace"\`
    TaskQueue     string \`yaml:"task_queue"\`
    ServerAddress string \`yaml:"server_address"\`
    AuditTrail    string \`yaml:"audit_trail"\`
}

type PastureMsgWant struct {
    Namespace     string \`yaml:"namespace"\`
    TaskQueue     string \`yaml:"task_queue"\`
    ServerAddress string \`yaml:"server_address"\`
    DefaultFormat string \`yaml:"default_format"\`
}

type ConfigLoadingFixtures struct {
    Tests []ConfigLoadingCase \`yaml:"tests"\`
}

func TestConfigYAMLLoading(t *testing.T) {
    var fixtures ConfigLoadingFixtures
    testutil.LoadFixtures(t, "config_loading", &fixtures)
    require.NotEmpty(t, fixtures.Tests)

    for _, tc := range fixtures.Tests {
        t.Run(tc.ID, func(t *testing.T) {
            clearAllEnvVars(t) // ensure clean slate

            var cfgPath string
            if tc.UseMissingPath {
                cfgPath = filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "nonexistent.yaml")
            } else {
                dir := t.TempDir()
                cfgPath = filepath.Join(dir, "config.yaml")
                require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(cfgPath, []byte(tc.YAMLContent), 0o644))
            }

            cmd := newPasturedTestCmd()
            cfg := config.ResolvePasturedConfigFromFile(cmd, cfgPath)

            // Note: ResolvePasturedConfigFromFile does not return errors;
            // malformed YAML falls through to defaults. Test accordingly.
            if tc.WantPastured != nil {
                assert.Equal(t, tc.WantPastured.Namespace, cfg.Connection.Namespace)
                assert.Equal(t, tc.WantPastured.TaskQueue, cfg.Connection.TaskQueue)
                assert.Equal(t, tc.WantPastured.ServerAddress, cfg.Connection.ServerAddress)
                assert.Equal(t, types.AuditBackend(tc.WantPastured.AuditTrail), cfg.AuditTrail)
            }
        })
    }
}

package acp_test

import (
    "testing"

    "github.com/dayvidpham/pasture/internal/acp"
    "github.com/dayvidpham/pasture/internal/testutil"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type ContentBlockCase struct {
    ID         string        \`yaml:"id"\`
    Description string       \`yaml:"description"\`
    Block      ContentInput  \`yaml:"block"\`
    WantText   string        \`yaml:"want_text"\`
    WantSource string        \`yaml:"want_source"\`
}

type ContentInput struct {
    Type    string \`yaml:"type"\`
    Content string \`yaml:"content"\`
    Text    string \`yaml:"text"\`
}

type ContentBlockFixtures struct {
    Tests []ContentBlockCase \`yaml:"tests"\`
}

func TestContentBlockDualFields(t *testing.T) {
    t.Parallel()

    var fixtures ContentBlockFixtures
    testutil.LoadFixtures(t, "content_block", &fixtures)
    require.NotEmpty(t, fixtures.Tests)

    for _, tc := range fixtures.Tests {
        t.Run(tc.ID, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Parallel()
            block := acp.ContentBlock{
                Type:    tc.Block.Type,
                Content: tc.Block.Content,
                Text:    tc.Block.Text,
            }

            // Test the indexer's resolution logic: Content wins over Text
            update := acp.SessionUpdate{
                SessionID: "test-session",
                Role:      "assistant",
                Content:   []acp.ContentBlock{block},
            }

            indexer := acp.NewSharedIndexer()
            entries := indexer.Index(update)

            if tc.WantText == "" {
                // For non-text blocks or empty text, preview may be nil
                if len(entries) > 0 && entries[0].ContentPreview != nil {
                    assert.Empty(t, *entries[0].ContentPreview,
                        "expected empty preview for %s", tc.ID)
                }
            } else {
                require.NotEmpty(t, entries, "expected indexed entries")
                require.NotNil(t, entries[0].ContentPreview,
                    "expected non-nil ContentPreview for %s", tc.ID)
                assert.Contains(t, *entries[0].ContentPreview, tc.WantText,
                    "content preview should contain expected text")
            }
        })
    }
}

## Implementation Slices

| Slice | Wave | Scope | Files Created/Modified | Tests Added |
|-------|------|-------|----------------------|-------------|
| S1 | 1 | Fixture loader infrastructure | internal/testutil/fixtures.go, internal/testutil/fixtures_test.go | 3 (load, missing file, malformed) |
| S2 | 2 | CLI smoke tests (pasture-msg) | cmd/pasture-msg/main_test.go, cmd/pasture-msg/testdata/cli_smoke.yaml | 8 subtests |
| S3 | 2 | CLI smoke tests (pasture-release) | cmd/pasture-release/main_test.go, cmd/pasture-release/testdata/cli_smoke.yaml | 6 subtests |
| S4 | 2 | RunAgentSession full lifecycle | internal/temporal/run_agent_session_test.go, internal/temporal/testdata/run_agent_session.yaml | 4 subtests |
| S5 | 2 | Config YAML file loading | internal/config/config_yaml_test.go, internal/config/testdata/config_loading.yaml | 5 subtests |
| S6 | 2 | ContentBlock dual fields | internal/acp/content_block_test.go, internal/acp/testdata/content_block.yaml | 6 subtests |

**Wave 1** (S1): Foundation. S2-S6 all depend on the fixture loader.
**Wave 2** (S2-S6): All independent, can be parallelized.

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] internal/testutil/fixtures.go compiles and is importable from all test packages
- [ ] LoadFixtures fails tests on missing/malformed YAML (not silently)
- [ ] testify/assert and testify/require used consistently in all new tests
- [ ] All new tests use t.Run() for subtests with YAML-driven IDs
- [ ] All new tests use t.Parallel() where safe
- [ ] cmd/pasture-msg/testdata/cli_smoke.yaml covers --help, --version, unknown subcommand, each subcommand help
- [ ] cmd/pasture-release/testdata/cli_smoke.yaml covers --help, unknown subcommand, each subcommand help
- [ ] RunAgentSession tests cover: happy path, context cancellation, multi-update, connect error
- [ ] Config tests verify CLI > env > YAML > defaults resolution with YAML file loading
- [ ] ContentBlock tests document that Content is canonical, Text is live-wire fallback
- [ ] All 329 existing tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] go vet ./... passes with no warnings
- [ ] No new external dependencies added

## BDD Acceptance Criteria

**AC1: Fixture Loader**
- Given a testdata/example.yaml file exists in the calling package
- When LoadFixtures(t, "example", &target) is called
- Then target is populated with the unmarshalled YAML data
- Should not silently ignore missing or malformed YAML files

**AC2: CLI Smoke Tests**
- Given pasture-msg binary is compiled via go build
- When executed with --help
- Then exit code is 0 and stdout contains "pasture-msg"
- Should not require Temporal server running

**AC3: CLI Smoke Tests (pasture-release)**
- Given pasture-release binary is compiled via go build
- When executed with an unknown subcommand
- Then exit code is non-zero and stderr contains an error message
- Should not require git repository or version files

**AC4: RunAgentSession Happy Path**
- Given a fake agent binary that emits session/update JSON-RPC lines
- When RunAgentSession is executed via Temporal test environment
- Then result.EntriesRecorded >= 1, result.SessionID matches, result.StopReason matches
- Should not require a real Temporal cluster

**AC5: Config YAML File Loading**
- Given a temp YAML config file with connection.namespace set to "yaml-ns"
- When ResolvePasturedConfigFromFile is called with no CLI flags or env vars
- Then cfg.Connection.Namespace equals "yaml-ns"
- Should not mutate global state or leak env vars between tests

**AC6: ContentBlock Dual Fields**
- Given a ContentBlock with both Content="canonical" and Text="wire"
- When indexed by SharedIndexer
- Then the ContentPreview contains "canonical" (Content wins)
- Should not use Text when Content is non-empty

## Components Demonstrated

### Component: SKILL.md file structure (heading placement)

**What was shown:**

BEFORE (TUI showed comment as description):

    <!-- BEGIN GENERATED FROM aura schema -->   <- line 1, TUI read this
    # Worker Agent                               <- line 2, never reached
    **Role:** `worker` | **Phases owned:** p9-worker-slices
    ...
    <!-- END GENERATED FROM aura schema -->

AFTER (TUI shows heading as description):

    # Worker Agent                               <- line 1, TUI reads "Worker Agent"

    <!-- BEGIN GENERATED FROM aura schema -->   <- line 3
    **Role:** `worker` | **Phases owned:** p9-worker-slices
    ...
    <!-- END GENERATED FROM aura schema -->

**Consequence explained:** The `# {Role} Agent` heading is now hand-authored content (lives in the prefix, never regenerated). If a role's display name changed in types.py, the SKILL.md heading would need a manual update.

**Question asked:** "The heading ('# Worker Agent', '# Supervisor Agent', etc.) is now hand-authored content that lives before the BEGIN marker. This means: (1) the generator never overwrites it, (2) if a role's name changes in types.py, the SKILL.md heading won't auto-update. Which tradeoff is acceptable?"

**Options presented:**
- Hand-authored is fine (current) — Role names are stable; a one-time manual update if a name ever changes is acceptable. Generator stays simpler.
- Generator should own it — Move the heading inside the markers so it regenerates automatically.

**User response (verbatim):** "Generator should own it"

**Follow-up user message (verbatim):** "Okay, it's fine then. Doesn't matter: let's just have the weird header. Isn't there some kind of frontmatter we could write that the Claude Code TUI uses?"

## Final Decision
ACCEPT — current heading-before-BEGIN implementation is accepted as-is.

## Follow-up
Investigate whether SKILL.md `description` frontmatter field controls TUI autocomplete display (tracked separately).

description:
---
references:
  request: aura-plugins-oc1tm
---

## Summary

Port the Python codegen tooling (gen_skills.py, gen_schema.py, gen_agents.py, context_injection.py) to Go, using the templ library for template rendering. The Go types already exist in pkg/protocol/ and internal/types/ — this builds the generation layer on top.

## Scope

### In Scope
1. **context_injection.go** — Role/phase context builder (port of context_injection.py)
2. **gen_schema.go** — schema.xml generation from Go types (port of gen_schema.py)
3. **gen_skills.go** — SKILL.md header generation with BEGIN/END markers (port of gen_skills.py)
4. **gen_agents.go** — Agent definition markdown generation (port of gen_agents.py)
5. **Makefile generate target** — orchestrate all codegen
6. **Tests** — round-trip validation, output parity checks

### Out of Scope
- gen_types.go (one-time bootstrap tool — Go types already hand-written)
- schema_parser.go (schema.xml → types direction — not needed since Go types are canonical)
- Modifying existing Go types in pkg/protocol/ or internal/types/

## Architecture

### Data Flow (same as Python)
```
Go types (canonical)
    ├─→ gen_schema.go ──→ schema.xml
    ├─→ gen_skills.go ──→ skills/{role}/SKILL.md headers
    │       ↑ context_injection.go
    └─→ gen_agents.go ──→ agents/{role}.md
            ↑ context_injection.go
```

### Package Structure
```
pasture/
  internal/
    codegen/
      context.go        — RoleContext, PhaseContext builders
      context_test.go
      schema.go         — schema.xml generation (encoding/xml)
      schema_test.go
      skills.go         — SKILL.md generation with BEGIN/END markers
      skills_test.go
      agents.go         — agent definition generation
      agents_test.go
      markers.go        — BEGIN/END marker text surgery
      markers_test.go
      templates/        — templ template files (.templ)
        skill_header.templ
        skill_sub_figure.templ
        agent_definition.templ
  cmd/
    pasture-codegen/    — CLI entry point
      main.go           — cobra commands: gen-schema, gen-skills, gen-agents, gen-all
```

### Key Design Decisions

**D1: templ for templates** — User-specified. templ compiles templates to type-safe Go code, catching template errors at compile time rather than runtime. Templates produce text output (not just HTML).

**D2: encoding/xml for schema.xml** — gen_schema builds XML programmatically (same as Python's ElementTree approach). No templates needed for XML — struct marshalling + custom builders.

**D3: internal/codegen package** — All codegen lives in one internal package with clear file boundaries. Not exported — only consumed by cmd/pasture-codegen.

**D4: Same canonical lookup dicts pattern** — Port ROLE_SPECS, COMMAND_SPECS, CONSTRAINT_SPECS etc. as Go maps with typed keys. These already partially exist in Go types; codegen adds the context-injection layer.

**D5: BEGIN/END marker strategy** — Same as Python: validate markers exist, replace between them, preserve hand-authored content below END marker. MarkerError type for strict validation.

**D6: Constraint mapping inversion** — Same as Python: hand-authored role→constraints and phase→constraints maps, inverted at init time for schema generation.

## Vertical Slices

| Slice | Wave | Scope | Files |
|-------|------|-------|-------|
| S1 | 1 | Canonical spec dicts + context injection | internal/codegen/context.go, context_test.go |
| S2 | 1 | BEGIN/END marker text surgery | internal/codegen/markers.go, markers_test.go |
| S3 | 2 | schema.xml generation | internal/codegen/schema.go, schema_test.go |
| S4 | 2 | SKILL.md generation (templ templates) | internal/codegen/skills.go, skills_test.go, templates/*.templ |
| S5 | 2 | Agent definition generation | internal/codegen/agents.go, agents_test.go |
| S6 | 3 | CLI entry point + Makefile | cmd/pasture-codegen/main.go, Makefile |

Wave 1 (S1, S2) — foundation types and utilities
Wave 2 (S3, S4, S5) — the 3 generators (parallel, independent)
Wave 3 (S6) — CLI wiring + build integration

## Acceptance Criteria

AC1: Given Go types exist, when running gen-schema, then output schema.xml matches Python-generated schema.xml structure
AC2: Given a SKILL.md with BEGIN/END markers, when running gen-skills, then generated header replaces marker region and hand-authored body is preserved
AC3: Given a role with tools defined, when running gen-agents, then agents/{role}.md is generated with constraints, phases, and checklists
AC4: Given no SKILL.md exists, when running gen-skills --init, then markers are prepended to create initial file
AC5: Given make generate is run, then all 3 generators execute and output is up-to-date

## Open Questions

Q1: templ library generates HTML by default — need to verify it supports plain text/markdown output mode, or if we need text/template as fallback for non-HTML output
Q2: Should the Go codegen consume the same schema.xml as input (like gen_types.py), or should Go types remain the canonical source and schema.xml be purely output?
title: PROPOSAL-1: Port codegen (gen_skills, gen_schema, gen_agents) to Go pasture

## Problem Space

**Axes:** This is a cross-cutting documentation/protocol refactoring touching 10+ files. No parallelism — changes are sequential and must be consistent across files. The schema.xml is the canonical source; markdown files are derived.

**Has-a relationships:** The supervisor role HAS constraints, behaviors, invariants, standing-teams, workflows. The Ride the Wave workflow HAS stages (plan, build, review). The review cycle HAS exit conditions.

**Is-a relationships:** The supervisor IS an agent definition (agents/supervisor.md). Explore subagents ARE ephemeral (not standing). Reviewers ARE ephemeral subagents (same as Phase 4).

## Engineering Tradeoffs

| Decision | Option A | Option B | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|----------|----------|--------|-----------|
| T1: Exploration model | Standing Cartographers (3 persistent agents) | Ephemeral Explore subagents per query | B: Ephemeral | Supervisor stays lean; no standing team overhead; simpler lifecycle (URD D1) |
| T2: Review model | Cartographer role-switch to reviewer | Ephemeral reviewer subagents per round | B: Ephemeral | Consistent with Phase 4 pattern; no persistence between rounds (URD D2) |
| T3: Worker spawning | Always TeamCreate teammates | Default subagents, TeamCreate for high-integration | B: Default subagents | Pragmatic — most slices don't need cross-worker coordination (URD D3) |
| T4: Supervisor tool enforcement | Narrative-only constraint | Agent definition with tools field | B: Agent definition | Structural enforcement via agents/supervisor.md (URD R2) |
| T5: Clean review definition | No BLOCKERs only | No BLOCKERs + no IMPORTANTs | B: Strict | Matches user requirement — MINOR ok, IMPORTANT not ok (URD D5, R3) |
| T6: Autonomous progression | All phases ask user | 5 user-gated phases only | B: 5 gates | research depth, URE, Plan UAT, Ratification, Impl UAT (URD D6, R5) |

## Changes by File

### SLICE-1: agents/supervisor.md (CREATE)
New agent definition file. Follows agents/tester.md pattern.
- Frontmatter: name, description, tools, model
- Tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Skill, Agent, Task
- EXCLUDED: Edit, Write, NotebookEdit
- Body: role narrative, constraints, key behaviors

### SLICE-2: skills/protocol/schema.xml
- Remove C-supervisor-cartographers constraint (line 909)
- Replace with new C-supervisor-explore-ephemeral constraint
- Remove standing-teams section (lines 458-470)
- Update supervisor invariant "NEVER explores codebase directly — delegates to standing explore team" → "delegates to ephemeral Explore subagents"
- Update B-sup-cartographer-reuse → B-sup-explore-ephemeral
- Update B-sup-ride-the-wave to remove Cartographer references
- Update ownership-narrative (line 473) to remove "Cartographer/reviewers"
- Update h1 skill-invocation note (line 818) to remove "explore team setup"
- Update C-slice-review-before-close (line 911) to remove "by Cartographers"
- Update C-max-review-cycles (line 912) to remove "Cartographer" and add clean review exit
- Update P10 transition condition (line 337) to include "no IMPORTANTs"
- Add new C-clean-review-exit constraint
- Add new C-autonomous-progression constraint
- Add tools element to supervisor role definition
- Update cmd-explore note (line 788) to remove "standing explore team"

### SLICE-3: skills/supervisor/SKILL.md
- Remove entire "Cartographers (Standing Explore + Review Team)" section (~135 lines, 409-545)
- Remove Cartographer references from "Ride the Wave" section (271-339)
- Rewrite Ride the Wave stages:
  - Stage 1: Plan — supervisor uses ephemeral Explore subagents (not Cartographers)
  - Stage 2: Build — workers as subagents by default, TeamCreate for high-integration
  - Stage 3: Review — ephemeral reviewer subagents per round (not Cartographer role-switch)
- Update exit conditions for clean review (no BLOCKERs + no IMPORTANTs)
- Remove C-supervisor-cartographers constraint, replace with C-supervisor-explore-ephemeral
- Remove B-sup-cartographer-reuse, replace with B-sup-explore-ephemeral
- Update B-sup-ride-the-wave
- Update startup sequence (Step 3: remove "Create standing explore team")
- Update completion checklist ("All 3 Cartographers" → "All 3 reviewers")
- Update "What You Own" narrative
- Add autonomous progression behavior
- Add worker subagent vs teammate decision criteria
- Rewrite ASCII diagram for Ride the Wave

### SLICE-4: skills/protocol/PROCESS.md
- Remove "Cartographers (Standing Explore + Review Team)" section (lines 416-423)
- Rewrite Phase 8 startup sequence (line 399): remove "Create 3 Cartographers"
- Rewrite Phase 10 overview (line 643): remove Cartographer role-switch
- Update review-fix cycle procedure (lines 650-655): reviewers are ephemeral subagents
- Update exit conditions table (lines 659-665): add "no IMPORTANTs" to clean exit
- Rewrite "Ride the Wave — Worker Persistence" (lines 615-635): remove Cartographer refs
- Update slice closure rules (lines 630-635): remove "by Cartographers"
- Update Tools & Capabilities Matrix (lines 1160-1170): reference supervisor agent def
- Add autonomous progression documentation

### SLICE-5: skills/protocol/AGENTS.md
- Update Supervisor section to remove Cartographer references
- Reference agents/supervisor.md for tool restrictions
- Update key constraints list

### SLICE-6: skills/protocol/CLAUDE.md
- Update "Given you are a supervisor when codebase exploration is needed" constraint
- Remove "standing explore team" language, replace with "ephemeral Explore subagents"
- Remove "Minimum 1 standing explore agent" requirement
- Add autonomous progression constraint

### SLICE-7: skills/protocol/figures/ride-the-wave.yaml
- Rewrite ASCII diagram: remove all Cartographer references
- Update Phase 8/9/10 descriptions
- Update exit conditions

### SLICE-8: skills/epoch/SKILL.md
- Update "RIDE THE WAVE" core principle (line 23)
- Update Phase 8 description (line 37): remove "3 Cartographers explore"
- Update Phase 10 description (line 39): remove "Cartographers review"
- Update Given/When/Then for Phase 8 (line 70): remove "3 Cartographers created via TeamCreate"
- Add autonomous progression principle

### SLICE-9: skills/protocol/CONSTRAINTS.md
- No Cartographer references to remove (constraints are in schema.xml)
- Add clean review exit definition to Issue Severity section if needed

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] No remaining references to "Cartographer" or "cartographer" in any file
- [ ] No remaining references to "standing explore team" or "dual-role"
- [ ] agents/supervisor.md exists with correct tool restrictions
- [ ] schema.xml C-supervisor-cartographers replaced
- [ ] schema.xml standing-teams section removed
- [ ] Ride the Wave rewritten in supervisor/SKILL.md, PROCESS.md, ride-the-wave.yaml
- [ ] Clean review exit (no BLOCKERs + no IMPORTANTs) documented in all relevant locations
- [ ] Autonomous progression rules documented in epoch, supervisor, and architect skills
- [ ] P10 transition condition updated in schema.xml
- [ ] All files self-consistent (no contradictions between schema.xml and markdown)

## BDD Acceptance Criteria

**Given** a supervisor agent is launched **when** it attempts to use Edit, Write, or NotebookEdit **then** the tools are not available (restricted by agent definition) **should never** have file-modification tools available

**Given** supervisor needs codebase context **when** exploring **then** spawns ephemeral Explore subagent (Agent tool, subagent_type=Explore) that returns findings and exits **should never** create standing teams or TeamCreate for exploration

**Given** supervisor reaches Phase 10 **when** spawning reviewers **then** spawns 3 ephemeral reviewer subagents per round (same pattern as Phase 4) **should never** reuse reviewers across rounds or create persistent reviewer teams

**Given** all workers complete implementation **when** review round returns results **then** the wave can only exit if all 3 reviewers report zero BLOCKERs AND zero IMPORTANTs (CLEAN REVIEW) **should never** exit on a worker wave or with any IMPORTANT findings outstanding

**Given** a non-user-gated phase completes **when** transitioning to next phase **then** proceeds autonomously without asking user permission **should never** ask "Should I proceed?" for phases 1 (except research depth), 3, 4, 7, 8, 9, 10, 12

**Given** user provides input during URE, UAT, or ratification **when** recording responses **then** updates URD verbatim via bd comments add **should never** summarize or paraphrase user responses

## MVP Scope
All 9 slices above. This is a documentation/protocol refactoring — no production code changes. All changes are to .md, .xml, and .yaml files in skills/ and agents/.

description:
## Finding

In constraints.py, check_handoff_required() defines _SAME_ACTOR as a local variable inside the method:

    def check_handoff_required(self, from_phase: PhaseId, to_phase: PhaseId) -> ...:
        _SAME_ACTOR: frozenset[tuple[PhaseId, PhaseId]] = frozenset(
            {
                (PhaseId.P5_UAT, PhaseId.P6_RATIFY),
                (PhaseId.P6_RATIFY, PhaseId.P7_HANDOFF),
            }
        )

This frozenset is recreated on every call to check_handoff_required(). Compare with the module-level _HANDOFF_REQUIRED_TRANSITIONS and _REVIEW_PHASES constants which are correctly defined at module scope.

## Contrast
All other similar constants in constraints.py are module-level (lines 57-79):
- _REVIEW_AXES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({'A', 'B', 'C'})
- _REVIEW_PHASES: frozenset[PhaseId] = frozenset(...)
- _HANDOFF_REQUIRED_TRANSITIONS: frozenset[...] = frozenset(...)

_SAME_ACTOR should follow the same pattern.

## Fix
Move _SAME_ACTOR to module scope:
    _SAME_ACTOR_TRANSITIONS: frozenset[tuple[PhaseId, PhaseId]] = frozenset(
        {(PhaseId.P5_UAT, PhaseId.P6_RATIFY), (PhaseId.P6_RATIFY, PhaseId.P7_HANDOFF)}
    )
Consistent naming with _HANDOFF_REQUIRED_TRANSITIONS.
title: SLICE-3-REVIEW-C-1-F1: _SAME_ACTOR defined as local frozenset inside check_handoff_required method body

## Specification
Temporal workflow wrapping EpochStateMachine with durable execution. Signals for mutations, queries for reads, search attributes for forensics.

### Key Types
- EpochInput, EpochResult (frozen dataclasses)
- PhaseAdvanceSignal, ReviewVoteSignal (frozen signal types)
- Search attribute keys: SA_EPOCH_ID, SA_PHASE, SA_ROLE, SA_STATUS, SA_DOMAIN

### Workflow
- EpochWorkflow (@workflow.defn)
  - run(input) — wait for signals, advance through phases
  - advance_phase(signal) — signal handler
  - submit_vote(signal) — signal handler
  - current_state() — query
  - available_transitions() — query

### Activities
- check_constraints(state, to_phase) — activity
- record_transition(record) — activity

### pyproject.toml Changes
- Add temporalio>=1.9.0 to [project].dependencies
- Add pytest>=8.0, pytest-asyncio>=0.24 to [project.optional-dependencies].dev

## Files Owned
- scripts/aura_protocol/workflow.py (NEW)
- tests/test_workflow.py (NEW)
- pyproject.toml (MODIFY — add dependencies)

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC6: Given running EpochWorkflow when advance_phase signal then state + search attrs updated atomically should not have non-deterministic ops
- AC7: Given workflow at P9 when querying AuraPhase="p9" then workflow returned should not have stale attrs

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] EpochWorkflow with signals, queries, search attrs
- [ ] Activities for constraint checking and transition recording
- [ ] Temporal sandbox tests pass
- [ ] pyproject.toml deps added
- [ ] Search attributes updated on every transition
